- **x86_64** — x86 devices / some emulators

BannerHub can coexist with the original GameHub app (different package name).

releases.name: BannerHub v3.0.0
releases.body:
> ⚠️ **Install stable releases only.** Do not install pre-release builds or CI artifacts — they are untested snapshots used for development. Only stable releases (like this one) are debugged and verified across devices.

## New since v2.9.3

- **Config metadata** — exported configs now tagged with `app_source="bannerhub"` for community config site filtering and source-based purge

## Config Cross-Compatibility with BannerHub Lite

Configs exported from BannerHub are fully compatible with BannerHub Lite, and vice versa.

**Why it works:** Both apps are built on the same GameHub engine. Per-game Wine settings are stored in SharedPreferences under the same keys (`pc_g_setting<gameId>`). The Export feature reads those keys and writes them to a JSON file. The Import feature reads that same JSON and writes the keys back — the app that originally exported it doesn't matter.

**How to transfer:** Export a config in one app (saved to `/sdcard/BannerHub/configs/`), copy the JSON to the same folder on the device running the other app, then Import it from the game's settings menu. The same folder path is used by both apps intentionally so configs are found automatically without moving files.

The `app_source` field in the JSON is only used by the community config site to filter by origin — it has no effect on import behavior.

> 💬 **Community game configs:** Share and download per-game settings at [the412banner.github.io/bannerhub-game-configs](https://the412banner.github.io/bannerhub-game-configs). The library grows with every contribution.

## Installation

9 APK variants are built — pick the one matching your existing GameHub installation:

- **Normal / Normal(GHL)** — `banner.hub` / `gamehub.lite`
- **PuBG** — `com.tencent.ig`
- **AnTuTu / alt-AnTuTu** — `com.antutu.ABenchMark` / `com.antutu.benchmark.full`
- **PuBG-CrossFire** — `com.tencent.tmgp.cf`
- **Ludashi** — `com.ludashi.aibench`
- **Genshin** — `com.miHoYo.GenshinImpact`
- **Original** — `com.xiaoji.egggame`

Uninstall your existing GameHub before installing — BannerHub is signed with a different certificate and cannot update over the original.

releases.name: Bannerhub v2.9.3
releases.body:
> [!WARNING]
> **Only install official stable releases** (like this one). Do NOT install pre-releases or workflow artifacts — they are untested builds that may be unstable, crash, or corrupt your game configurations.

## New since v2.9.2

- **Fix: Export/Import Config showed 0 settings, 0 components for all games**
  Previously, the Export and Import Config buttons always reported `settings_count: 0` and `components_count: 0` in the JSON file. Root cause: the gameId was always passed as integer `0` instead of resolving to the correct SharedPreferences key. Now uses GameHub's own resolution logic — catalog games use their integer ID string (e.g. `"271590"`), locally-added games use their UUID string (e.g. `"local_5f129d63-..."`).

- **Fix: GOG Akamai CDN chunk URL construction**
  Chunk URLs with Akamai CDN endpoints were being built incorrectly, causing download failures for affected GOG titles.

- **Fix: Genshin Impact package name case**
  Corrected package name to `com.miHoYo.GenshinImpact` (mixed case) — the previous lowercase variant failed to detect the installed game.

---

> **Community Game Configs — [the412banner.github.io/bannerhub-game-configs](https://the412banner.github.io/bannerhub-game-configs)**
> **525 games · 1,071 configs** — Share and import community-tested configurations for your games. The library grows with every contribution — if a config works well for you, upload it and help others.

## Installation

Download the APK that matches your existing GameHub package name from the [latest release](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub/releases/latest):

| APK | Package | App Label |
|-----|---------|-----------|
| `BannerHub-vX.Y.Z-Normal.apk` | `banner.hub` | BannerHub |
| `BannerHub-vX.Y.Z-Normal.GHL.apk` | `gamehub.lite` | BannerHub |
| `BannerHub-vX.Y.Z-PuBG.apk` | `com.tencent.ig` | BannerHub PuBG |
| `BannerHub-vX.Y.Z-AnTuTu.apk` | `com.antutu.ABenchMark` | BannerHub AnTuTu |
| `BannerHub-vX.Y.Z-alt-AnTuTu.apk` | `com.antutu.benchmark.full` | BannerHub AnTuTu |
| `BannerHub-vX.Y.Z-PuBG-CrossFire.apk` | `com.tencent.tmgp.cf` | BannerHub PuBG CrossFire |
| `BannerHub-vX.Y.Z-Ludashi.apk` | `com.ludashi.aibench` | BannerHub Ludashi |
| `BannerHub-vX.Y.Z-Genshin.apk` | `com.mihoyo.genshinimpact` | BannerHub Genshin |
| `BannerHub-vX.Y.Z-Original.apk` | `com.xiaoji.egggame` | BannerHub Original |

**Which APK do I need?**

If you do not already have any GameHub variant installed, use the **Normal APK** (`banner.hub`). It installs as a completely separate app alongside the official GameHub Lite — both can coexist. If you want BannerHub to take over the `gamehub.lite` package slot (e.g. so apps that launch GameHub Lite by package name launch BannerHub instead), use **Normal.GHL** — but you must **uninstall the official GameHub Lite first** since they share the same package name and have different signing certificates. If you want to use a specific GameHub variant for performance spoofing (e.g. PuBG or Genshin), pick the matching APK. **Be aware: performance spoofing variants push your device harder and generate significantly more heat. Do not use these variants without proper cooling and a clear understanding of what you are doing. Use at your own risk.**

All 9 variants can be installed simultaneously. All APKs are signed with AOSP testkey (v1/v2/v3).

> **Note:** You must uninstall your existing BannerHub build before installing a new release if the signing certificate changed. Data is not preserved across uninstall.

releases.name: Bannerhub v2.9.2
releases.body:
> ⚠️ **Only install stable releases** (like this one). Do NOT install pre-releases or CI artifacts — they are untested intermediate builds and may be broken or incomplete.

## New since v2.9.1

- **Fix: imported GPU driver now applies correctly at launch** — configs exported from other devices stored the GPU driver display label as the component name. On import this caused the EmuComponents lookup to fail silently, so the game launched without the GPU driver even though all other settings applied correctly. After downloading the GPU driver component, BannerHub now detects the actual registered component name from the filesystem and corrects the game settings entry automatically. Manual re-selection is no longer needed.
- **Fix: exported configs only include components selected for that game** — previously, all BannerHub-injected components a user had installed could appear in an exported config, prompting unnecessary downloads on import. Exports now only include components actually selected for the specific game being exported.

> 📋 **Community game configs — 364 configs across 227 games**
> Browse, download, and share per-game Wine/DXVK/component configurations at **[the412banner.github.io/bannerhub-game-configs](https://the412banner.github.io/bannerhub-game-configs/)** — the community grows with every contribution.

## Installation

Download the APK matching your GameHub package name:

| APK | Package |
|-----|---------|
| `BannerHub-v2.9.2-Normal.apk` | `banner.hub` (fresh install / standalone) |
| `BannerHub-v2.9.2-Normal.GHL.apk` | `gamehub.lite` |
| Other variants | PuBG / AnTuTu / CrossFire / Ludashi / Genshin / Original |

All 9 variants can coexist simultaneously. The **Normal APK** installs alongside official GameHub Lite without conflict.

---

*To those celebrating Easter this weekend — Happy Easter! To everyone else, hope you're having a great Sunday regardless.*

releases.name: Base APK
releases.body: Base APK for CI builds (Gamehub 5.3.5 ReVanced, 136MB)

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.6.2
releases.body:
# Bannerlator 2.6.2 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. **2.6.2 is a fixes-and-hardening update.** No new headline system — this one sharpens what's already here. The **in-game performance HUD** got a ground-up hardening pass (one shared FPS source, device-complete metric readers, and a new overlay style), **component downloads** now keep going when you leave the app, and a batch of reported bugs are fixed. Like the last several releases it's **entirely app-side** — **no ImageFS reinstall** — your containers, themes, custom accent and per-game settings all carry over untouched. Just install over 2.6.
✨ Full feature list — click to expand everything Bannerlator offers Everything the app offers, at a glance. **🍷 Windows compatibility** - **Wine** — run native Win32/Win64 apps & games. - **Box64 / Box86** x86(-64)→ARM translation with performance presets. - **WOWBox64** for arm64ec containers. - **FEXCore** alt x86/x64 backend, with automatic unixlib (`.so`) matching per version. - **arm64ec** and **x64** container support. **🎨 Graphics & translation layers** - **DXVK** (DX8/9/10/11→Vulkan; GPLAsync & Sarek variants). - **VKD3D-Proton** (DX12→Vulkan). - **WineD3D / DirectDraw** OpenGL fallback for older titles. - **Proton bionic** layers (via GameNative) — **Proton 11.0-1** & **Proton 10.0-4** (arm64ec; FEX-unixlib + fast-yield), per-SDK (28 / 35). - **VEGAS** — Adreno-optimized DXVK (less stutter, real-time upscaling). - **Turnip / Mesa** Adreno drivers (+timeline-semaphore patches; `turnip-26.1.0` as a direct ICD for Android 10 / pre-11). - **BCn transcoding for Mali / Xclipse** — "Wrapper + bcn_layer" driver + BCn settings panel (plus experimental "Wrapper-gamenative"). **🖥️ Renderers** - **Vulkan / OpenGL / SurfaceFlinger / VirGL** host renderers. - **SurfaceFlinger colour fix** — R/B-swap + crash fix, per-container/game toggle. - **Native Rendering (Low-Latency Mode)** — direct-scanout, cuts input lag (Vulkan & OpenGL). - **Spatial upscalers** — SGSR, FSR / FSR-Fit, NIS, Sharpen (RCAS), Linear / Nearest; live in-game, saved per game. - **Fullscreen aspect modes** — Off / Fit / Stretch / Fill / Integer, live switch, per game. - **Supersampling** — 1.25× / 1.5× / 2× render scale, Lanczos-2 downsample. - **Screen effects** (Vulkan & OpenGL) — FXAA, Toon, CRT, NTSC, colour grading, CAS, fake-HDR. - **Debanding (Vulkan)** — adjustable dither pass for smooth gradients. - **ReShade** — real `.fx` effects on DXVK / VKD3D, on-device compile, ~100-effect catalog + your own, live in-game tab, per game, solo or stacked. - **VRR** — match refresh to FPS (Auto or 60 / 90 / 120 / 144 Hz), all three renderers. - Adjustable resolution & frame-rate limit. **🎞️ Frame generation & pacing** - **Two engines** — Off / **bionic-fg** (bundled) / **lsfg-vk** (bring your own `Lossless.dll`), shown as a badge in-game. - **Live controls** — Off / 2× / 3× / 4× + flow-scale slider, hot-reloaded. - **Standalone FPS limiter** — engine-independent present-pacing cap with quick 30 / 60 / 90 / 120 presets; steps aside when lsfg multiplies; both renderers. **📦 Containers** - Multiple isolated Wine containers with redesigned spec-chip cards. - **Run as administrator** (per-container toggle) for installers/games needing elevation. - **Auto-close on game exit** (per-container toggle). - **Import / export** containers. - Per-container Wine / driver / DXVK / VKD3D / Box64 / drives / Z-drive / env-vars, with env-var **presets**. - **Desktop wallpaper picker** (per-container or global). - **Compatibility Layers download menu** — browse / install / remove every component with progress bars, now continuing in the background. **🕹️ Games, shortcuts & input** - Game library (grid / list, sort, filters) with redesigned cards. - Add shortcuts from storage; **back up / restore saves** (GameHub-compatible zips). - **SteamGridDB** cover scraping; per-game language / locale. - **On-screen touch controls** (theme-follow or per-game colour) + virtual gamepads. - **Physical controllers** (SDL2) + touchpad / mouse emulation; rebuilt binding screen. **🌐 Community Configs** - **Catalog browser** (globe) with search, filters, sort, and "Matches my device". - **One-tap Apply** — surgically merges just that config's settings, preserving the rest. - **Smart install** of a config's missing DXVK / VKD3D / FEXCore / Turnip, then auto-applies. - **Config detail** — provenance, what-it-sets, before-apply diff, upvotes / downloads / comments. - **Share your own** — export the full recipe (never your files, logins or tuning), anonymous by default; **My uploads** to edit / delete. - **Optional account** (username + password, recovery-key, no email) so uploads follow you and carry your name — everything works anonymously without it. - **Read-only for your setup** — nothing changes unless you tap Apply. **🛒 Built-in stores & cross-store Download Manager** - **Steam** — user/pass or QR, owned library, built-in depot engine (JavaSteam), 4-tier speed, session hardening, DLC picker, true-size fix, optional **Goldberg** patch. - **Epic / Amazon** — sign in, browse, download / install / launch (incl. Epic free games). - **GOG** — DRM-free library with cloud-save sync. - **⬇ Cross-store Download Manager** — one view of all downloads + installed library, live progress, background + shade notifications, launch / verified uninstall. **🔒 Security & your store accounts** - Third-party sign-ins (not affiliated with the stores) — **use at your own risk**. - **Credentials redacted from logs** — signed URLs, OAuth codes, GOG secret / refresh-token, account IDs — in logcat and shareable files. **🧰 Bundled Start-menu utilities** - **WFM**, **AIO Graphics Test**, **Game Controller Test**; `.lnk` "Start in" working-directory support. **🎛️ Interface & in-game overlay** - **Jetpack Compose** UI with an icon-led drawer; your theme recolours the whole app *and* the in-game drawer. - **16 themes** + HSV custom-accent picker. - In-game overlay drawer + Task Manager (works on any renderer). - **File Manager with Favorites** — storage-badged, real thumbnails, doubles as the universal import picker. - **Performance HUD — three overlay styles** (Classic / GameHub / GameNative-style with live graphs), one shared FPS source + device-complete metrics (FPS / frame time / CPU-GPU load & temp / RAM / battery / power), accent outline, position saved per game. - **FEX runtime indicator** — live arm64ec vs x86-64, translator, and unixlib vs DLL. **📥 Builds & distribution** - Three flavors (standard / PuBG / Ludashi), optimized release builds, AOSP-testkey signed, GitHub Actions CI + tagged releases.
## ✨ What's New ### 🎛️ In-game HUD — hardened + a new overlay style The performance HUD got a ground-up pass: - **One shared FPS source** — every overlay style now reads the *same* number (they could drift before). - **Device-complete metric readers** — GPU load, CPU / GPU temperatures, RAM, battery / power were rewritten with proper vendor discovery, so values that used to read **0% on many non-Adreno GPUs** now populate correctly. - **A new third overlay** — a **GameNative-style HUD** with live graphs (compact pill or stacked list), alongside the Classic and GameHub styles, switchable **live in-game**. - **Polish** — the config UI moved to compact chips in an aligned grid; the outline is now a **slider-controlled accent border** matching your theme; the FPS limiter gained quick **30 / 60 / 90 / 120** presets; and the GPU-model row shows a clean chip name (e.g. `Adreno 750`) instead of the raw driver string. ### ⬇️ Component downloads keep going in the background Downloading a large compatibility layer (Proton, DXVK, box64, FEXCore, rootfs) no longer stops when you **minimize or lock** the phone — it continues via a foreground service with a **shade notification**, and **resumes** if interrupted. *(Thanks [@kylinzang](https://github.com/kylinzang) — [#122](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator/issues/122).)* ### 🔧 Fixes - **DXVK + VKD3D** — the container editor no longer lets you pair a **DXVK 1.x** build with VKD3D. They're binary-incompatible (DXVK 1.x's `dxgi` can't back VKD3D-Proton), which used to fail the DX12 path silently. *(Thanks [@GmoLargey](https://github.com/GmoLargey) — [#113](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator/issues/113).)* - **WOWBox64 label** — on **arm64ec** containers the per-game editor now correctly labels the x86 backend **"WOWBox64"** instead of "Box64". *(Thanks [@railexcatapangdiaz-ux](https://github.com/railexcatapangdiaz-ux) — [#111](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator/issues/111).)* - **HUD** — the Classic overlay no longer shows both orientations when you toggle a metric, and no longer resizes on toggle; **power draw** now reads correctly on devices (Xiaomi / Poco) whose battery-current sign is inverted. *(Thanks to Discord user **devaspe** for the reports.)* - Guarded a **failed component install** from reporting success. ### 🔐 Run games as administrator New per-container **"Run as administrator"** toggle (on by default) for installers and games that need elevation. ## 🌐 Community — thank you 🙏 Everything you've shared **directly to Bannerlator's own Community Config repo** now adds up to:

   

And because the in-app browser **also merges the BannerHub catalog**, the full set you can browse and one-tap-apply *inside the app* is bigger still:

 

**166 games** and **184 configs** shared straight to Bannerlator by **252** of you — and **2,257 games / 1,548 configs** usable in-app once the BannerHub catalog is merged in. All of it growing. Every config you share, every game you add, every account that joins makes this better for the next person. **This project grows *only* because of the community's support and participation — thank you.** - 📦 **Bannerlator config repo** — [github.com/The412Banner/bannerlator-game-configs](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerlator-game-configs) - 📦 **BannerHub catalog** (also browsable in-app) — [github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-game-configs](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-game-configs) - 🌐 **Browse online** — [the412banner.github.io/bannerlator-game-configs](https://the412banner.github.io/bannerlator-game-configs/) ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-2.6.2-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-2.6.2-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-2.6.2-ludashi.apk` | > ℹ️ **Updating in-app?** 2.6.2 is **entirely app-side** — the standard APK covers everything. There's **no ImageFS reinstall**: your containers, themes, custom accent, per-game settings and control colours all carry over. Just install over 2.6. ## 🙏 Credits - **[GameNative](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative)** — the new **GameNative-style HUD overlay** in this release is adapted from their `PerformanceHudView`, and the hardened, device-complete metric readers build directly on their GPU/thermal/battery coverage work. *(Bannerlator's Vulkan `AHardwareBuffer` present path, the standalone FPS-limiter mechanism, and the SurfaceFlinger colour fix also come from / were cross-examined against GameNative.)* Huge thanks to the GameNative team. - **Reports & requests this cycle** — [@kylinzang](https://github.com/kylinzang) ([#122](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator/issues/122)), [@GmoLargey](https://github.com/GmoLargey) ([#113](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator/issues/113)), [@railexcatapangdiaz-ux](https://github.com/railexcatapangdiaz-ux) ([#111](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator/issues/111)), and Discord user **devaspe** (HUD dual-orientation & power-reading reports). - **The wider stack** — built on the Winlator → cmod → Star Bionic lineage ([brunodev85](https://github.com/brunodev85/winlator), [coffincolors](https://github.com/coffincolors/winlator), [Pipetto-crypto](https://github.com/Pipetto-crypto/winlator), [star-emu](https://github.com/star-emu/star)) with DXVK, VKD3D-Proton, Box64, FEXCore, [JavaSteam](https://github.com/Longi94/JavaSteam) and [leegao](https://github.com/leegao)'s bcn_layer. Full attribution in the [repo README](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator#-credits). ## 🔗 Notes - Fixes-and-hardening release — no new subsystem, and nothing about your existing containers or settings changes on update.

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.6.1
releases.body:
# Bannerlator 2.6.1 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. **2.6.1 is a small hotfix-and-tweak release on top of 2.6.** The headline is an **Adreno graphics fix**: on 2.5 and later, some **Adreno (Qualcomm)** devices saw everything run slower and heavy titles like **Skyrim AE stop launching entirely** — a BC-texture (BCn) transcode meant only for **Mali / Xclipse** GPUs was being forced on Adreno too, where BCn is already handled in hardware, so it was pure wasted work. It's now switched **off on Adreno** (Mali / Xclipse behaviour is completely unchanged). Two small tweaks round it out. Like the last few releases this is an **entirely app-side** update — **no ImageFS reinstall** — your containers, themes, custom accent and per-game settings all carry over untouched. Just install over 2.6.
✨ Full feature list — click to expand everything Bannerlator offers Everything the app offers, at a glance. **🍷 Windows compatibility** - **Wine** — run native Win32/Win64 apps & games. - **Box64 / Box86** x86(-64)→ARM translation with performance presets. - **WOWBox64** for arm64ec containers. - **FEXCore** alt x86/x64 backend, with automatic unixlib (`.so`) matching per version. - **arm64ec** and **x64** container support. **🎨 Graphics & translation layers** - **DXVK** (DX8/9/10/11→Vulkan; steps aside when lsfg multiplies; both renderers. **📦 Containers** - Multiple isolated Wine containers with redesigned spec-chip cards. - **Auto-close on game exit** (per-container toggle). - **Import / export** containers. - Per-container Wine / driver / DXVK / VKD3D / Box64 / drives / Z-drive / env-vars, with env-var **presets**. - **Desktop wallpaper picker** (per-container or global). - **Compatibility Layers download menu** — browse / install / remove every component with progress bars. **🕹️ Games, shortcuts & input** - Game library (grid / list, sort, filters) with redesigned cards. - Add shortcuts from storage; **back up / restore saves** (GameHub-compatible zips). - **SteamGridDB** cover scraping; per-game language / locale. - **On-screen touch controls** (theme-follow or per-game colour) + virtual gamepads. - **Physical controllers** (SDL2) + touchpad / mouse emulation; rebuilt binding screen. **🌐 Community Configs** - **Catalog browser** (globe) with search, filters, sort, and "Matches my device". - **One-tap Apply** — surgically merges just that config's settings, preserving the rest. - **Smart install** of a config's missing DXVK / VKD3D / FEXCore / Turnip, then auto-applies. - **Config detail** — provenance, what-it-sets, before-apply diff, upvotes / downloads / comments. - **Share your own** — export the full recipe (never your files, logins or tuning), anonymous by default; **My uploads** to edit / delete. - **Optional account** (username + password, recovery-key, no email) so uploads follow you and carry your name — everything works anonymously without it. - **Read-only for your setup** — nothing changes unless you tap Apply. **🛒 Built-in stores & cross-store Download Manager** - **Steam** — user/pass or QR, owned library, built-in depot engine (JavaSteam), 4-tier speed, session hardening, DLC picker, true-size fix, optional **Goldberg** patch. - **Epic / Amazon** — sign in, browse, download / install / launch (incl. Epic free games). - **GOG** — DRM-free library with cloud-save sync. - **⬇ Cross-store Download Manager** — one view of all downloads + installed library, live progress, background + shade notifications, launch / verified uninstall. **🔒 Security & your store accounts** - Third-party sign-ins (not affiliated with the stores) — **use at your own risk**. - **Credentials redacted from logs** — signed URLs, OAuth codes, GOG secret / refresh-token, account IDs — in logcat and shareable files. **🧰 Bundled Start-menu utilities** - **WFM**, **AIO Graphics Test**, **Game Controller Test**; `.lnk` "Start in" working-directory support. **🎛️ Interface & in-game overlay** - **Jetpack Compose** UI with an icon-led drawer; your theme recolours the whole app *and* the in-game drawer. - **16 themes** + HSV custom-accent picker. - In-game overlay drawer + Task Manager (works on any renderer). - **File Manager with Favorites** — storage-badged, real thumbnails, doubles as the universal import picker. - **Performance HUD** — FPS / frame time / CPU-GPU temp / RAM, position saved per game. - **FEX runtime indicator** — live arm64ec vs x86-64, translator, and unixlib vs DLL. **📥 Builds & distribution** - Three flavors (standard / PuBG / Ludashi), optimized release builds, AOSP-testkey signed, GitHub Actions CI + tagged releases.
## ✨ What's New ### 🟣 Adreno: BCn transcode no longer forced — fix The integrated-wrapper BCn emulation (`WRAPPER_EMULATE_BCN`) was being emitted for **every** GPU vendor, but the BCn-aware wrapper builds shipped since 2.5 (**Wrapper-leegao** / **Wrapper-gamenative**) act on it — so on **Adreno 7xx** it forced a pointless per-texture transcode of formats the GPU already reads natively. The result on Adreno since 2.5: **global slowdown** and **BC-heavy DX11 games (e.g. Skyrim AE) failing to launch**. - It's now gated **off on Qualcomm (`0x5143`)** across the auto / full / default paths, mirroring the existing `bcn_layer` and compute-decode vendor guards. - **Mali / Xclipse / PowerVR behaviour is untouched** — the entire point of the 2.5 Mali hardening work still stands; only Adreno stops doing the redundant transcode. - Diagnosed from an **Adreno-750 / Snapdragon 8 Gen 3** report. If you're on Adreno and rolled back to 2.4 because 2.5+ was slow or wouldn't launch your games, **this is your fix.** ### 🔗 "Add to Shortcuts" for `.exe` files — new The built-in **File Manager** gains an **Add to Shortcuts** action on any `.exe`, so you can turn an executable straight into a game shortcut without going through the container editor. ### 🎮 Community-config XInput round-trips correctly — fix A shared config's **XInput** setting is a bitmask, not a plain on/off flag — it was being written back as `1/0`, so applying certain shared configs could land the wrong controller mode. It now round-trips faithfully. ### 🍷 New Proton 10.0-4 layer — available in-app A new **Proton 10.0-4** bionic compatibility layer is now downloadable from the in-app component list — **arm64ec**, with FEX-unixlib support and fast-yield, freshly rebuilt to be **~3× smaller and faster to install** (stripped + zstd, ~88 MB). Grab it under **Container → Wine/Proton**, pick **SDK 28** (Android 9) or **SDK 35** (Android 15), and run it on FEXCore. No app update or ImageFS reinstall needed. Prefer a manual install? Download the `.wcp` directly from the [**build-p10-20260713**](https://github.com/The412Banner/proton-wine/releases/tag/build-p10-20260713) release.
Previously in 2.6 — Community Config Sharing + optional accounts 2.6 completed **Community Configs**: 2.5.2 gave you the browse-and-apply half, and 2.6 added the other half — **share your own working setups**, locally or online, straight from the same Community Configs menu. Sharing captures your **entire per-game setup** (38 of 39 shortcut settings — the full DXVK/VKD3D config, graphics, emulator, wrapper incl. **VEGAS**, Turnip driver and input), and a brand-new **optional account system** (username + password, one-time recovery key, **no email**) lets your uploads follow you across devices and carry your name — everything still works anonymously without one. Also entirely app-side, **no ImageFS reinstall**. *(Includes a community VEGAS-dialog improvement from [isygold](https://github.com/isygold) — [#84](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator/pull/84).)*
## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-2.6.1-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-2.6.1-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-2.6.1-ludashi.apk` | > ℹ️ **Updating in-app?** 2.6.1 is **entirely app-side** — the standard APK covers everything. There's **no ImageFS reinstall**: your containers, themes, custom accent, per-game settings and control colours all carry over. Just install over 2.6. ## 🔗 Notes - Small hotfix-and-tweak release. The Adreno BCn change only affects **Qualcomm** GPUs; Mali / Xclipse / PowerVR paths are byte-for-byte unchanged. Nothing about your existing containers or settings changes on update.

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.6
releases.body:
# Bannerlator 2.6 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. **2.6 completes Community Configs.** 2.5.2 gave you the browse-and-apply half; 2.6 adds the other half — **share your own working setups**, both **locally and online**, right from the same Community Configs menu. Sharing now captures **your entire per-game setup** (not just the core versions), and there's a brand-new **optional account system** so your uploads can follow you across devices and carry your name. Like the last few releases this is an **entirely app-side** update — **no ImageFS reinstall** — your containers, themes, custom accent and per-game settings all carry over untouched. Just install over 2.5.2.

> 🌐 **Browse the community config library on the web:** [**the412banner.github.io/bannerlator-game-configs**](https://the412banner.github.io/bannerlator-game-configs/) — search everything shared from Bannerlator by game and device, compare side by side, and (signed in with your app account) manage your own uploads.
✨ Full feature list — click to expand everything Bannerlator offers Everything the app offers, at a glance. **🍷 Windows compatibility** - **Wine** — run native Win32/Win64 apps & games. - **Box64 / Box86** x86(-64)→ARM translation with performance presets. - **WOWBox64** for arm64ec containers. - **FEXCore** alt x86/x64 backend, with automatic unixlib (`.so`) matching per version. - **arm64ec** and **x64** container support. **🎨 Graphics & translation layers** - **DXVK** (DX8/9/10/11→Vulkan; GPLAsync & Sarek variants). - **VKD3D-Proton** (DX12→Vulkan). - **WineD3D / DirectDraw** OpenGL fallback for older titles. - **Proton bionic** layers (via GameNative) — incl. **Proton 11.0-1** arm64ec & x86-64, per-SDK (28 / 35). - **VEGAS** — Adreno-optimized DXVK (less stutter, real-time upscaling). - **Turnip / Mesa** Adreno drivers (+timeline-semaphore patches; `turnip-26.1.0` as a direct ICD for Android 10 / pre-11). - **BCn transcoding for Mali / Xclipse** — "Wrapper + bcn_layer" driver + BCn settings panel (plus experimental "Wrapper-gamenative"). **🖥️ Renderers** - **Vulkan / OpenGL / SurfaceFlinger / VirGL** host renderers. - **SurfaceFlinger colour fix** — R/B-swap + crash fix, per-container/game toggle. - **Native Rendering (Low-Latency Mode)** — direct-scanout, cuts input lag (Vulkan & OpenGL). - **Spatial upscalers** — SGSR, FSR / FSR-Fit, NIS, Sharpen (RCAS), Linear / Nearest; live in-game, saved per game. - **Fullscreen aspect modes** — Off / Fit / Stretch / Fill / Integer, live switch, per game. - **Supersampling** — 1.25× / 1.5× / 2× render scale, Lanczos-2 downsample. - **Screen effects** (Vulkan & OpenGL) — FXAA, Toon, CRT, NTSC, colour grading, CAS, fake-HDR. - **Debanding (Vulkan)** — adjustable dither pass for smooth gradients. - **ReShade** — real `.fx` effects on DXVK / VKD3D, on-device compile, ~100-effect catalog + your own, live in-game tab, per game, solo or stacked. - **VRR** — match refresh to FPS (Auto or 60 / 90 / 120 / 144 Hz), all three renderers. - Adjustable resolution & frame-rate limit. **🎞️ Frame generation & pacing** - **Two engines** — Off / **bionic-fg** (bundled) / **lsfg-vk** (bring your own `Lossless.dll`), shown as a badge in-game. - **Live controls** — Off / 2× / 3× / 4× + flow-scale slider, hot-reloaded. - **Standalone FPS limiter** — engine-independent present-pacing cap; steps aside when lsfg multiplies; both renderers. **📦 Containers** - Multiple isolated Wine containers with redesigned spec-chip cards. - **Auto-close on game exit** (per-container toggle). - **Import / export** containers. - Per-container Wine / driver / DXVK / VKD3D / Box64 / drives / Z-drive / env-vars, with env-var **presets**. - **Desktop wallpaper picker** (per-container or global). - **Compatibility Layers download menu** — browse / install / remove every component with progress bars. **🕹️ Games, shortcuts & input** - Game library (grid / list, sort, filters) with redesigned cards. - Add shortcuts from storage; **back up / restore saves** (GameHub-compatible zips). - **SteamGridDB** cover scraping; per-game language / locale. - **On-screen touch controls** (theme-follow or per-game colour) + virtual gamepads. - **Physical controllers** (SDL2) + touchpad / mouse emulation; rebuilt binding screen. **🌐 Community Configs** - **Catalog browser** (globe) with search, filters, sort, and "Matches my device". - **One-tap Apply** — surgically merges just that config's settings, preserving the rest. - **Smart install** of a config's missing DXVK / VKD3D / FEXCore / Turnip, then auto-applies. - **Config detail** — provenance, what-it-sets, before-apply diff, upvotes / downloads / comments. - **Share your own** — export the full recipe (never your files, logins or tuning), anonymous by default; **My uploads** to edit / delete. - **Optional account** (username + password, recovery-key, no email) so uploads follow you and carry your name — everything works anonymously without it. - **Read-only for your setup** — nothing changes unless you tap Apply. **🛒 Built-in stores & cross-store Download Manager** - **Steam** — user/pass or QR, owned library, built-in depot engine (JavaSteam), 4-tier speed, session hardening, DLC picker, true-size fix, optional **Goldberg** patch. - **Epic / Amazon** — sign in, browse, download / install / launch (incl. Epic free games). - **GOG** — DRM-free library with cloud-save sync. - **⬇ Cross-store Download Manager** — one view of all downloads + installed library, live progress, background + shade notifications, launch / verified uninstall. **🔒 Security & your store accounts** - Third-party sign-ins (not affiliated with the stores) — **use at your own risk**. - **Credentials redacted from logs** — signed URLs, OAuth codes, GOG secret / refresh-token, account IDs — in logcat and shareable files. **🧰 Bundled Start-menu utilities** - **WFM**, **AIO Graphics Test**, **Game Controller Test**; `.lnk` "Start in" working-directory support. **🎛️ Interface & in-game overlay** - **Jetpack Compose** UI with an icon-led drawer; your theme recolours the whole app *and* the in-game drawer. - **16 themes** + HSV custom-accent picker. - In-game overlay drawer + Task Manager (works on any renderer). - **File Manager with Favorites** — storage-badged, real thumbnails, doubles as the universal import picker. - **Performance HUD** — FPS / frame time / CPU-GPU temp / RAM, position saved per game. - **FEX runtime indicator** — live arm64ec vs x86-64, translator, and unixlib vs DLL. **📥 Builds & distribution** - Three flavors (standard / PuBG / Ludashi), optimized release builds, AOSP-testkey signed, GitHub Actions CI + tagged releases.
## ✨ What's New ### 🌐 Community Config Sharing — new You can now **share your own configs** straight from the Community Configs browser. Export a game's working settings and either keep them locally or **upload them for the community** in a couple of taps — anonymously by default. A **My uploads** area lets you edit a config's description inline or delete it any time, and uploads are ingested into the in-app catalog automatically. - **Captures your full game setup.** A shared config now saves **38 of 39 shortcut settings** (everything except one obsolete legacy fullscreen flag that's already folded into the fullscreen-mode setting) — not just the core wrapper/driver versions: - **DXVK / VKD3D:** the *full* config — version, async, asyncCache, framerate, vkd3dLevel, ddrawrapper, csmt, GPU spoof (gpuName / videoMemorySize), strict shader math, offscreen-rendering mode, renderer and custom DXVK conf. - **Graphics:** resolution, renderer, render scale, SurfaceFlinger compat, fullscreen mode, frame generation, FPS limiter, sharpness / FSR, ReShade (mode / effect / loadout / params). - **Emulator:** Box64 version + preset, FEXCore version + preset. - **Wrapper** selection (including **VEGAS**) + **GPU (Turnip) driver** version. - **Input:** xinput / dinput, exclusive / disable xinput, sim-touchscreen, controller count, control profile. - **Kept separate and private-by-default.** Bannerlator stores its configs in **its own space**, and a shared config never carries your files, your store logins, or device-specific driver tuning — just the game's settings and which device they came from. > 📖 **[Read the full plain-English guide →](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator/blob/main/docs/community-configs-guide.md)** for a friendly, non-technical walkthrough of browsing, applying, sharing, and the optional account / recovery-key system. ### 👤 Optional accounts — new A brand-new, **completely optional** account system. You never need one — browsing, applying, sharing, managing your uploads, upvoting and commenting all still work **anonymously**. But signing up adds three conveniences: - **Your uploads follow you** to a new device or after a reinstall. - **Your name and picture** appear on the configs you share (instead of "Anonymous user"). - **No email required** — just a username and password, recovered with a **one-time recovery key** instead of an email reset. Sign in from the globe browser or the navigation drawer; a friendly avatar replaces the person icon once you set one. ### 🎨 Config polish - **Every wrapper choice now round-trips.** A shared config carries the exact wrapper you picked — **VEGAS included** — instead of only inferring it from version numbers, so applying it reproduces your setup faithfully. - **Clearer "Your device" header.** The Community Configs header now shows your **device model *and* your graphics chip / SoC** as two separate values, and reads **"Unresolved"** for either one Android can't report — so you always know exactly what was detected. (Device matching is unchanged.) - **VEGAS config dialog, improved — community contribution.** Merged from **[isygold](https://github.com/isygold)** ([#84](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator/pull/84)): the VEGAS DXVK config dialog gains **delete**, **local install** and a **download progress bar**, and a duplicate VEGAS wrapper entry was cleaned up. > 🚀 **Support VEGAS Development** — low-level graphics dev & vibecoder: debugging, refactoring & improving original DXVK code for Adreno. **[❤️ Sponsor isygold →](https://github.com/sponsors/isygold)** ## 🙌 Powered by the community Community Configs is built on configs created by **real people** who have used and supported **[BannerHub](https://github.com/The412Banner/BannerHub)** and **BannerHub Lite** and shared their working per-game setups. With 2.6 the loop is complete — now **anyone can contribute back** and help the shared catalog grow. **Thank you to everyone who has shared a config.** 🙏 - 📦 **Source repo:** - 🌐 **Live catalog:** As of this release the community cache holds **10,038 configs** across **2,128 games**, of which Bannerlator matches **1,263 games** into its in-app catalog — **covering 8,942 configs (~89% of everything uploaded)** — and it keeps growing every time someone shares, now including configs uploaded straight from Bannerlator with 2.6's new sharing. ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-2.6-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-2.6-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-2.6-ludashi.apk` | > ℹ️ **Updating in-app?** 2.6 is **entirely app-side** — the standard APK covers everything. There's **no ImageFS reinstall**: your containers, themes, custom accent, per-game settings and control colours all carry over. Just install over 2.5.2. ## 🔗 Notes - **Community Config Sharing** and **accounts** are backed by a first-party service, kept separate from your device and from any third party. Accounts are optional and email-free; nothing about your existing containers or settings changes unless you apply a config.

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.5.2
releases.body: # Bannerlator 2.5.2 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. **2.5.2 is the Community Configs release.** It adds a way to browse **community-shared, per-game / per-device tuning configs** right inside the app and apply a known-good setup to your game in **one tap** — no more hunting through Discord screenshots or guessing which DXVK/driver/FEX combo someone used. It also ships **two new Proton 11 x86-64 compatibility layers** in the downloadable catalog. Like the last few releases this is an **entirely app-side** update — **no ImageFS reinstall** — your containers, themes, custom accent and per-game settings all carry over untouched. Just install over 2.5.1. ## ✨ What's New ### 🌐 Community Configs — new A new **globe** button in the **Games** header opens a browsable catalog of community game-configs. Bannerlator matches your installed games to the catalog, and a **"Matches my device"** filter narrows it to configs shared from hardware like yours. Each shared config is its own card showing its **★ upvotes** and **↓ downloads** (best-rated first), the device it came from, and its date. - **One-tap Apply.** Tap a config → **Apply to game**, and Bannerlator **surgically merges** just that config's settings — DXVK / VKD3D / Turnip driver / FEX preset / renderer / resolution / launch args / environment variables — into your shortcut, **preserving everything else you've set** (BCn options, HUD, colour toggles and the rest stay untouched). You can even apply a config to a *different* game — it warns you first. - **Smart install of what's missing.** If a config needs a **DXVK / VKD3D / FEXCore** build or a **Turnip GPU driver** you don't have, install it inline right there — an exact match installs with one confirm, or you pick from the closest versions (or browse all) — and the config **auto-applies** once it lands, with a ✓. FEX date-stamped builds are matched by their **YYMM** monthly tag so a dated config resolves to the right build, and a component you already have is recognised instead of erroring. - **Config detail page.** Tap **View details** for a config's provenance (source device / SoC / app / date), exactly **what it sets** in Bannerlator's own component terms, and a **preview of what would change** on your shortcut before you apply. The detail page also shows the config's **description, upvotes, downloads and comments** live — and you can **upvote** and **leave a comment** yourself. > ℹ️ Community Configs is **read-only for your setup** — it only ever changes a shortcut when *you* tap Apply, and it never touches your containers, ImageFS or existing settings. ### 🍷 Two new Proton 11 x86-64 compatibility layers — new Two **Proton 11.0-1 (x86_64)** compatibility layers are now downloadable in-app from the **Compatibility Layers** menu (Wine/Proton tab), packaged per Android version: - **`proton-11.0-1-x86_64-sdk28`** — for **Android 9-era** devices (SDK 28) - **`proton-11.0-1-x86_64-sdk35`** — for **Android 15** (SDK 35) Pick the one matching your Android version. ## 🙌 Powered by the community Every config in this catalog was created by **the community** — real people who have used and supported **[BannerHub](https://github.com/The412Banner/BannerHub)** and **BannerHub Lite** and exported their working per-game setups, building up this shared cache over time. Community Configs simply brings that library into Bannerlator. **A huge thank-you to everyone who contributed a config** — this whole feature is built entirely on your work. 🙏 - 📦 **Source repo:** - 🌐 **Live catalog:** As of this release the community cache holds **9,965 configs** across **2,121 games**, of which Bannerlator matches **1,258 games** into its in-app catalog — **92% of all uploaded configs covered** — and it keeps growing as more people share. ## 🔜 Coming next — share your own configs This release is the **browse-and-apply** half of Community Configs. The **final phase** is being worked on over the coming days: the ability to **share your own configs — both locally and online — right from the same Community Configs menu** added in this release. Once it lands you'll be able to contribute your working setups back to the community and help the catalog grow even faster. ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-2.5.2-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-2.5.2-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-2.5.2-ludashi.apk` | > ℹ️ **Updating in-app?** 2.5.2 is **entirely app-side** — the standard APK covers everything. There's **no ImageFS reinstall**: your containers, themes, custom accent, per-game settings and control colours all carry over. The two new Proton 11 x86-64 layers are optional downloads from the Compatibility Layers menu. ## 🔗 Notes - **Community Configs** is backed by a first-party config service; browsing, applying, upvoting and commenting all happen through it. It's additive and opt-in — nothing about your existing containers or settings changes unless you apply a config.

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.5.1
releases.body: # Bannerlator 2.5.1 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. **2.5.1 is a compatibility-and-tooling follow-up to 2.5.** It fixes crashes and wrong colours on the **SurfaceFlinger** renderer, adds a live **FEX runtime indicator** so you can see exactly what's translating your game, makes **FEXCore unixlib (`.so`)** handling correct when you swap FEXCore versions per game, and expands the **Environment Variable presets**. Like 2.5, this is an **entirely app-side** update — **no ImageFS reinstall** — your containers, themes, custom accent and per-game settings all carry over untouched. Just install over 2.5. ## ✨ What's New ### 🎨 SurfaceFlinger renderer: colour & crash fix — new The **SurfaceFlinger** host renderer (ASurfaceRenderer) got a crash-and-colour-accuracy fix: the red/blue channel swap is corrected, a GPU-side format converter and proper fencing were added, and the OpenGL / Vulkan / DRI3 paths are left untouched. Games that crashed or rendered with swapped colours on the SurfaceFlinger renderer now display correctly — **with no regression to the Vulkan or OpenGL renderers**. - New **"Correct SurfaceFlinger colours"** toggle — available **per-container and per-game**, shown inline under the **Renderer** picker when SurfaceFlinger is selected (on by default). - Device-verified on **Adreno 750**: *DiRT 3* on **DXVK + SurfaceFlinger** renders with correct colours, no R/B swap, and no Vulkan/OpenGL regression. > 🔁 Ported from **GameNative #1620 / #1644**. ### 🔎 FEX runtime indicator — new The **Graphics** tab now shows a small badge telling you exactly what's running under the hood, read **live from the running game** — no guessing: - **arm64ec** vs **x86-64** - the translator — **FEXCore** / **wowbox64** / **Box64** - for FEXCore, whether the native **unixlib (`.so`)** or the classic **DLL** path is active ### 🧩 FEXCore unixlib (`.so`) auto-match — new Users often run **different FEXCore versions per game or container** — some DLL-only, some with the native `.so` unixlib. 2.5.1 keeps the shared `.so` **exactly in sync with the version you selected, on every launch**: - Pick a unixlib FEXCore → its **matching** `.so` is placed automatically. - Pick a DLL-only FEXCore → the `.so` is **cleared**, so you get a true DLL run. - Flip back and forth freely — each launch re-stamps it, so there's **no stale `.so`** silently overriding a DLL-only choice and **no mismatched `.dll`/`.so` pair**. - Uninstalling a unixlib FEXCore now removes its `.so` too. ### 🧰 18 new Environment Variable presets The **Add Environment Variable** picker gained **18 presets** across **DXVK, VKD3D, Wine and Mesa** — so common tuning vars are one tap away instead of typed by hand. Highlights: `DXVK_DISABLE_TIMELINE_SEMAPHORES`, `VKD3D_SHADER_MODEL`, `DXVK_FRAME_RATE`, `VKD3D_CONFIG`, `WINEFSYNC`, `WINE_FULLSCREEN_FSR`, `MESA_VK_WSI_PRESENT_MODE` and more. > 💬 Requested by **Angel** (`DXVK_DISABLE_TIMELINE_SEMAPHORES` + `VKD3D_SHADER_MODEL`). ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-2.5.1-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-2.5.1-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-2.5.1-ludashi.apk` | > ℹ️ **Updating in-app?** 2.5.1 is **entirely app-side** — the standard APK covers everything. There's **no ImageFS reinstall**: your containers, themes, custom accent, per-game settings and control colours all carry over. The FEXCore `.so` sync happens automatically on your next game launch — including cleaning up any stale `.so` left from earlier installs. ## 🔗 Issues in this release - **GameNative #1620 / #1644** — SurfaceFlinger crash + colour fix 🚀 Ported & shipped --- ## 🙏 Credits - **Angel** — requested the environment-variable presets that seeded the new preset set (`DXVK_DISABLE_TIMELINE_SEMAPHORES`, `VKD3D_SHADER_MODEL`). - **[GameNative](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative)** (utkarshdalal) — upstream source of the SurfaceFlinger crash + colour fix (#1620 / #1644) the ASurfaceRenderer fix is ported from. The SurfaceFlinger colour toggle, the FEX runtime indicator, the FEXCore `.so` auto-match, and the preset expansion — the wiring into Bannerlator — is original engineering by **The412Banner**. Full lineage and credits are in the [README](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator#-credits). ----- If Mali users have serious issues after updating to 2.5.1 , use the 2.5 apk files on THIS RELEASE to roll back. Provide logs,otherwise I have no way to fix anything

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.5
releases.body: # Bannerlator 2.5 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. **2.5 is the Mali hardening release.** If you're on a **Mali** or **Xclipse** GPU, this is the big one: BC-texture games that used to crash or render as black/garbled textures now actually work. It's backed by a full sign-off on real Mali-G57 hardware, plus a round of in-game logging quality-of-life so reporting problems no longer means hunting through `Android/data`. This is an **entirely app-side** update — **no ImageFS reinstall** — your existing containers, themes, custom accent and per-game settings all carry over untouched. ## ✨ What's New ### 🟢 Mali & Xclipse: BC-texture games now work — new Mali and Xclipse GPUs don't decode BC (BCn) textures in hardware, so a huge number of games crashed, ran out of VRAM, or showed black/missing textures. **2.5 adds a new graphics driver — "Wrapper + bcn_layer"** — that transcodes those textures on the GPU at runtime so the games just run. - Pick it per container: **Container → Graphics driver → "Wrapper + bcn_layer"**. - Device-proven on **Mali-G57 (Helio G99)**: *MiSide* (Unity/BCn) went from crashing to rendering correctly at **~34 fps**, with **zero** buffer errors. - Backed by leegao's **bcn_layer shader-v3** — up to ~3.5× faster BCn/ASTC transcoding than the first version. > 💬 Driven end-to-end by **@kylinzang** on Mali-G57 — #70 (originally #54 / #53) ### 🧪 Wrapper-gamenative — new (experimental) A second BCn-capable driver with the transcoder baked into the wrapper itself. It's **Adreno-only** in practice (on Mali it initialises but the BCn path never fires), so it ships **labelled experimental** as an A/B option — `Wrapper + bcn_layer` remains the Mali path. ### 🧰 BCn Layer Settings A new section in the container's graphics-driver dialog to tune the transcoder: force-decode, **ETC2** / **ASTC** transcode toggles, image-view vs. staging mode, and debug logging. ### 🧾 In-game logging overhaul Reporting a bug from inside a game is finally painless: - **Copy button** in the in-game Debug Logs panel — copies the whole log (including the BCn transfer stats) to the clipboard, so you can paste it straight into a report instead of screenshotting. - **Selectable log location** (**Settings → Logs**) — App data, **Download/bannerlator**, **Documents/bannerlator**, or a custom folder. Grab every log straight from a file manager — **no shizuku or adb needed**. - **DXVK, DXGI and VKD3D logs are co-located** with the Wine log in one folder (they no longer hide inside the game directory), kept as separate files. - The **Wine Debug Channels** dialog now **scrolls** — you can reach `vulkan`, `heap` and every other channel past the first screenful. > 💬 All three logging ideas suggested by **@kylinzang** — #70 ## 🔧 Fixes & polish - **`wrapper_DestroyBuffer: null buffer` spam eliminated** — the bundled Vulkan wrapper was bumped to **ETC2-Milestone-2**, which cleans up buffer tracking (0 errors confirmed on Mali-G57). - **Copy-logs button now always reachable** — the in-game Debug Logs action row (Copy / Clear / Pause / Close) is pinned to the bottom of the dialog; in landscape it was previously pushed off-screen. - **Release-notes pipeline hardened** — backticks and shell metacharacters in release notes no longer break the update-manifest step. ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-2.5-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-2.5-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-2.5-ludashi.apk` | > ℹ️ **Updating in-app?** 2.5 is **entirely app-side** — the standard APK covers everything. There's **no ImageFS reinstall**: your containers, themes, custom accent, per-game settings and control colours all carry over. The new Mali driver assets are pulled into your existing containers automatically on next launch. ## 🔗 Issues in this release - #70 — Mali BCn layer (tracking hub for #54 / #53 / #63 / #64) 🚀 Shipped - #30 — Mali-G610 BCn candidate 🔎 In testing --- > 🍷 **Also fresh — Proton 11.0-1 (experimental, separate download):** a brand-new **bionic arm64ec** build of **Proton 11.0-1** (Wine 11.0 final, with esync / fsync / ntsync compiled in) is available as an installable Wine runtime — grab it here: **[build-p11-20260707](https://github.com/The412Banner/proton-wine/releases/tag/build-p11-20260707)**. Import it through the in-app compat-layer download / file picker as a Wine version. It's a separate, experimental runtime — **not required for 2.5**. --- ## 🙏 Credits 2.5 exists because the Mali community kept testing and reporting. Thank you: - **[@kylinzang](https://github.com/kylinzang)** — the driving force behind the entire Mali/BCn effort: the original request (#54 / #53), the full env-var spec, and iterative on-device testing on Mali-G57 through mali-v4 → mali-v6, ending in a full sign-off. The logging overhaul was his idea too (#70). - **[@rizky2-crypto](https://github.com/rizky2-crypto)** — Mali-G610 testing (#30). Built on the work of: - **[leegao](https://github.com/leegao)** — [`bcn_layer`](https://github.com/leegao/bcn_layer) (shader-v3) and [`bionic-vulkan-wrapper`](https://github.com/leegao/bionic-vulkan-wrapper) (ETC2-Milestone-2). This release would not exist without it. - **[GameNative](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative)** (utkarshdalal) — the base for the experimental `Wrapper-gamenative` driver. Everything wiring these into Bannerlator — the driver options, the BCn Layer Settings UI, the logging overhaul and the fixes above — is original engineering by **The412Banner**. Full lineage and credits are in the [README](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator#-credits). And thanks to everyone in the Discord and on GitHub who tested these builds — your reports drove this release.

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.4
releases.body: # Bannerlator 2.4 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. **2.4 is the fit & finish release.** Take control of how your game fills the screen, import files without fighting Android's system picker, and make your in-game choices stick — per game. This is an **entirely app-side** update — **no ImageFS reinstall** — your existing containers, themes, custom accent and per-game settings all carry over untouched; it's a straight in-place APK update. ## ✨ What's New ### 🖥️ Fullscreen aspect-ratio modes — new No more forced stretching on wide games. Choose exactly how the picture fits the screen, **per container *and* per game**: - **Off** — windowed, letterboxed - **Fit** — fullscreen, aspect preserved (letterbox bars) - **Stretch** — fills the screen, ignores aspect - **Fill** — fills the screen with aspect kept, cropping the overflow (no bars) - **Integer** — largest whole-number scale, pixel-perfect and centered Switch live from the in-game drawer with a new **five-button selector** that applies instantly and keeps the drawer open so you can compare modes. Works on the OpenGL, Vulkan and SurfaceFlinger renderers. > 💬 Requested by **@kylinzang** — #71 ### 📁 Import files with the built-in File Manager — new Android's system file picker (SAF) silently fails or crawls on a lot of OEM skins. Every local import now opens **Bannerlator's own File Manager** instead — the same screen you already browse containers with, in a pick mode — so importing works reliably on every device and is far faster for big files. - Covers **every import** — content packs (`.wcp`), control profiles (`.icp`), wallpapers, custom icons, saves, shortcuts, drivers, and the big **Proton / Wine / DXVK / box64 / FEXCore** assets. - Starts in **Download**, remembers your last folder, and filters to the file types you need. - **Image files show a real thumbnail** (jpg/png/webp…) instead of a generic icon — so picking a wallpaper or icon is no longer done blind. - Large imports now show a **progress bar with percentage and ETA** — no more wondering if a big Proton extract froze. - Prefer the old way? The system picker is still there as a **"Pick via system…"** option for cloud / document providers. > 💬 Requested by **@kylinzang** — #73 ### 🎚️ In-game choices that stick — per game — new Three settings you tune in-game are now remembered per game and restored on relaunch: - **Scaling mode** — SGSR / FSR / FSR-Fit / Sharpen / NIS used to reset to a plain filter every launch; now your pick is saved. - **Fullscreen mode** — your Off / Fit / Stretch / Fill / Integer choice is kept. - **FPS overlay position** — drag the classic or GameHub-style HUD where you want it and it stays put next time. ### 🧩 Choose your DLC + smarter Steam installs — new - **DLC picker** — a "Choose DLC" sheet lets you opt out of owned DLC before downloading, with the download size updating **live** as you check and uncheck. - **"Includes DLC" line** on a game's detail page, plus correct handling of DLC that ships inside a base game. - **True-size install fix** — Steam's catalog over-reported some games' sizes, so a fully-downloaded game could be wrongly flagged "incomplete" and refuse to launch. Bannerlator now fetches the real depot-manifest sizes and installs complete. - **Size breakdown** on the detail page — on-disk footprint, download size, catalog size and free space at a glance. - **Download ETA + speed** across the detail page, the Download Manager and the notification, smoothed so the numbers don't jump. > 💬 Steam install-blocker reported by **@Devaspe** ### 🖼️ Container wallpaper picker — new Pick an image as your container's desktop wallpaper — the picker now actually appears in the container editor — and choose whether a wallpaper applies to **just this container** or **globally** to all of them (and "global" now truly means global). > 💬 Reported by **@SombraShadow** — #66 ### 💾 Back up & restore game saves — new Export and import your saves as **GameHub-compatible zips**, with per-game save discovery, a confirm checklist before anything is overwritten, and a caution prompt so you verify before replacing originals. ## 🔧 Fixes & polish - **Magnifier follows the cursor on Vulkan** — parity with OpenGL, in both fullscreen and windowed. (#44) - **Magnifier no longer dims the whole screen** while zoomed (OpenGL + Vulkan). - **Settings crash fixed** — the "Enable File Provider" help button no longer force-closes. - **Scrape cover** is now available from the list-view overflow menu, not just the grid. - Assorted download-state and size-reporting corrections carried over from the storefronts work. > 💬 Magnifier reported by **@abdogm** — #44 ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-2.4-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-2.4-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-2.4-ludashi.apk` | > ℹ️ **Updating in-app?** 2.4 is **entirely app-side** — the standard APK covers everything. There's **no ImageFS reinstall** required: your containers, themes, custom accent, per-game settings and control colours all carry over untouched. ## 🔗 Issues in this release - #71 — Fullscreen aspect ratio ✅ Closed - #66 — Wallpaper picker ✅ Closed - #44 — Magnifier ✅ Closed - #73 — In-app file picker 🚀 Shipped --- ## 🙏 Credits 2.4 is a community-driven release — nearly every headline feature came from a report or request on GitHub. Thank you to the people who filed them, tested builds, and drove this release: - **[@kylinzang](https://github.com/kylinzang)** — Fullscreen aspect ratio (#71) **and** the in-app file picker (#73) - **[@SombraShadow](https://github.com/SombraShadow)** — Container wallpaper picker (#66) - **[@abdogm](https://github.com/abdogm)** — Magnifier fixes (#44) - **[@Devaspe](https://github.com/Devaspe)** — Steam install-blocker report that drove the true-size install fix The true-size Steam install fix builds on the depot engine from **[JavaSteam](https://github.com/Longi94/JavaSteam)** (Longi94). Everything built on top — the fullscreen aspect-ratio pipeline, the in-app file picker, the DLC picker, per-game persistence and the fixes above — is original engineering by **The412Banner**. Full lineage and credits are in the [README](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator#-credits). And thanks to everyone in the Discord and on GitHub who tested these builds and reported issues — your reports drove this release.

releases.name: Bannerlator mali-v6
releases.body: ## Changelog | Commit | Title | |--------|--------| | `547527a` | fix(debug-drawer): pin Copy/Clear/Pause/Close row on-screen in landscape | | `b9396f6` | ci(release): pass release_notes via env in update.json step so backticks/$/newlines don't trigger shell command-substitution (exit 127) | | `3ed151d` | mali-v5: label build (versionName 2.5-pre2-mali-v5, vc stays 39) — adds #70 logging batch on top of device-proven mali-v4 | | `ada05b5` | Logs UX: selectable log location, co-located DXVK/VKD3D logs, in-game Copy button (#70) | | `c178ccb` | mali-v4: hold versionCode at 39 (=2.4) for the tester pre-release so 2.4 installs over it cleanly (#70) | | `6fcf27e` | wip(steam-cloud): safe Steam Cloud Saves foundation (staging-folder; UFS→prefix mapping TODO) | | `7e5cae3` | fix(bcn): emit BCN_PROFILE_TRANSFERS with debug log + show ASTC toggle on gamenative (#70) | | `56c1233` | assets: bump wrapper-leegao.tzst to bionic-vulkan-wrapper ETC2-Milestone-2 | | `4e2b969` | Fix un-scrollable Wine Debug Channels dialog (#70) | | `eaa8168` | mali-v4: bump to 2.5-pre2 (vc41) for Mali BCn bug-fix pass (#70) | mali-v6 fixes the one open item from mali-v5: the in-game Copy-logs button. What changed - The in-game Debug Logs dialog action row (Copy / Clear / Pause / Close) is now pinned to the bottom of the panel and always visible. Previously, in landscape, the log list filled the whole height and pushed the Copy button off the bottom edge. The log list now scrolls within the space above the buttons. - Everything else is identical to mali-v5 (shader-v3 bcn_layer, ETC2-Milestone-2 wrapper, selectable log location, co-located DXVK/DXGI/VKD3D logs). Install - versionCode 39 (same as 2.4) and same signature, so it installs over 2.4 or mali-v5 with no uninstall and your containers are preserved. - This is a pre-release: it is not offered to stable users and does not touch the 2.4 in-app updater. - If you are on the earlier mali-v4 CI artifact (vc41), uninstall that first (vc41 to vc39 is a downgrade and Android will block it). Credits: leegao (bcn_layer shader-v3 + ETC2-Milestone-2).

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.5-pre2 — Mali BCn preview (mali-v5)
releases.body: ## Changelog | Commit | Title | |--------|--------| | `b9396f6` | ci(release): pass release_notes via env in update.json step so backticks/$/newlines don't trigger shell command-substitution (exit 127) | | `3ed151d` | mali-v5: label build (versionName 2.5-pre2-mali-v5, vc stays 39) — adds #70 logging batch on top of device-proven mali-v4 | | `ada05b5` | Logs UX: selectable log location, co-located DXVK/VKD3D logs, in-game Copy button (#70) | | `c178ccb` | mali-v4: hold versionCode at 39 (=2.4) for the tester pre-release so 2.4 installs over it cleanly (#70) | | `6fcf27e` | wip(steam-cloud): safe Steam Cloud Saves foundation (staging-folder; UFS→prefix mapping TODO) | | `7e5cae3` | fix(bcn): emit BCN_PROFILE_TRANSFERS with debug log + show ASTC toggle on gamenative (#70) | | `56c1233` | assets: bump wrapper-leegao.tzst to bionic-vulkan-wrapper ETC2-Milestone-2 | | `4e2b969` | Fix un-scrollable Wine Debug Channels dialog (#70) | | `eaa8168` | mali-v4: bump to 2.5-pre2 (vc41) for Mali BCn bug-fix pass (#70) | | `7038ff2` | feat(bcn): integrated-BCn Wrapper-gamenative + ASTC toggle (2.5-pre1, vc40) | **Mali BCn preview build (issue #70) — testers only.** This is a **pre-release**: it is *not* offered to stable users and does not change the 2.4 update channel. Version `2.5-pre2-mali-v5` · versionCode **39** (same as 2.4). Because every build shares one signature, **2.4 installs over this and this installs over 2.4 freely** — no uninstall needed. ⚠️ If you're on the earlier **mali-v4 CI artifact (vc41)**, uninstall it first (vc41 → vc39 is a downgrade and will be blocked). ### ✅ Confirmed on Mali (G57 / MiSide, device-tested by @kylinzang) - **`Wrapper + bcn_layer` (leegao shader-v3)** — runs correctly, ~34 fps (up from 21–31), 842 BCn-decode + 842 ASTC-encode calls, avg 311 MB/s. - **Wrapper bumped to leegao ETC2-Milestone-2** — **0 `wrapper_DestroyBuffer` errors** (was batches per scene load), + G57 r32p1 feature-faking. - **BCn debug log now writes** — the toggle now sets both `BCN_LAYER_LOG_LEVEL` and `BCN_PROFILE_TRANSFERS`. - **Wine Debug Channels dialog scrolls** — the full channel list (vulkan, heap, …) is reachable. - `Wrapper-gamenative` is confirmed **Adreno-only** on the BCn path and stays labelled experimental — use `Wrapper + bcn_layer` on Mali. ### 🆕 New in mali-v5 (logging UX, from @kylinzang's suggestions) - **Copy-logs button** on the in-game debug-logs drawer — copies the whole log to the clipboard. - **All logs saved in one folder, kept as separate files** — `wine_debug.log`, DXVK `*_d3d11.log` / `*_dxgi.log`, and `vkd3d-proton.log` now land in the same chosen folder (DXVK/VKD3D no longer hide in the game folder). - **Selectable log location** — Settings › Logs › **Log location**: App data (default) / Download/bannerlator / Documents/bannerlator / Choose folder… — so you can grab logs from a file manager without shizuku/adb. ### Credits leegao — bcn_layer **shader-v3** + **ETC2-Milestone-2** wrapper. Testing: @kylinzang (Mali-G57), @rizky2-crypto (#30, Mali-G610).

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.3
releases.body:
# Bannerlator 2.3 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. **2.3 is the storefronts release.** Bannerlator now has a full **built-in Steam store** — sign in with your account (username/password **or** QR code), browse your owned library, and **download and install** your games straight to a container via a native depot engine. Alongside Steam it adds **Epic Games** and **Amazon Games** stores and wires the existing **GOG** store into a new **cross-store Download Manager** — one place to see every active download and your whole installed library across all four stores, launch, and uninstall, with **background downloads** that keep going (and show up in your **notification shade**) after you leave the app. For offline / emulated play there's an optional **Goldberg auto-patch** for Steam games, and this release also **hardens store logs** so your account credentials and tokens are scrubbed from anything you might share. This is an **entirely app-side** update — **no ImageFS reinstall** — your existing containers, themes and settings carry over untouched; it's a straight in-place APK update. ## ✨ What's New ### 🎮 Built-in Steam store — new Sign in to **Steam** right inside Bannerlator and play from your own library. - **Sign in your way** — **username + password** *or* **QR code** (scan it with the Steam mobile app). Your session is remembered between launches. - **Browse your owned library** and open a game's detail page with cover art and store metadata. - **Download + install** games through the built-in **depot engine** (built on **[JavaSteam](https://github.com/Longi94/JavaSteam)**), straight into a Wine container ready to launch. - **GameNative-style download speed — 4 tiers.** Pick **Slow / Medium / Fast / Blazing**; the tier scales concurrency by CPU cores × a ratio, so you can trade heat/battery for throughput. - **Session hardening.** Long installs used to die around the ~1-hour mark when Steam's connection manager logged the session off. Bannerlator now **detects the logoff and recovers** — reconnect, re-login, hold a wakelock and keep the session alive — so big downloads survive to the end. - **Connection / login status pill** in the store header tells you at a glance whether you're connected and signed in. - **Depot-download OOM fix** — decompress and file-write concurrency are capped so large games no longer run the app out of memory mid-download. - Optional per-download **"Log debug session"** toggle for diagnostics (off by default). ### 🕹️ Goldberg auto-patch — new On a Steam game's detail page you can apply a **[Goldberg](https://mr_goldberg.gitlab.io/goldberg_emulator/) / gbe_fork** patch for **offline / emulated** play, in three tiers — **Regular**, **Experimental** and **ColdClient** — installed for you automatically (and cleanly reverted when you switch back to Off). > ⚠️ **Use at your own risk.** The Steam Emulator patch modifies a game's shipped files to run it offline/emulated. It's provided as-is for games you own; use it at your own discretion. ### ⬇️ Cross-store Download Manager — new One unified **⬇ Download Manager** across **all four stores**. - **Everything in one place** — active downloads *and* your installed library from Steam, Epic, GOG and Amazon, side by side. - **Live two-bar progress** — a distinct download bar and install bar with byte-accurate size and percentage. - **Background downloads + shade notifications** — a foreground service keeps downloads running when you leave the app, with a live notification you can tap to jump back into the manager. Leave the app, come back, it's still going. - **Launch or uninstall** any installed game from the manager, with a **verified uninstall** (progress spinner + themed result bar). - **One source of truth for install state** — a game's installed / update-available status, cover art and metadata now stay in sync across its detail page, its download card and the store list. - New **"Default screen on launch"** setting so the app opens where you want it. ### 🛍️ Epic, GOG & Amazon stores - **Amazon Games** and **Epic Games** stores added — sign in, browse your owned library, and **download / install / launch** your games (including Epic's **free games**). - The existing **GOG** store is now wired into the Download Manager and behaves like the rest. - **All store pages restyled to Material 3** — every non-Steam store now matches the app theme and the Steam store's layout, with live download **%** on detail pages, cover art, launch fixes, a **themed container picker**, and **themed toasts** (fixing an unreadable black-box toast some ROMs showed). ### 🔒 Security & your accounts — please read Bannerlator's Steam / Epic / GOG / Amazon sign-ins are a **third-party login system, exactly like any other emulator or launcher that logs into these stores.** Bannerlator is **not affiliated with, authorised by, or endorsed by** Valve/Steam, Epic Games, GOG, or Amazon. You are **logging your real store account into a community app — use it at your own risk.** This release **actively redacts** your credentials and account identifiers from logs. A new `StoreLog.redactUrl` helper strips the sensitive parts out of everything the stores write — to **logcat *and* to the shareable diagnostic files** — including: - **signed download / manifest URLs** (Amazon, Epic, GOG CDN links carry access tokens in the query), - **OAuth authorization codes** in login redirects, - **GOG `client_secret` + `refresh_token`**, - and **account identity IDs** (Epic account ID, GOG user ID). Steam credentials were already redacted. Even so, **be careful about sharing any log or debug file publicly** — logs can still contain other diagnostic detail, so only share one if you're comfortable doing so. None of this changes how login, downloads or cloud saves actually work; it only changes what gets written to a log. ### 🔓 Steam QR sign-in re-enabled QR sign-in was temporarily greyed out while the ~1-hour session-drop issue was investigated. With the new logoff-recovery path in place, **QR login is back on** — a QR session now stores the same credentials as a password login and recovers the same way. An on-screen note advises: if downloads or the session keep dropping after a QR sign-in, sign out and use **username + password** (the more durable path).
Previously in 2.2.2 — the in-game ReShade release (already in your build if you're on 2.2.2) - **🎬 In-game ReShade effects** — run real ReShade `.fx` post-processing (colour grading, sharpen, film grain, CRT, tonemap, LUTs…) on **DXVK / VKD3D** games, compiled **on-device** via the bundled **[vkBasalt](https://github.com/DadSchoorse/vkBasalt)** layer. Configured **per game**, tuned **live** in a dedicated in-game ReShade tab with auto-generated typed controls, and run **solo or stacked**. An on-demand catalog of ~100 curated MIT/CC0 effects, or drop your own into the `ReShade/` folder. - **♻️ Existing containers auto-updated** their ReShade (vkBasalt) layer on next launch — no reinstall needed. - **🛟 In-game drawer rail now scrolls** so Exit is always reachable on short screens. - **🎛️ FPS limiter now sticks** between sessions for shortcut-launched games. - **🎨 Fixes** — white on-screen controls stay white, very light / dark custom accents stay readable, and container creation can no longer get stuck on a stale shortcut.
## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-2.3-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-2.3-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-2.3-ludashi.apk` | > ℹ️ **Updating in-app?** This release is **entirely app-side** — the standard APK update covers everything in 2.3. There's **no ImageFS reinstall** required: your existing containers, themes, custom accent, per-game settings and control colours all carry over untouched. --- ## 🙏 2.3's storefronts are built on the work of others. The Steam store's connection client and its entire depot-download engine come from **[JavaSteam](https://github.com/Longi94/JavaSteam)** (maintainer **Longi94**, with the **javasteam-depotdownloader** fork by **joshuatam**). The optional Steam emulator patch is **[Goldberg Steam Emu](https://mr_goldberg.gitlab.io/goldberg_emulator/)** (Mr_Goldberg) and **gbe_fork** (Detanup01). The **4-tier download-speed model** and the **Steam session-hardening patterns** were ported/adapted from **[GameNative](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative)** (utkarshdalal), with Steam-client patterns also referenced from **Pluvia**. Built on that foundation, the original engineering in 2.3 — the **cross-store Download Manager**, the **four storefront integrations**, the multi-week **Steam session-hardening** work, the **depot OOM fix**, the **Goldberg auto-patch** integration, the store **Material-3 restyle**, and the store-log **credential redaction** — is by **The412Banner**. Full lineage and credits are in the [README](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator#-credits). And thanks to everyone in the Discord and on GitHub who tested the store downloads, Goldberg tiers and session-recovery work and reported issues — your reports drove this release.

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.2.2
releases.body:
# Bannerlator 2.2.2 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. **2.2.2's headline is in-game ReShade.** You can now run real ReShade `.fx` post-processing on your DirectX games, set up **per game**, tuned **live** in the in-game drawer, and **stacked** if you like. On top of that it lands a batch of fixes from GitHub and Discord: the **FPS limiter no longer resets** between sessions, on-screen controls finally honour a **white** colour, **container creation** can no longer get stuck, and very light / dark custom accents stay readable. This release is **entirely app-side** — **no ImageFS reinstall** — and your **existing containers refresh their ReShade layer automatically** on next launch, so AMOLED stays the default and updating won't change your look. ## ✨ What's New ### 🎬 In-game ReShade effects — new Run real ReShade `.fx` effects — colour grading, sharpen, film grain, CRT, tonemap, LUTs and more — on **DXVK / VKD3D** games, compiled **on-device** via the bundled **[vkBasalt](https://github.com/DadSchoorse/vkBasalt)** layer. - **Per-game setup.** Pick effects when editing a **container** or a **game shortcut**; your selection is saved with that game and restored every launch. - **On-demand catalog.** Browse and search a catalog of **~100 curated MIT / CC0 effects** and download only the ones you want — nothing is bundled into the APK. Or **drop your own** effect into the `ReShade/` folder (guide in the repo README). - **A dedicated in-game ReShade tab.** It reads each shader and auto-generates the *right* control for every parameter — **sliders, toggles, dropdowns and colour pickers** — with a **Reset-to-defaults** button. Toggle effects on/off and tune them **live**, no restart, and your in-game changes **persist per game** across quit → relaunch. - **Solo or stack.** Run a single effect, or layer several at once. > ⚠️ **Stacking multiple effects? Add them a few at a time.** Each ReShade effect compiles on-device and costs GPU, so **selecting too many at once can stop a game from starting** — you'll get a **flat / blank screen** instead of the game. If that happens, go back into the per-game **ReShade effect** settings and **uncheck the effects one at a time** (or the specific heavy one) until the game boots correctly, then add more gradually. > > *(Colour effects today; depth effects such as SSAO / depth-of-field aren't supported yet. ReShade only affects DirectX games running through DXVK / VKD3D.)* ### ♻️ Existing containers auto-update the ReShade layer Updating from an earlier version? Your **existing containers pick up the new ReShade (vkBasalt) layer automatically** on their next launch — you don't have to recreate a container or reinstall the ImageFS for ReShade to work. ### 🛟 In-game drawer rail now scrolls On shorter screens the left icon rail could push **Exit** off the bottom edge.
