The rail is now **scrollable**, so every control — including **Exit** — is always reachable. ### 🎛️ FPS limiter now sticks between sessions Toggling the in-game **FPS limiter** for a game launched from a shortcut is now **saved to that game** — it no longer reverts every time you close and reopen the game. *(Fixes the second half of #46.)* ### 🎨 Fixes - **White on-screen controls** — setting the virtual-control colour to **white** now stays white instead of falling back to blue. *(#46)* - **Readable light / dark accents** — a very **light** custom theme accent no longer paints the `+` / Play / badges white-on-white, and a very **dark** one no longer goes dark-on-dark; the glyph colour is derived from the accent automatically. **AMOLED and the built-in presets are byte-identical to before.** - **Container creation could get stuck** — after deleting a container that still had a leftover shortcut, creating a new container could silently fail and stay broken. The stale entry is now cleaned up and no longer blocks creation. *(#45)*
Previously in 2.2 — the themeable-interface overhaul (already in your build if you're on 2.2) - **🎨 A themeable interface, redesigned** — your selected theme recolours the **whole app *and* the in-game side drawer**, with a rebuilt drawer and accent-driven buttons. - **🌈 9 new colour presets (16 total)** — Midnight Cobalt, Phosphor, Carbon & Ember, Amethyst, Crimson, Synthwave, Royal Gold, Frost and Monochrome, plus a custom HSV accent picker. **AMOLED stays the default.** - **🕹️ Per-game on-screen control colours** — controls follow your theme, or take a per-game colour you set in the Controls editor. - **⭐ File Manager — Favorites / bookmarked folders** — pin the folders you open most and jump to them from a dedicated **★ Favorites** list, each entry tagged by storage source (Internal / SD / a container's Drive C: or Z:) and full path. - **🎮 Controller-binding screen rebuilt** in the modern UI — readable under any theme, cards, live button feedback. - **🧰 In-game Task Manager** — "New Task" works on the Vulkan / Native renderers, and processes show as cards.
## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-2.2.2-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-2.2.2-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-2.2.2-ludashi.apk` | > ℹ️ **Updating in-app?** This release is **entirely app-side** — the standard APK update covers everything in 2.2.2. There's **no ImageFS reinstall** required: your existing containers refresh their ReShade layer on next launch, and your selected theme, custom accent and per-game control colours carry over untouched. --- 🙏 Thanks to everyone in the Discord and on GitHub who tested the ReShade work and reported the control-colour, container-creation and FPS-limiter issues — your reports drove this release.

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.2.1 Pre-release 1
releases.body: ## Changelog | Commit | Title | |--------|--------| | `5bb33db` | docs(log): in-game rail scroll fix + 2.2.1 pre-release 1 | | `8c6298a` | chore(release): bump to 2.2.1 (versionCode 36) for pre-release 1 | | `332bd04` | fix(ingame-drawer): make the left icon rail scrollable on short screens | | `9f51ed7` | docs(log): release 2.2 description rewrite + UI-rewrite (Compose vs XML/Java) explainer | | `6355d0e` | docs(README): correct control-colour toggle label to "Follow app theme" | | `2ccfda8` | release: bump to 2.2 (versionCode 35) + README for the UI overhaul, themes, Favorites | | `b55ac24` | docs: progress-log — UI rebuild merged to main + 9 new theme presets | | `5d75439` | feat(theme): add 9 opt-in presets + preset-index migration | | `35c8a28` | Merge feat/ui-rebuild: UI rebuild (drawers, theme centralization, P2-P4b, controls, File Manager Favorites) | | `f6088c4` | docs: progress-log — File Manager Favorites (code done, CI green) | ### 🩹 Fix - **In-game side menu:** the left icon rail now **scrolls** when the screen is too short to fit every button, so the **Exit / Pause / Task Manager** buttons at the bottom are always reachable (previously they could overflow off-screen and you could not close the game). When the rail does fit, it looks exactly as before. _Pre-release for testers — only offered to users who enable **Settings → Include pre-releases**. Built on top of 2.2._

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.2
releases.body: # Bannerlator 2.2 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. **2.2 is a big visual overhaul.** The whole interface — and the **in-game side drawer** — now follow your chosen theme, with a redesigned drawer, **nine new colour presets**, and **per-game on-screen control colours**. It also adds **Favorites** to the File Manager and **rebuilds the controller-binding screen** in the modern UI, alongside a batch of readability and consistency fixes. This release is entirely app-side — **no ImageFS reinstall needed**, and **AMOLED stays the default**, so updating won't change your look unless you pick a new theme. ## ✨ What's New ### 🎨 A themeable interface — redesigned The app drawer and the **in-game side drawer** were rebuilt with new icons and accent-driven buttons, and — the big change — **your selected theme now recolours the entire app *and* the in-game drawer**. Previously large parts of the UI stayed blue regardless of theme; now your preset or custom accent reaches the screens, dialogs, drawer, chips, sliders and overlays. ### 🌈 9 new colour presets — total of 16 Nine new opt-in presets join the lineup, bringing the total to **16** (plus the custom HSV accent picker): - **Midnight Cobalt** · **Phosphor** · **Carbon & Ember** · **Amethyst** · **Crimson** · **Synthwave** · **Royal Gold** · **Frost** · **Monochrome** - alongside the existing **Classic Dark**, **AMOLED**, **Ocean**, **Forest**, **Sunset**, **Rose** and **Steel**. > **AMOLED stays the default and is unchanged.** Your previously selected theme and custom accent are preserved across the update — nothing about your look changes unless you choose a new one. ### 🕹️ Per-game on-screen control colours — new The on-screen touch controls follow your app theme by default, and you can now **override their colour per game**. Turn off **"Follow app theme"** in the Controls editor (in the in-game drawer or the out-of-game Input Controls page), pick a colour, and it's **saved with that game's profile** — so each game can keep its own control colour while the rest of the app stays on your theme. ### ⭐ File Manager — Favorites / bookmarked folders — new Pin the folders you open most and jump straight to them. A **★ button** opens a dedicated **Favorites** list, and each entry shows **exactly where it lives**: - a colour-coded badge for the storage source — **Internal**, **SD card**, **Drive C:**, or **Drive Z:**, - the **container name** for a container's Drive C:, so two games' `C:\Program Files` are never confused, - and the **full path**. Pin from a folder's **⋮ menu** or with **"Pin current folder"**; unpin with the filled ★. Favorites persist across launches, and entries for a deleted container quietly drop off. ### 🎮 Controller-binding screen rebuilt The external controller-binding screen was rebuilt in the modern UI: **labels are clearly readable under any theme**, each binding is a card matching the rest of the app, and **buttons you press while binding appear instantly**. (Builds on the 2.1.1 readability fix — bindings still **persist**, and **Ludashi-format profile import** still works.) ### 🧰 In-game Task Manager **"New Task" now works on the Vulkan / Native renderers** — the dialog used to be invisible over those surfaces — and running processes are now shown as **cards**, matching the File Manager rows. ### 🧹 Consistency & readability The **Games** and **Containers** lists now share one card style with consistent depth on every theme, and the legacy dropdowns, spinners, dialogs and section headers follow your accent too — with a **luminance floor** so text never goes dark-on-dark even on a very dark custom accent. ## 🧱 Under the hood — the UI rewrite, in plain terms A few people asked **what the "UI rewrite" actually is** and how much of the app it covers. Honest answer: **What kind of UI it is.** The interface is **Jetpack Compose** with **Material 3** — Kotlin, declarative, no XML layouts. The whole out-of-game app is a **single Activity** driving a **Navigation-Compose** graph (so the drawer destinations are Compose routes, not separate XML activities), wired up with **Hilt** for dependency injection. That's what makes a theme able to recolour everything at once: the colours come from one `MaterialTheme` the screens all read, instead of hundreds of hardcoded XML values. **What's actually converted.** The **entire out-of-game app is now Compose** — there are no XML screens left in it: - **Library & setup** — Containers, Container detail/editor, Games (shortcuts), Contents, Input Controls, Adrenotools GPU Drivers, Saves, **File Manager**, Settings, **Appearance**, and the Splash/setup flow. - **Storefronts** — GOG, Epic, Amazon and Steam (main, login, QR sign-in, library and game-detail screens) are all Compose. - **New in 2.2** — the **external controller-binding screen** was the last big out-of-game holdout on the old toolkit; it's now Compose too. That's roughly **240 Compose UI functions across ~90 Kotlin files**. **What's deliberately *not* Compose.** The **in-game experience** is still a **classic Java activity** (`XServerDisplayActivity`) — and it stays that way on purpose, because it hosts the **native render surface** (the `SurfaceView` / `GLSurfaceView` the X11 server, Vulkan/OpenGL renderer and Wine draw into). You can't render a game *inside* a Compose tree. What 2.2 did do is turn the in-game **side drawer** and its **dialogs** into **Compose "islands"** layered on top of that surface — which is why the in-game drawer now follows your theme and the redesigned controls/Task-Manager UI looks like the rest of the app. **So "how much is Compose vs old XML/Java?"** Measured by *screens*, the app you tap through is **essentially 100% Compose**. Measured by *lines of code* it looks like more Java than Kotlin — but that Java is overwhelmingly the **emulation engine**, not UI: the X11 server, the Vulkan/OpenGL renderer, box64 / FEXCore, the Wine handler, the input-controls engine and container management. None of that is "screens," and none of it was ever meant to be Compose. What genuinely *remains* as legacy XML/Java UI is now a short list — the **performance HUD overlay**, **Big Picture** mode, the **on-screen control-element editor**, a couple of file/folder pickers, and a few native over-the-game dialogs — and it keeps shrinking each release. ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-2.2-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-2.2-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-2.2-ludashi.apk` | > ℹ️ **Updating in-app?** This release is **entirely app-side** — the standard APK update covers everything in 2.2. There's **no ImageFS reinstall** and no container changes required, and your selected theme, custom accent and per-game control colours carry over untouched. --- 🙏 Thanks to everyone in the Discord and on GitHub who suggested the theme presets and the File Manager Favorites, and who reported the controller-binding and Task Manager rough edges.

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.1.1
releases.body: # Bannerlator 2.1.1 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. **2.1.1 adds a major new feature — ReShade post-processing — plus a low-latency mode for the OpenGL renderer, and fixes two community-reported bugs.** You can now run real ReShade `.fx` effects on top of your DXVK / VKD3D games, compiled on-device and tunable live in the in-game drawer; the direct-scanout **Native Rendering** low-latency path now works on the **OpenGL** renderer too; and the **controller-binding screen** and **container Duplicate / Export** issues are fixed. ## ✨ What's New ### 🎨 ReShade post-processing — new Real ReShade `.fx` effects now run on top of your games — colour grading, sharpening, film grain, CRT looks, tonemapping and more — compiled **on-device** through a bundled [vkBasalt](https://github.com/DadSchoorse/vkBasalt) layer, so there's no PC step. - **On-demand catalog** — pick from a built-in list of **~100 curated, license-safe effects** right in the container / shortcut editor. Each effect downloads on tap (the app itself stays small), then fills in and is ready to use; downloaded effects work offline. - **Drop-in folder** — drop your own self-contained effect folders into `Android/data//files/ReShade/` and they appear in the picker alongside the catalog. - **Dedicated in-game ReShade tab** — each effect's controls are **auto-generated from the shader's own parameters**, with the right control type for each: **sliders**, **on/off toggles**, **dropdowns** and **colour pickers**. Toggle an effect **on/off live** and **tune every parameter while the game runs**, with a **Reset to defaults** button. #### 🧩 Bringing your own effects (drop-in) — step by step Want an effect that isn't in the catalog? Add it yourself: 1. **Open this folder on your device** (create the `ReShade` folder if it isn't there): `Android/data/com.winlator.banner/files/ReShade/` *(Standard build. PuBG = `com.tencent.ig`, Ludashi = `com.ludashi.benchmark`.)* 2. **Make one folder per effect**, named whatever you want it called in the menu (e.g. `MySepia`). 3. **Put all of the effect's files inside that folder, next to the `.fx`:** the `.fx` itself, **any `.fxh` files it `#include`s** (e.g. `ReShade.fxh`, `ReShadeUI.fxh`), and any **textures** it uses. ``` ReShade/ MySepia/ MySepia.fx ReShade.fxh ← copy in any .fxh the .fx #includes ReShadeUI.fxh ``` 4. **In the app**, edit a **container** or **game shortcut** → **ReShade effect** picker → your folder now appears, select it. 5. Launch a **DirectX (DXVK / VKD3D) game** → in-game drawer → **ReShade tab** to toggle it and tune sliders live. > **Not showing up / no effect?** Make sure it's in **its own subfolder** with a `.fx` inside; copy in **every `#include`d `.fxh`** and texture next to the `.fx`; and remember it's **colour effects on DirectX games only** (no depth/SSAO/DOF, no OpenGL/WineD3D titles). Can't find `Android/data`? Many stock file managers hide it on Android 11+ — use one that can open it, or copy the folder over from a PC via USB. > 🎮 ReShade runs through the bundled **vkBasalt** Vulkan layer, which compiles `.fx` → SPIR-V on-device and feeds Turnip — so it affects **Vulkan-backed (DXVK / VKD3D)** games on any host renderer. **Colour effects work today; depth effects (SSAO / DOF) are not included yet.** A few catalog effects may not compile on every device. ### ⚡ Native Rendering (Low-Latency Mode) on OpenGL — new The direct-scanout **Native Rendering** path — which skips the compositor blit to cut input lag — now works on the **OpenGL** renderer, not just Vulkan. It's a per-container toggle; like the Vulkan path it's mutually exclusive with that renderer's post-processing effects / scaling (they grey out while it's on, since it bypasses the compositor). ### 🎮 Controller binding screen now readable — fixed (#37) On the external-controller binding screen the dropdown menus rendered **dark text on a dark background**, leaving the bindings effectively invisible. The text is now clearly readable. This completes the controller-binding fixes (bindings now **persist**, and **Ludashi-format profile import** works). ### 📦 Container Duplicate / Export — fixed (#39) Duplicating a container — especially a downloaded **Proton 11** one — could **silently produce nothing**, or a copy that lost its game drive-letters, lost its settings, or wouldn't boot. This turned out to be **five separate bugs**: - drive-letter **symlinks were dropped** instead of recreated (so the copy couldn't find your games), - a single unreadable file **aborted the whole copy**, - only a handful of settings were copied (**graphics driver, frame-gen, etc. were lost**), - a **crash** when a referenced graphics driver was missing, - and the wine socket directory was copied in a way that **stopped the copy from booting**. Duplicate and export now copy a container faithfully — it **appears in the list, keeps its drive mappings and all settings, and boots**. A failed duplicate now shows a clear message instead of silently doing nothing. ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-2.1.1-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-2.1.1-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-2.1.1-ludashi.apk` | > ℹ️ **Updating in-app?** The standard APK update covers everything in this release. ReShade effects download on demand from the in-app catalog the first time you pick them. ## 🙏 Credits — ReShade / post-processing | Feature | Source | Author | License | |---|---|---|---| | **ReShade `.fx` compiler + post-processing layer** | [vkBasalt](https://github.com/DadSchoorse/vkBasalt) | **DadSchoorse** | zlib | | **vkBasalt → Winlator integration** | [Winlator (vkBasalt to shortcuts)](https://github.com/Pipetto-crypto/winlator) | **Pipetto-crypto** | — | | **Vulkan rendering line carrying it forward** | [Winlator-Ludashi](https://github.com/StevenMXZ/Winlator-Ludashi) | **StevenMXZ** | — | | **Bundled / catalog `.fx` effects** | [ReShade shaders](https://github.com/crosire/reshade-shaders) · [prod80](https://github.com/prod80/prod80-reshade-repository) · [FXShaders](https://github.com/luluco250/FXShaders) · fubax | crosire · prod80 · luluco250 · fubax | MIT / CC0 | Bannerlator's ReShade feature is a **continuation of DadSchoorse's vkBasalt** — the bundled layer is built from its source, patched for live on-device effect toggle and slider control. Only MIT / CC0 effects are bundled or offered in the catalog. --- 🙏 Thanks to everyone who reported the controller-binding (#37) and container-duplication (#39) issues.

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.1
releases.body: # Bannerlator 2.1 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. **2.1 is a smoothness & polish release.** The headline is **VRR / refresh-rate matching** — your screen's refresh rate can now follow your in-game frame rate for smoother, less-torn, lower-power gameplay — joined by a new **NIS** upscaler and a **debanding** pass on the Vulkan compositor, plus two notable fixes: the in-game **Task Manager** now works on the Vulkan renderer, and the install progress bar no longer hangs at 98%. ## ✨ What's New ### 🏎️ Match refresh rate to FPS (VRR) — new Your display's refresh rate can now **track your frame rate** instead of staying locked at the panel's maximum: - **Auto (match FPS)** — one toggle that drives the panel refresh to follow whatever the game is running at (and your FPS cap). - **Manual refresh-rate slider** — pin a fixed rate that snaps to your panel's supported modes: **Off / 60 / 90 / 120 / 144 Hz**, with a live readout of the rate the display actually settled on. - Available in both the **in-game drawer** and the **container settings editor**; **greys out automatically** on displays without variable-refresh support. - Works across **all three host renderers** — Vulkan, OpenGL, and SurfaceFlinger. The payoff: smoother motion, less tearing, and lower power draw whenever a game runs below your panel's max refresh. > 🎮 **Handheld note (AYANEO / AYASpace and similar):** set the system **Refresh Rate** to **Auto** in the device's own settings so the app is allowed to drive the panel — a fixed system rate will override it. ### 🟩 NIS upscaler — new scaling mode **NVIDIA Image Scaling (NVScaler)** joins **SGSR** and **FSR** in the in-game **Scaling mode** picker on the Vulkan renderer — a single-pass, aspect-fit upscaler with its own **sharpness** slider, for another option when a game renders below display resolution. ### 🎨 Debanding — smoother gradients A new **debanding** pass on the Vulkan compositor dithers away the visible "stripes" in smooth gradients, skies, and dark scenes on 8-bit output. Switch it on with an adjustable **strength** slider; it runs as the very last step before the image reaches the screen, with no meaningful performance cost. ### 🩹 In-game Task Manager fixed on Vulkan / ASR The in-game drawer's **Task Manager** — **End Process** and **Bring to Front** — was dead on the **default Vulkan renderer** (and the SurfaceFlinger / ASR path) and only worked on OpenGL. Both actions now work on every renderer, so you can kill a hung game from the drawer again. ### 🩹 Install progress no longer hangs at 98% On a fresh first-launch setup — and on **Settings → Reinstall ImageFS** — the progress bar used to **freeze at 98%** while the **Proceed** button was already showing, leaving people unsure whether to keep waiting. It now correctly reaches **100% / Installation complete** at the true end of setup. ### 🧪 Updated AIO Graphics Test (v1.6.1) New containers ship with **AIO Graphics Test v1.6.1**, adding dedicated **banding** and **scaling** test scenes for checking the debander and the upscalers on your own device. ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-2.1-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-2.1-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-2.1-ludashi.apk` | > ℹ️ **Updating in-app?** The standard APK update covers everything in this release. **NIS** is NVIDIA's open-source Image Scaling (MIT); **debanding** uses a terminal dither pass before the 8-bit swapchain; **VRR** votes the display refresh rate through Android's `Surface.setFrameRate` / `SurfaceControl` APIs. ## 💬 New: Ask the AI about Bannerlator You can now ask questions about how Bannerlator works directly on GitHub — an AI reads the actual source code and answers with file references. Just open a new issue and you'll get a reply within a couple of minutes. *(This is a repo/community feature; it isn't part of the app.)* --- 🙏 Thanks to everyone who reported the Task Manager and install-progress issues.

releases.name: Bannerlator 2.0
releases.body: # Bannerlator 2.0 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. The graphics-enhancement program is now **complete across both renderers**, alongside a redesigned game/container UI, a few quality-of-life features, and broader device support. 1.9.2 brought real spatial upscaling and the full effect chain to the **Vulkan** renderer; **2.0 brings the same upscaler suite to the OpenGL renderer**, retunes every sharpness slider to a clean 0→max range, fixes the in-game magnifier, redesigns the game and container cards, auto-closes a session when the game quits, adds a FEXCore TSO preset, and — for older phones — **adds Turnip support on Android 10 / pre-11 devices**. ## ✨ What's New ### 🖼️ OpenGL renderer upscalers — full parity with Vulkan The same **Scaling mode** picker is now live in the in-game drawer on the **OpenGL** renderer too: - **SGSR** — Snapdragon Game Super Resolution 1.0 (edge-adaptive) - **FSR** / **FSR-Fit** — AMD FidelityFX Super Resolution 1.0 (EASU + RCAS), fill or aspect-preserving - **Sharpen** — RCAS-only sharpening, plus **Linear** / **Nearest** and a **Sharpness** slider The OpenGL path renders the scene at a reduced internal resolution and reconstructs it back up — so you get a sharper image *and* some performance headroom on OpenGL, not just Vulkan. ### 🎚️ Retuned sharpness sliders (both renderers) Every sharpness slider now spans a clean, useful range — **0 = off (no sharpening at all)** through **100 = maximum**: - **SGSR** sharpness range **doubled** for a much stronger effect at the top end. - **FSR** / **Sharpen** now pass through completely untouched at 0 (previously they always added a little sharpening). - **Sharpen** snaps to **5 clear steps** (0 / 25 / 50 / 75 / 100), matching its five discrete sharpening levels — no more dead slider travel. ### 🛠️ OpenGL renderer plumbing The **filter mode** (Nearest / Linear) is now a real, live setting on the OpenGL renderer — the in-game cursor stays sharp under Nearest — and trivial copy stages use a faster `glBlitFramebuffer` path. ### 🃏 Redesigned game & container cards The **Games** list and **Containers** list now share a cleaner, consistent card design: a primary row of chips (renderer · DXVK · frame-gen) over a muted secondary line (driver · VKD3D · backend), with the resolution in the subtitle. This also fixes long driver / component names crowding out or blanking the game title and pushing the ⋮ menu off-screen. ### 🚪 Auto-close on game exit When you launch a game from the library or a shortcut, the Wine session now **closes itself once the game quits** — no more black Wine desktop left behind. There's a per-container **"Close when game exits"** toggle (on by default) and a per-shortcut override. ### 🔍 Magnifier fixed The in-game **Magnifier** (zoom) overlay now works on the **default Vulkan renderer** (and the others). Previously its **+ / −** buttons did nothing unless you happened to be on the OpenGL renderer. ### ⚙️ FEXCore "Performance (TSO)" preset (new) A new built-in FEXCore preset: **Performance** settings with **Total Store Ordering** enabled — for the many games that need correct memory ordering without paying the full TSO cost. It appears alongside the existing presets in the FEXCore preset list. ### 📱 Turnip on Android 10 / older devices (new) A new **`turnip-26.1.0`** graphics-driver option loads Mesa Turnip as a **direct system Vulkan ICD** instead of through the adrenotools hook, so it works on **Android versions below 11** (e.g. Snapdragon 845 / Adreno 630) where the hook-based drivers silently fall back to the system driver. Pick it from the container's **Graphics Driver** list. The existing adrenotools drivers are unchanged — this is simply an additional option. ## ⚠️ Updating from an older version — please reinstall imageFS The new **`turnip-26.1.0`** driver is installed into **imageFS**, so a normal app update won't add it on its own. To get it: **Open the app's Settings → scroll to the bottom → Reinstall ImageFS.** A **fresh** install of 2.0 already includes it — no action needed. ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-2.0-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-2.0-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-2.0-ludashi.apk` | ## 🙏 Credits — graphics & drivers | Feature | Source | Author | License | |---|---|---|---| | **SGSR** upscaler | [snapdragon-gsr](https://github.com/SnapdragonStudios/snapdragon-gsr) | Qualcomm / Snapdragon Game Studios | BSD-3-Clause | | **FSR** EASU + RCAS, **Sharpen** | [FidelityFX-FSR](https://github.com/GPUOpen-Effects/FidelityFX-FSR) | AMD GPUOpen | MIT | | **Turnip 26.1.0** (Android-10 direct-ICD driver) | [Mesa / Freedreno](https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/mesa/mesa) — packaged in the direct-ICD form **[Winlator](https://github.com/brunodev85/winlator)** ships | Freedreno team @ Mesa · brunodev85 | MIT | The OpenGL upscalers reuse the same SGSR (BSD-3, Qualcomm) and FSR / CAS (MIT, AMD GPUOpen) shader code as the Vulkan path, ported to the GLES effect pipeline, with the upstream license headers retained.

releases.name: Bannerlator 1.9.2
releases.body:
# Bannerlator 1.9.2 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. This release brings a **full graphics-enhancement suite to the default Vulkan renderer**. Previously the Vulkan path had only basic sampler scaling and no post-processing — every screen effect was limited to the OpenGL renderer. Now the Vulkan compositor gets **real spatial upscaling (SGSR + FSR1, both new)** *and* the **full screen-effect chain** (CAS, fake-HDR, FXAA, Toon, Color, CRT, NTSC). Credit where due: FSR1-on-the-Vulkan-compositor was pioneered by [GameNative](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative) (the blueprint we built on), and SGSR appears on the Vulkan path of other Pluvia-based forks too. What Bannerlator adds is bringing **both** spatial upscalers — SGSR *and* FSR1 — together on the default path, alongside the effect chain. ## ✨ What's New ### 🖼️ Vulkan upscalers (new) — live in the in-game drawer Pick a **Scaling mode** live from the in-game drawer; it engages whenever a game renders below your screen resolution: - **SGSR** — Snapdragon Game Super Resolution 1.0 (single-pass, edge-adaptive) - **FSR** / **FSR-Fit** — AMD FidelityFX Super Resolution 1.0 (EASU + RCAS), fill or aspect-preserving - **Sharpen** — RCAS-only sharpening at native resolution - plus **Linear** / **Nearest** and an adjustable **Sharpness** slider ### 🔍 Supersampling (new) — set before launch - **Render scale** (Off / 1.25× / 1.5× / 2×) — a **container / per-game shortcut** setting (chosen *before* launch, since it changes the render resolution). The game renders *above* your screen resolution and the Vulkan compositor downsamples it with a Lanczos-2 filter — DSR / OGSSAA-style supersampling anti-aliasing. This is the opposite direction to the upscalers above. ### 🎨 Vulkan post-processing effects (new) — live in the in-game drawer Composable toggles and sliders in the in-game drawer, layered on top of any scaling mode: - **CAS** — AMD Contrast-Adaptive Sharpening, with a sharpness slider - **Fake HDR** — bloom + tone boost - **FXAA**, **Toon**, **CRT**, **NTSC** - **Color** grading — brightness / contrast / gamma ### ⚙️ Behaviour - **Native Rendering** and the post-processing presets are now **mutually exclusive** — enabling a preset turns Native off (Native does a direct scanout that bypasses the compositor), and enabling Native resets the presets, so the drawer never shows controls that silently do nothing. - **Linear** is now the default scaling mode. ### 🐛 Fixes - **On-screen controls opacity**: at low Overlay Opacity the filled "key" buttons (**MRB / BKSP / SPACE / ENTER**) kept a solid blue background while everything else faded out — the drop-shadow glow ignored the opacity setting. Fixed: the shadow now tracks opacity, so 0% truly hides every control. - Fixed an invisible black-on-black **"Native Rendering"** toast. ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-1.9.2-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-1.9.2-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-1.9.2-ludashi.apk` | ## 🙏 Credits — graphics enhancements The new Vulkan upscalers and effects port or reimplement the open-source work below. All sources are GPL-3.0-compatible, and the upstream license headers are kept inline in each shader at `app/src/main/cpp/winlator/*.frag`. | Feature | Source | Author | License | |---|---|---|---| | **SGSR** upscaler | [snapdragon-gsr](https://github.com/SnapdragonStudios/snapdragon-gsr) — `sgsr/v1/.../sgsr1_shader_mobile.frag` | Qualcomm / Snapdragon Game Studios | BSD-3-Clause | | **FSR** EASU + RCAS, **Sharpen** | [FidelityFX-FSR](https://github.com/GPUOpen-Effects/FidelityFX-FSR) — `ffx-fsr/ffx_fsr1.h` | AMD GPUOpen | MIT | | **CAS** sharpening | [FidelityFX-CAS](https://github.com/GPUOpen-Effects/FidelityFX-CAS) | AMD GPUOpen | MIT | | **FSR-Fit** + compositor present-slot blueprint | [GameNative](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative) | utkarshdalal & GameNative | GPL-3.0 — *approach reimplemented, not copied* | | **Fake HDR · FXAA · Toon · CRT · NTSC · Color** | Bannerlator's existing OpenGL GLES2 effect set (inherited from the upstream Winlator-Ludashi renderer), ported to the Vulkan post chain | — | — | - **SGSR** (BSD-3) and **FSR / CAS** (MIT) shader code is bundled with the full upstream license headers retained. - The **GameNative** integration *shape* — FSR running in the compositor's present slot, plus the FSR-Fit aspect mode — was reimplemented from its approach, **not** copied verbatim (GameNative is GPL-3.0). General project credits (Winlator / Box64 / Wine / DXVK / Turnip / etc.) are in the [README](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator#-credits). ---
Changelog (commits since 1.9.1) | Commit | Title | |--------|--------| | `84b6bc1` | release: bump to 1.9.2 (versionCode 31) | | `1d9439e` | fix(controls): fade GameHub button drop-shadow with overlay opacity | | `1c9c576` | fix(vulkan): black-box native toast + default scaling mode to Linear | | `3ed78bb` | feat(vulkan): mutually exclude Native Rendering and post-pass presets | | `77c6b76` | fix(vulkan): scaling modes dropped the screen-effect chain | | `5dfcdbf` | feat(vulkan): Phase 2 — port 5 GL screen effects to the Vulkan post chain | | `4fecbc6` | feat(vulkan): composable CAS + fake-HDR post effects + real upscaler sharpness | | `0655d61` | fix(vulkan): enable Sharpen mode 6 + drop duplicate container filter-mode dropdown | | `c3cbe49` | feat(vulkan): sharpen-only mode + supersampling (render scale) downscale | | `28ab22d` | feat(drawer): show only the active renderer's graphics controls | | `5f5a4a0` | feat(vulkan): SGSR + FSR1 spatial upscaler on the Vulkan compositor |

releases.name: Bannerlator 1.9.1
releases.body:
# Bannerlator 1.9.1 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. > 🩹 **This is a hotfix** — a small patch on top of [**1.9**](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator/releases/tag/1.9) addressing the multi-flavor install conflict below. No new features; everything in 1.9 (the SurfaceFlinger renderer and DXVK 3.0 / Vulkan 1.4 support) still applies. ## ✨ What's New ### 🐛 Fixes - **Multi-flavor install conflict**: the **PUBG** and **Ludashi** builds could fail to install alongside the **Standard** build with a *package conflict* error (`INSTALL_FAILED_CONFLICTING_PROVIDER`). The app's content-provider (FileProvider / shared-storage) authorities were hardcoded to one shared name across all three flavors; they are now keyed per-flavor, so each variant installs side-by-side without colliding. - **Save sharing**: sharing an exported save archive used a stale provider authority that didn't exist and would throw — it now resolves correctly on every flavor. - **Perf HUD** now renders correctly under the **SurfaceFlinger** and **Vulkan** host renderers. ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-1.9.1-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-1.9.1-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-1.9.1-ludashi.apk` | ## 🙏 Credits **Project & upstream** - **[brunodev85](https://github.com/brunodev85/winlator)** — original Winlator (Wine + Box64 + Turnip on Android), the foundation of this fork. - **[coffincolors](https://github.com/coffincolors/winlator)** — the `cmod` Winlator fork this codebase builds on. - **[Pipetto-crypto](https://github.com/Pipetto-crypto/winlator)** — Winlator Bionic + Box64 fix branch (the "Bionic" half of Star Bionic). - **[jacojayy](https://github.com/jacojayy/star)** & the **[star-emu](https://github.com/star-emu)** team — the Star Bionic / Winlator Frost lines this build continues, plus Turnip Timeline-Semaphore patches. - **[StevenMXZ](https://github.com/StevenMXZ/Winlator-Ludashi)** (Winlator-Ludashi) — the **Vulkan rendering** path and the bundled **Winlator File Manager**. - **[isygold](https://github.com/isygold/vegas-releases)** — Star Engine / VEGAS DXVK fork. 📖 **[VEGAS DXVK FAQ](https://htmlpreview.github.io/?https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator/blob/main/docs/vegas_faq.html)** · 🚀 **Support VEGAS Development** — low-level graphics dev & vibecoder: debugging, refactoring & improving original DXVK code for Adreno. **[❤️ Sponsor →](https://github.com/sponsors/isygold)** - Frame generation: **[xXJSONDeruloXx](https://github.com/xXJSONDeruloXx/bionic-fg)** (bionic-fg) · **[PancakeTAS](https://github.com/PancakeTAS/lsfg-vk)** (lsfg-vk) · **[FrankBarretta](https://github.com/FrankBarretta/lsfg-vk-android)** (Android port). `Lossless.dll` is user-supplied ([Lossless Scaling](https://store.steampowered.com/app/993090/Lossless_Scaling/) by THS). - Upstream stack: **Wine** ([WineHQ](https://www.winehq.org/)) · **Box64** ([ptitSeb](https://github.com/ptitSeb)) · **FEXCore** ([FEX-Emu](https://github.com/FEX-Emu)) · **DXVK** ([doitsujin](https://github.com/doitsujin)) · **VKD3D-Proton** ([HansKristian-Work](https://github.com/HansKristian-Work)) · **Turnip/Mesa** ([Freedreno](https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/mesa/mesa)). Full credits are in the [README](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator#-credits). ---

releases.name: Bannerlator 1.9
releases.body:
# Bannerlator 1.9 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. ## ✨ What's New ### 🖼️ SurfaceFlinger renderer (Experimental) A new **third host renderer** joins OpenGL and Vulkan. Instead of compositing through a GL/Vulkan pass, **SurfaceFlinger** hands game frames straight to Android's display hardware — one SurfaceControl layer per window — for lower latency and overhead. Selectable **per-container** and **per-game**. > ⚠️ **EXPERIMENTAL — USE AT YOUR OWN RISK.** On some devices/SoCs the direct display path can fault the GPU/display driver and **reboot your device**, possibly losing unsaved progress or corrupting a container. It is validated mainly on recent **Adreno** GPUs. Selecting it requires confirming an in-app warning, and the default renderer is unchanged — you only use it if you opt in. ### 🟣 DXVK 3.0 support (Vulkan 1.4) Added a **Vulkan 1.4** option to the **Turnip/Wrapper Driver Configuration**. [**DXVK 3.0**](https://github.com/doitsujin/dxvk/releases/tag/v3.0) requires Vulkan 1.4, so set **Vulkan Version → 1.4** there to run it. Needs a VK 1.4-capable driver (recent Mesa Turnip on **Adreno 7xx/8xx**); on Adreno 6xx, stay on DXVK 2.7.1. > 🟢 **Need a VK 1.4 Turnip driver?** Grab bleeding-edge Mesa Turnip builds (rebuilt on every upstream Mesa commit, Vulkan 1.4) from **[Banners-Turnip](https://github.com/The412Banner/Banners-Turnip/releases)** and install them via the in-app graphics-driver downloader. ### 🐛 Fixes - **Per-game download menu**: the DXVK/VKD3D download sheet no longer opens *behind* the shortcut settings screen — it now appears on top and is fully usable. - **Perf HUD** now correctly labels the **SurfaceFlinger** renderer (it previously read "OpenGL") and shows live FPS under it. ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-1.9-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-1.9-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-1.9-ludashi.apk` | ## 🙏 Credits **This release** - The **SurfaceFlinger renderer (ASurfaceRenderer / ASR)** is ported from **[GameNative](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative)** — the ASR implementation by **André Vito** (GameNative PR #1582) — which is itself built on the SurfaceControl direct-scanout work of **[StevenMXZ](https://github.com/StevenMXZ/Winlator-Ludashi)** (Winlator-Ludashi). Huge thanks to both for the foundation this renderer stands on. - The **Vulkan rendering** path and the bundled **Winlator File Manager** are from **[StevenMXZ](https://github.com/StevenMXZ/Winlator-Ludashi)** (Winlator-Ludashi). **Project & upstream** - **[brunodev85](https://github.com/brunodev85/winlator)** — original Winlator (Wine + Box64 + Turnip on Android), the foundation of this fork. - **[coffincolors](https://github.com/coffincolors/winlator)** — the `cmod` Winlator fork this codebase builds on. - **[Pipetto-crypto](https://github.com/Pipetto-crypto/winlator)** — Winlator Bionic + Box64 fix branch (the "Bionic" half of Star Bionic). - **[jacojayy](https://github.com/jacojayy/star)** & the **[star-emu](https://github.com/star-emu)** team — the Star Bionic / Winlator Frost lines this build continues, plus Turnip Timeline-Semaphore patches. - **[isygold](https://github.com/isygold/vegas-releases)** — Star Engine / VEGAS DXVK fork. 📖 **[VEGAS DXVK FAQ](https://htmlpreview.github.io/?https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator/blob/main/docs/vegas_faq.html)** · 🚀 **Support VEGAS Development** — low-level graphics dev & vibecoder: debugging, refactoring & improving original DXVK code for Adreno. **[❤️ Sponsor →](https://github.com/sponsors/isygold)** - Frame generation: **[xXJSONDeruloXx](https://github.com/xXJSONDeruloXx/bionic-fg)** (bionic-fg) · **[PancakeTAS](https://github.com/PancakeTAS/lsfg-vk)** (lsfg-vk) · **[FrankBarretta](https://github.com/FrankBarretta/lsfg-vk-android)** (Android port). `Lossless.dll` is user-supplied ([Lossless Scaling](https://store.steampowered.com/app/993090/Lossless_Scaling/) by THS). - Upstream stack: **Wine** ([WineHQ](https://www.winehq.org/)) · **Box64** ([ptitSeb](https://github.com/ptitSeb)) · **FEXCore** ([FEX-Emu](https://github.com/FEX-Emu)) · **DXVK** ([doitsujin](https://github.com/doitsujin)) · **VKD3D-Proton** ([HansKristian-Work](https://github.com/HansKristian-Work)) · **Turnip/Mesa** ([Freedreno](https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/mesa/mesa)). Full credits are in the [README](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator#-credits). ---

releases.name: Bannerlator 1.8
releases.body:
# Bannerlator 1.8 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. ## ✨ What's New ### 📲 In-app updater Bannerlator can now **update itself**. A new **Updates** section in **Settings** (plus an **Update now** action in the About dialog and an app-wide banner) checks for new releases, downloads the right APK for your build, and installs it — no more hunting for the latest APK by hand. - Picks the correct flavor automatically (**Standard / PUBG / Ludashi**). - Optional **"Include pre-releases"** toggle to ride the beta channel and get `-preN` builds early. - Offline-safe: the last check is cached, and you can **skip** a version or turn notifications off. > ℹ️ This is the **first stable to carry the updater baseline**. Installs from 1.8 onward will self-update; older builds won't see the in-app prompt because they predate the feature. ### 🎮 GameHub-style performance HUD A brand-new, fully-featured **performance HUD** joins the classic FPS overlay as a **second selectable overlay** — with **Classic / Neon / Mono** skins, an FPS graph, and detailed metrics (FPS, CPU/GPU usage, RAM, temperature, power draw, engine & GPU model). - **Per-container** configuration with full control over fields, colors, outline, scale and opacity. - **Live-swap** between the GameHub and classic overlays **mid-game** — no relaunch needed. - Every toggle is mirrored in the **in-game side drawer**, so you can tweak it on the fly. ### 🖥️ Setup-screen branding fix The first-launch setup screen no longer shows the old upstream **"Star Bionic"** name or a stale version — it now shows the correct app name and current version per flavor. ### 🔄 Updater correctness fix Fixed how the updater chooses the newest release: it now sorts by publish date rather than trusting GitHub's list order, so a freshly-cut stable can no longer shadow a newer pre-release on the beta channel. ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-1.8-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-1.8-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-1.8-ludashi.apk` | ## 🙏 Credits **This release** - The new **performance HUD** is a clean-room reimplementation inspired by the in-game overlay in **GameHub 6.0.9**. - The bundled **Winlator File Manager** and the **Vulkan rendering** path are from **[StevenMXZ](https://github.com/StevenMXZ/Winlator-Ludashi)** (Winlator-Ludashi). **Project & upstream** - **[brunodev85](https://github.com/brunodev85/winlator)** — original Winlator (Wine + Box64 + Turnip on Android), the foundation of this fork. - **[coffincolors](https://github.com/coffincolors/winlator)** — the `cmod` Winlator fork this codebase builds on. - **[Pipetto-crypto](https://github.com/Pipetto-crypto/winlator)** — Winlator Bionic + Box64 fix branch (the "Bionic" half of Star Bionic). - **[jacojayy](https://github.com/jacojayy/star)** & the **[star-emu](https://github.com/star-emu)** team — the Star Bionic / Winlator Frost lines this build continues, plus Turnip Timeline-Semaphore patches. - **[isygold](https://github.com/isygold/vegas-releases)** — Star Engine / VEGAS DXVK fork. 📖 **[VEGAS DXVK FAQ](https://htmlpreview.github.io/?https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator/blob/main/docs/vegas_faq.html)** · 🚀 **Support VEGAS Development** — low-level graphics dev & vibecoder: debugging, refactoring & improving original DXVK code for Adreno. **[❤️ Sponsor →](https://github.com/sponsors/isygold)** - Frame generation: **[xXJSONDeruloXx](https://github.com/xXJSONDeruloXx/bionic-fg)** (bionic-fg) · **[PancakeTAS](https://github.com/PancakeTAS/lsfg-vk)** (lsfg-vk) · **[FrankBarretta](https://github.com/FrankBarretta/lsfg-vk-android)** (Android port). `Lossless.dll` is user-supplied ([Lossless Scaling](https://store.steampowered.com/app/993090/Lossless_Scaling/) by THS). - Upstream stack: **Wine** ([WineHQ](https://www.winehq.org/)) · **Box64** ([ptitSeb](https://github.com/ptitSeb)) · **FEXCore** ([FEX-Emu](https://github.com/FEX-Emu)) · **DXVK** ([doitsujin](https://github.com/doitsujin)) · **VKD3D-Proton** ([HansKristian-Work](https://github.com/HansKristian-Work)) · **Turnip/Mesa** ([Freedreno](https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/mesa/mesa)). Full credits are in the [README](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator#-credits). ---

releases.name: Bannerlator 1.7
releases.body:
# Bannerlator 1.7 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. ## ✨ What's New ### 🎮 Steam store — downloads fixed Steam game downloads that failed with *"Download failed: Unknown error"* now work. Two separate bugs were fixed: - A **login race** during Steam connection cycling — a download could start before the session finished re-logging-on after a reconnect, so the depot-manifest request timed out. - The full **BouncyCastle** crypto provider was never registered, so saving a depot manifest crashed with a `SHA-1 for provider BC` error. The bundled provider is now installed at startup. ### 🧩 Components installer A new in-container **Components** menu installs Wine dependencies into a container's prefix from a browsable catalog — **mono, gecko, .NET, vcredist, d3dx** and more. It supports both file-drop installs and an execute engine for .NET/vcredist-style installers, a **Win7/WinXP** mode, and a persisted **"Installed"** status per container. ### 🎚️ On-screen overlay opacity The overlay-opacity slider moved into the **in-game side menu (Controls tab)**. It's now **live** and a true **0–100 %** — 0 % makes the overlay fully invisible. ### 📊 FPS overlay improvements **Tap the overlay** to toggle its **vertical / horizontal** orientation live, and it now shows a live **D3D API label** so you can see at a glance whether the game is running on **VKD3D** or **DXVK**. ### 🖥️ Scrollable Advanced Vulkan dialogs The Advanced Vulkan / Graphics Driver configuration dialogs no longer cut off on smaller screens — they scroll. ### 🎞️ Video playback Bundled the full **ffmpeg-8** libraries for winedmo, improving in-game video / FMV decode. > 📦 **The ffmpeg-8 libraries live inside imageFS**, so a normal app update won't add them on its own. **Reinstall imageFS** (see below) to get the improved video / FMV playback. ## ⚠️ Updating from an older version — please reinstall imageFS This release adds the **ffmpeg-8** libraries to imageFS, so **reinstalling imageFS is recommended** to get the improved video / FMV decode. It's also the fix if a game fails to start after updating. **How:** open the app's **Settings → scroll to the bottom → Reinstall ImageFS**. ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-1.7-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-1.7-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-1.7-ludashi.apk` | ## 🙏 Credits **This release** - The **Steam store** download/auth engine is built on **[JavaSteam](https://github.com/Longi94/JavaSteam)** (Longi94) and its DepotDownloader. - The **Components installer** uses Bottles/winetricks-format dependency manifests, mirroring the **[Bottles](https://github.com/bottlesdevs/dependencies)** dependency catalog. - The bundled **Winlator File Manager** and the **Vulkan rendering** path are from **[StevenMXZ](https://github.com/StevenMXZ/Winlator-Ludashi)** (Winlator-Ludashi). **Project & upstream** - **[brunodev85](https://github.com/brunodev85/winlator)** — original Winlator (Wine + Box64 + Turnip on Android), the foundation of this fork. - **[coffincolors](https://github.com/coffincolors/winlator)** — the `cmod` Winlator fork this codebase builds on. - **[Pipetto-crypto](https://github.com/Pipetto-crypto/winlator)** — Winlator Bionic + Box64 fix branch (the "Bionic" half of Star Bionic). - **[jacojayy](https://github.com/jacojayy/star)** & the **[star-emu](https://github.com/star-emu)** team — the Star Bionic / Winlator Frost lines this build continues, plus Turnip Timeline-Semaphore patches. - **[isygold](https://github.com/isygold/vegas-releases)** — Star Engine / VEGAS DXVK fork. 📖 **[VEGAS DXVK FAQ](https://htmlpreview.github.io/?https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator/blob/main/docs/vegas_faq.html)** · 🚀 **Support VEGAS Development** — low-level graphics dev & vibecoder: debugging, refactoring & improving original DXVK code for Adreno. **[❤️ Sponsor →](https://github.com/sponsors/isygold)** - Frame generation: **[xXJSONDeruloXx](https://github.com/xXJSONDeruloXx/bionic-fg)** (bionic-fg) · **[PancakeTAS](https://github.com/PancakeTAS/lsfg-vk)** (lsfg-vk) · **[FrankBarretta](https://github.com/FrankBarretta/lsfg-vk-android)** (Android port). `Lossless.dll` is user-supplied ([Lossless Scaling](https://store.steampowered.com/app/993090/Lossless_Scaling/) by THS). - Upstream stack: **Wine** ([WineHQ](https://www.winehq.org/)) · **Box64** ([ptitSeb](https://github.com/ptitSeb)) · **FEXCore** ([FEX-Emu](https://github.com/FEX-Emu)) · **DXVK** ([doitsujin](https://github.com/doitsujin)) · **VKD3D-Proton** ([HansKristian-Work](https://github.com/HansKristian-Work)) · **Turnip/Mesa** ([Freedreno](https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/mesa/mesa)). Full credits are in the [README](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator#-credits). ---

releases.name: Bannerlator 1.6
releases.body:
# Bannerlator 1.6 Run Windows apps and games on Android — no PC and no root required. ## ✨ What's New ### 🧰 Compatibility Layers download menu Browse, download, install and remove versions of every translation layer from one place. Each component — **Wine/Proton, DXVK, VKD3D, Box64/WOWBox64, FEXCore** — now has a cloud button that opens a dedicated downloader with: - **Wine/Proton tabs** to switch between the two layers - An **"in use"** marker for the version the container is currently running - **Install from file** for local `.wcp` packages - **Byte-accurate download and install progress bars** > 🍷 **Now available to download:** the latest rebuild of **WinNative Proton 11 beta 5 (with Steam)** is offered as a compatibility layer straight from this menu — thanks to the **WinNative team**. ### 🎚️ Standalone FPS limiter The in-game FPS cap now works with **any** frame-generation engine — **Off, bionic-fg, or lsfg-vk** — on **both** the OpenGL and Vulkan host renderers, live. When lsfg-vk is multiplying (2×+), the limiter automatically steps aside so lsfg's own pacing governs — no double-cap. ### 🖥️ Advanced Vulkan settings Native Rendering+, present mode, texture filter and swap-R/B from the container's Vulkan Settings now actually take effect. The Renderer dropdown also shows the correct name and its settings button right away. ### 🎮 Per-game overrides Set **Renderer**, **Frame-Gen engine** and **FPS limiter** per game, each falling back to the container default. ### 🛠️ Fixes & improvements - **File Manager** — safe paste/move (no more data loss), run a `.exe` directly in its container, system **Back** now goes up one folder, and accurate copy progress. - **On-screen controls** — the D-pad / analog stick no longer freezes on multi-touch. - **Frame generation** now starts **off** in-game on every launch; re-enable it any time from the in-game Graphics drawer. ## ⚠️ Updating from an older version If a game fails to start after updating, **reinstall imageFS**: open the app's **Settings → scroll to the bottom → Reinstall ImageFS**. ## 📥 Downloads | Variant | File | |---|---| | **Standard** | `Bannerlator-1.6-standard.apk` | | **PUBG** | `Bannerlator-1.6-pubg.apk` | | **Ludashi** | `Bannerlator-1.6-ludashi.apk` | ## 🙏 Credits **This release** - The standalone **FPS limiter** — its guest-side pacing (delaying the X11 Present `IdleNotify` so the game itself throttles, plus the rule that lsfg-vk governs when its multiplier is ≥ 2) — was ported from **[GameNative](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative)** (utkarshdalal). - The **download menu** follows the pattern of the app's built-in Adrenotools GPU-driver downloader. - The bundled **Winlator File Manager** and the **Vulkan rendering** path are from **[StevenMXZ](https://github.com/StevenMXZ/Winlator-Ludashi)** (Winlator-Ludashi). - The **WinNative team** — for the latest rebuild of **Proton 11 beta 5 (with Steam)**, available as a downloadable compatibility layer. **Project & upstream** - **[brunodev85](https://github.com/brunodev85/winlator)** — original Winlator (Wine + Box64 + Turnip on Android), the foundation of this fork. - **[coffincolors](https://github.com/coffincolors/winlator)** — the `cmod` Winlator fork this codebase builds on. - **[Pipetto-crypto](https://github.com/Pipetto-crypto/winlator)** — Winlator Bionic + Box64 fix branch (the "Bionic" half of Star Bionic). - **[jacojayy](https://github.com/jacojayy/star)** & the **[star-emu](https://github.com/star-emu)** team — the Star Bionic / Winlator Frost lines this build continues, plus Turnip Timeline-Semaphore patches. - **[isygold](https://github.com/isygold/vegas-releases)** — Star Engine / VEGAS DXVK fork. 📖 **[VEGAS DXVK FAQ](https://htmlpreview.github.io/?https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator/blob/main/docs/vegas_faq.html)** · 🚀 **Support VEGAS Development** — low-level graphics dev & vibecoder: debugging, refactoring & improving original DXVK code for Adreno. **[❤️ Sponsor →](https://github.com/sponsors/isygold)** - Frame generation: **[xXJSONDeruloXx](https://github.com/xXJSONDeruloXx/bionic-fg)** (bionic-fg) · **[PancakeTAS](https://github.com/PancakeTAS/lsfg-vk)** (lsfg-vk) · **[FrankBarretta](https://github.com/FrankBarretta/lsfg-vk-android)** (Android port). `Lossless.dll` is user-supplied ([Lossless Scaling](https://store.steampowered.com/app/993090/Lossless_Scaling/) by THS). - Upstream stack: **Wine** ([WineHQ](https://www.winehq.org/)) · **Box64** ([ptitSeb](https://github.com/ptitSeb)) · **FEXCore** ([FEX-Emu](https://github.com/FEX-Emu)) · **DXVK** ([doitsujin](https://github.com/doitsujin)) · **VKD3D-Proton** ([HansKristian-Work](https://github.com/HansKristian-Work)) · **Turnip/Mesa** ([Freedreno](https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/mesa/mesa)). Full credits are in the [README](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator#-credits). ---

releases.name: Bannerlator 1.5
releases.body:
## Bannerlator 1.5
### ⚠️ Updating from 1.4? You MUST reinstall imageFS If you are **updating over an existing 1.4 install**, you need to **reinstall imageFS** after updating — open the app's **Settings**, scroll all the way to the **bottom**, and tap **Reinstall ImageFS**. Skipping this can leave the app in a broken/inconsistent state after the update.
**Performance** - Now ships **release builds** instead of debug. This was implemented to reduce the user-interface lag that some users had noticed. Device-confirmed. **Fixes & additions since 1.4** - GOG store login white-screen fixed (login WebView now completes the OAuth handshake). - Start menu: bundled **AIO Graphics Test** and **Game Controller Test** apps, with `.lnk` working-directory ("Start in") support. - Winlator File Manager (WFM): drives now show the correct "Local Disk" icon (StevenMXZ v3.1 hotfix build). APKs: standard / ludashi / pubg flavors, AOSP-testkey signed (updates install over previous installs). ## Changelog | Commit | Title | |--------|--------| | `a31bc4b` | chore(release): bump to 1.5 (versionCode 23) | | `b6d2dcf` | merge: ship RELEASE builds (fixes Compose UI lag), device-confirmed | | `ad5c99d` | fix(build): drop hand-committed assets/dexopt/baseline.prof(m) | | `ce66eb1` | fix(build): exclude okhttp-coroutines artifact (dup of vendored shim) | | `b62915a` | docs(progress): release-build switch + Game Controller Test + .lnk workingDir + APK size audit | | `d403d2e` | fix(build): crunchPngs false on release (GIF-as-.png drawables fail AAPT2) | | `84eda8c` | chore(build): ship RELEASE builds, not debug | | `403cd64` | merge: .lnk working directory (Start in) for start-menu shortcuts | | `9994581` | fix(startmenu): support .lnk working directory (Start in) for shortcuts | | `4a3e974` | merge: bundle Game Controller Test in Start menu | --- **Credits** The bundled **Winlator File Manager (`wfm.exe`)** — including the corrected "Local Disk" drive-icon behaviour shipped in 1.5 — is from **StevenMXZ's [Winlator-Ludashi 3.1 hotfix](https://github.com/StevenMXZ/Winlator-Ludashi/releases)** release. Thanks to StevenMXZ.

releases.name: Bannerlator 1.4
releases.body: ## Changelog | Commit | Title | |--------|--------| | `4fb3ecb` | release: bump to 1.4 (versionCode 22) + splash V 1.4 | | `6a69195` | fix(taskmgr): poll on render-independent Handler, not Compose frame clock | | `c318457` | docs(progress): 2026-06-22 — lsfg live reload confirmed, Off fix proven, engine badge, TM-on-Vulkan bug | | `740e779` | feat(framegen): show running engine badge in in-game FG drawer | | `e75d1d4` | debug(winhandler): log Task Manager process round-trip (INIT/send/recv/GET_PROCESS) | | `260600b` | docs(progress): lsfg-vk device test — works (2x) but no live reload on our stack | | `80e238a` | fix(lsfg-vk): in-game "Off" writes passthrough (multiplier 1), not 2 | | `ebdb752` | docs(progress): lsfg-vk second FG engine — recon, spike, integration, in-game live | | `7f7ffb5` | feat(lsfg-vk): unify FG controls — in-game drives the selected engine; container = engine choice only | | `1997a55` | feat(lsfg-vk): live in-game config via GameNative fork + conf.toml; gray-out + per-container mult/flow | ### Highlights - **lsfg-vk** added as a second selectable frame-generation engine (Off / bionic-fg / lsfg-vk), with a per-container engine selector, an in-game engine badge, and live in-game multiplier/flow-scale control for whichever engine the container runs. Bring your own `Lossless.dll` via the Settings DLL picker. - **Fixed:** in-game Task Manager was empty on the Vulkan host renderer — refresh now runs on a render-independent timer, so the process list populates on both Vulkan and OpenGL. - **Fixed:** in-game "Off" now correctly writes passthrough instead of forcing 2×. ### ⚠️ lsfg-vk requires your own Lossless.dll Bannerlator bundles **no** proprietary Lossless Scaling files. To use the **lsfg-vk** engine you must own **[Lossless Scaling](https://store.steampowered.com/app/993090/Lossless_Scaling/)** (THS, on Steam) and import its `Lossless.dll` via **Settings → Frame Generation (lsfg-vk) → pick DLL**. The option stays greyed out until a valid DLL is imported. **bionic-fg** needs no DLL and works out of the box. ### 🙏 Credits for this release - **Frame Generation (lsfg-vk)** — layer by **[PancakeTAS](https://github.com/PancakeTAS/lsfg-vk)**; Android/bionic port by **[FrankBarretta](https://github.com/FrankBarretta/lsfg-vk-android)**; in-game live `conf.toml` reload from **[GameNative](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative)** (utkarshdalal); `Lossless.dll` from **[Lossless Scaling](https://store.steampowered.com/app/993090/Lossless_Scaling/)** (THS, user-supplied). - **Frame Generation (bionic-fg)** — **[xXJSONDeruloXx](https://github.com/xXJSONDeruloXx/bionic-fg)** (bundled with permission). - **Vulkan host renderer** — rendering path from **[StevenMXZ](https://github.com/StevenMXZ/Winlator-Ludashi)** (Winlator-Ludashi); `AHardwareBuffer` present path that makes it display correctly ported from / cross-examined against **[GameNative](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative)**. - Task Manager-on-Vulkan fix and the Compose UI / in-game overlay are Bannerlator's own work, building on the upstream **[Winlator](https://github.com/brunodev85/winlator)** lineage. Full attribution: see [Credits in the README](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator#-credits).

releases.name: Bannerlator 1.3
releases.body: Please keep in mind this is my personal build for me, the AI frame generation will be refined over time . I did my best to get it working for a release and functional for anybody that decides to use this or test ## Changelog | Commit | Title | |--------|--------| | `89b2184` | ci(release): serialize flavor packaging to avoid parallel-package OOM | | `89cef5e` | docs: link upstream PR #6 in report | | `79f7458` | docs: rewrite bionic-fg upstream report to solved state (PR companion) | | `c20cb0e` | docs(progress): log FG/FPS-limiter merge to main + version relabel to 1.3 | | `90ce00b` | version: bump splash + about version label to V 1.3 (color unchanged, stays grey) | | `9ee5cb2` | version: set versionName 1.3 (was 1.4-marcescene), bump versionCode 21 | | `eb30d1b` | ci(artifacts): build all 3 flavors sequentially (avoid parallel-package OOM) | | `ddf46fb` | Merge bionic-fg frame generation + FPS limiter into main | | `f39b96a` | fix(icon): inset launcher foreground so adaptive mask stops clipping the border | | `9871eef` | fix(ui): dark-theme value controls in Box64/FEXCore preset dialogs | ### ✨ New in 1.3 — AI Frame Generation - **AI Frame Generation (bionic-fg)** — per-container toggle plus live in-game controls. Pick **Off / 2× / 3× / 4×** from the in-game Graphics drawer, with a flow-scale slider. Confirmed on both OpenGL and Vulkan host renderers. - **FPS Limiter** — cap the base frame rate (10–200) so on-screen FPS = limit × multiplier — steadier, smoother interpolation and lower power. - In-game HUD CPU-temperature fix; Box64/FEXCore preset-dialog readability fix; launcher-icon corner fix. Frame generation device-confirmed on Adreno 750 (Turnip / DXVK 2.4.1), 2×/3×/4× all working. --- ## 🙏 Frame Generation credit Bannerlator's AI Frame Generation is powered by **[bionic-fg](https://github.com/xXJSONDeruloXx/bionic-fg)** — the Android/bionic Vulkan frame-generation layer by **[xXJSONDeruloXx](https://github.com/xXJSONDeruloXx)**, included in-tree as a git submodule with the author's kind permission. Huge thanks for the work and for allowing it to ship here. - **Project:** https://github.com/xXJSONDeruloXx/bionic-fg - **Our upstream contribution (PR):** https://github.com/xXJSONDeruloXx/bionic-fg/pull/6 — *Single-device mode + layer-dispatch routing to make bionic-fg work on Android wrapper ICDs (Winlator/Turnip)*, the changes that enable frame generation on this stack, offered back upstream.

releases.name: Bannerlator 1.2
releases.body: ## Changelog | Commit | Title | |--------|--------| | `a598584` | chore(splash): bump version label V 1.1 -> V 1.2 for the 1.2 release | | `552a28e` | docs: log new neon launcher icon + adaptive corner-clip fix (neonicon build) | | `19d62f8` | feat(icon): new neon gamepad launcher icon (all densities, all 3 flavors) | | `0f3884e` | docs: log native-rendering toggle device test + HUD-freeze fix (hudfix build) | | `f724ec2` | fix(render): tick the perf HUD on the Vulkan FLIP/scanout present path | | `df3a5c7` | ui(drawer): gray out Screen Effects on the Vulkan renderer too | | `ba06bc3` | ui(drawer): gray out SGSR/HDR on the Vulkan renderer (GL-only effects) | | `779967a` | fix(render): wire in-game Native Rendering toggle + restore perf HUD on Vulkan | | `bd632e4` | docs: log the Vulkan/DXVK/vkd3d black-screen fix (vkfix3, both renderers confirmed) | | `7d5c9f8` | fix(render): port GameNative AHB present path so Vulkan content renders on GL+Vulkan | ## Bannerlator 1.2 **Rendering** - Fixed Vulkan / DXVK / vkd3d game content rendering **black** on both the OpenGL and Vulkan host renderers (full AHardwareBuffer present path is now wired end-to-end). - In-game **Native Rendering+** toggle now works on the Vulkan renderer (lower-latency direct scanout). - Fixed the performance HUD freezing while Native Rendering was enabled. - Screen Effects / SGSR / HDR are correctly grayed out on the Vulkan renderer (OpenGL-only effects). **Content / installs** - DXVK / VKD3D / Vegas version lists now refresh immediately after a download. **UI** - New neon gamepad app icon (fits the launcher mask cleanly — no clipped corners). - Splash version bumped to V 1.2. ## Credits The render-options fix in this release — the `AHardwareBuffer` present path that makes Vulkan / DXVK / VKD3D content render correctly on **both** the OpenGL and Vulkan host renderers, plus the **Native Rendering+** direct-scanout path — was ported from and cross-examined against [**GameNative**](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative) by **utkarshdalal**. Thank you. See the full [Credits](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator#-credits). --- > 🚀 **Support VEGAS Development** — VEGAS is the Adreno-optimized DXVK fork used in this build. Low-level graphics dev & vibecoder: debugging, refactoring & improving original DXVK code for Adreno. **[❤️ Sponsor isygold →](https://github.com/sponsors/isygold)** > > 📖 **Help & info:** **[VEGAS DXVK FAQ](https://htmlpreview.github.io/?https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator/blob/main/docs/vegas_faq.html)** — install, config, FSR, tiers, frame generation & shader-stutter troubleshooting.

Fixes - Local .wcp install no longer crashes (DXVK, VKD3D and Box64) — the file picker no longer throws a ClassCastException under themed dialogs/sheets. - arm64ec containers now correctly show WOWBox64 (section title, Version, Preset, and the download sheet) instead of Box64. - The 'Installing…' dialog now dismisses when an install fails, instead of spinning forever. - Contents/components: installed items float to the top with a light-blue installed check. releases.body: ## Changelog | Commit | Title | |--------|--------| | `68f60b6` | release(ci): Bannerlator-branded v1.0 release — rename APKs, own banner, mark as Latest (non-prerelease) | | `16d47c5` | docs(readme): fix Information section — marcescence base, V 1.0, app label, lineage | | `c84efa1` | fix(settings): overlap was a Compose Box bug; + splash v1.0 + app label | | `0b8a40c` | docs: log marcescence re-import + splash branding port | | `ad67a6a` | splash: use Bannerlator banner logo + drop title text (port to marcescence star-pkg splash) | | `c55fe68` | re-import: rebase source onto star @ marcescence (1.4-marcescene) | | `6155280` | docs: log splash rebrand, branding work, 2nd green build | | `8fdbf97` | feat(splash): use Bannerlator banner logo, drop title text, version v1.0 | | `b5e1b35` | docs: credit isygold (Star Engine / VEGAS DXVK fork) | | `23ebd5d` | docs: credit original Star/Frost dev team (star-emu) and leegao | First stable Bannerlator release — built on Star @ marcescence (1.4-marcescene). Vulkan renderer, Jetpack Compose Settings/Input Controls (overlap fix), SteamGridDB cover scrape, drive/container pickers. Three flavors: standard, ludashi, pubg. App label: Bannerlator Bionic. --- > 🚀 **Support VEGAS Development** — VEGAS is the Adreno-optimized DXVK fork used in this build. Low-level graphics dev & vibecoder: debugging, refactoring & improving original DXVK code for Adreno. **[❤️ Sponsor isygold →](https://github.com/sponsors/isygold)** > > 📖 **Help & info:** **[VEGAS DXVK FAQ](https://htmlpreview.github.io/?https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator/blob/main/docs/vegas_faq.html)** — install, config, FSR, tiers, frame generation & shader-stutter troubleshooting.

releases.name: ARMSX2 2.6.1.2
releases.body:
ARMSX2 2.6.1.2 

Performance:

The Vulkan renderer now submits work mid-frame in games that read the GS back. Some games synchronously read graphics memory every frame, occlusion tests like sun glare are the common case, and that read has to wait for everything queued before it. Previously the GPU only started working at that point, so the frame became the CPU's recording time and the GPU's work back to back instead of side by side. Work is now submitted as it accumulates, so the GPU runs while the next commands are still being recorded and the read finds its results already waiting. Games that never read the GS back are unaffected, and nothing about what gets drawn changes. Thanks to @bmdhacks for this! 

Reapplied PGO, it had grown stale from numerous changes and needed to be rebuilt against the current code, making for a more optimized compiler. It had drifted far enough behind that the graphics code's hottest paths were being compiled with no profile at all, including the main draw function. Those paths are guided again.

Game titles:

Games now take their name from the game database rather than the filename, so titles are consistent and free of dump markers. Japanese releases show their actual Japanese titles, and sort by their kana reading rather than by character codes, which is what makes an alphabetized list mean anything in Japanese. There is an English titles option in the library menu for anyone who prefers romanized names, and search matches both, so a game listed in Japanese still answers to its English name. Titles refresh on the next library scan, so be sure to hit refresh in the game library if you don't yet see it.

Auto-save while playing:

Save states can now be written automatically at an interval you choose, from one to thirty minutes, so a crash or a flat battery costs at most that much progress. It writes the same auto-save slot the existing on-exit option uses, so your numbered slots stay your own and auto-load on boot picks it up unchanged. Off by default. Saving briefly pauses the game, so a short interval is noticeable, five minutes is a reasonable place to start.

Controller skins:

Skin packs that draw the left and right analog sticks differently now show both correctly. Previously the two stick images were treated as one, so whichever the pack listed second was drawn under both sticks, a pack marking its sticks L and R showed R on both. Packs that provide a single shared stick image, including the bundled ones, are unchanged. If you already imported an affected pack you will need to import it again, because the two images were merged as the pack was installed and only one of them was kept. This bug is now fixed. 

Interface scaling:

UI Size and UI Font Size in the On-Screen tab now do what they say. Both settings saved and restored correctly but were never applied to anything since the new UI change, so the sliders moved and the interface did not. They now scale the library, the pause menu and the settings screens. The game image and the on-screen touch controls are deliberately left alone, since the pad is positioned by your own touch layout and resizing it would move the buttons out from under your thumbs.

releases.name: ARMSX2 2.6.1.1
releases.body:
ARMSX2 2.6.1.1

What's new: 

Added modifiable Shader parameters, every RetroArch shader preset exposes its own settings, and you can now change them. Opening Shader Parameters brings up a full-screen editor: pick a parameter, adjust it, and watch the game change behind the list as you do. It works with a controller (D-pad to move and adjust) or by touch, and it handles the very large presets. There is a confirmation before going through with a reset. Reset all restores the preset's own defaults and asks first, because it can't be undone.

Once you've dialed a preset in, save it under a name with the on screen keyboard (works with a controller as well). It appears under My Presets in the Shader Preset list, so you can pick it any time, and set it for one game only like any other preset. Reopening it shows your values as its defaults, so Reset means back to what you saved. A saved preset points at the shader pack it was built from, so keep that pack installed.

Shader packs:

You can now install a pack you already have, from a folder or a .zip. Retro Crisis GDV-NTSC is available as a new download, it's a preset collection built on the RetroArch pack's shaders, so it installs into that pack and needs it first, and its entry stays disabled until then. The GitHub mirror of the main pack has been removed, it did not install reliably and the buildbot download is the one RetroArch itself uses.

Controller mapping profiles:

Save your button map, stick modes and stick binds under a name and pick it back later. A profile applies to the player and scope you're editing, so you can keep one map per game. Profiles are written to the inputprofiles folder alongside touch layouts, so they survive moving your data folder and can be shared. 

Per-game controller skins:

A game can have its own per-game controller skin now. Open a game's settings, turn on Use a different skin for this game, and pick one, every other game keeps what you had. Skins are also reachable from the in-game menu now, next to the touch layout editor in the controller tab.

Macros:

Added more Macros, can fire the D-pad, the analog toggle, the pressure modifier and either stick's directions, not just the face and shoulder buttons. Each macro also has a Frequency: leave it on Hold for the old behaviour, or set an interval to turn it into a turbo that toggles while held.

Fixes:

Patch and cheat settings now follow the Global and Per-Game switch. That page always read and wrote global settings regardless of the switch, so a setting meant for one game was saved for every game, and the in-game menu's patch rows, which did respect the switch, contradicted it.

A per-game setting now stays with the game once you set it. Previously a per-game value that happened to match the global one was not recorded, so later changing the global setting silently changed it for that game too. Settings you never touched for a game still follow the global one.

Launching from a front-end such as Cocoon or iiSU for example now shows the Global and Per-Game switch, and saves to the tier it says. Previously the game had no identity in the app, so the switch was missing while settings were still saved per game.

Touch profile lists scroll. Past four profiles the rest were pushed off the screen and could not be reached, which looked like a limit. Nothing was ever limited, but more than four profiles would get blocked off from view.

releases.name: ARMSX2 2.6.1
releases.body:
ARMSX2 2.6.1 

What's new:

Added RetroArch shaders, ARMSX2 now runs RetroArch .slangp shader chains on both Vulkan and OpenGL/GLES, via librashader. Download shader packs directly in-app, the same way GPU drivers already work, and pick a preset from Settings --> Renderer ---> Shader Preset once you've enabled RetroArch Shaders, you can also access it from the in-game menu without leaving your game. Preset changes apply live.

Presets are grouped by family (Mega Bezel, koko-aio, CRT, Handheld, and so on), collapsed by default, with each family split by its own folders so nothing stalls when you open the picker. Every preset shows its pass count as information, that number is not a performance prediction, and we deliberately don't rank presets by it: xBRZ runs heavy at ~5 passes, while koko-aio's ambilight runs great at ~19, because cost depends on per-pass resolution and shader complexity rather than pass count.

Fixes:

Add to home screen never actually pinned anything. The launcher was rejecting the full-size cover art as a shortcut icon, the error was being swallowed, and a legacy fallback that Android has ignored since 8.0 reported success, so even the failure toast never appeared. Icons are now scaled correctly and real failures surface.

Audio stayed muted after leaving the in-game menu open for a while, once Android reclaimed the audio stream. It now reopens on resume.

Added Force Framebuffer Fetch (MediaTek Mali), which is off by default. For Mali users on Vulkan only.
Off (default): correct picture, slower.
On: faster, but some MediaTek drivers show black or missing textures, will vary by device.

Added Gyro hotkeys, bind Gyro On/Off (toggle) and Gyro (hold to aim) to any button. Toggle flips gyro aiming on and off, hold enables it only while the button is held, so you can leave gyro out of the way until you're actually aiming. 

Exit to Launcher now actually exits to your launcher. With "Exit to Launcher on Close (External Games)" enabled, quitting a game you launched from ES-DE or another frontend returns you to that frontend, instead of dropping you into the ARMSX2 library.

Other

Added three About sections in the left bar menu with Discord, GitHub and Website links.

### Credits

RetroArch shader support in ARMSX2 stands entirely on other people's work:

**[librashader](https://github.com/SnowflakePowered/librashader)** by
  SnowflakePowered, the runtime that compiles and executes the shader
  chains on both Vulkan and OpenGL. 

**[libretro / RetroArch](https://github.com/libretro/RetroArch)** the
  `.slangp` preset format and the [slang-shaders](https://github.com/libretro/slang-shaders) collection.
  Shader packs are downloaded from the libretro buildbot, nothing is bundled
  with ARMSX2.

**The shader authors.** Every preset is someone's craft, and the good ones
  represent years of it, among many others: **HyperspaceMadness**
  (Mega Bezel), **kokoko3k** (koko-aio), **TroggleMonkey** (crt-royale),
  **Themaister** (the slang shader spec and many core shaders), **Hyllian**
  (xBR family), **guest.r**, **Lottes**, **cgwg** (crt-geom).
  Each preset carries its own licence and author in its `.slangp`/`.slang`
  files. 

If you maintain a shader pack and I've credited you wrongly or missed you,
please open an issue and I'll fix it.
