releases.name: ARMSX2 2.6.0.5
releases.body:
ARMSX2 2.6.0.5 

What's new: 

Gyro + stick now work together. Aim gyro (and steering gyro) is now added on top of the physical stick instead of fighting it, use the right stick for coarse aim and tilt to fine-tune, both at once. The other stick (movement) is unaffected while you aim.

Fixed: per-game stick fixes leaked to every game. Right/Left Stick Invert X/Y, Swap X/Y, and D-pad as Left Stick now save per game when set in a game's properties (they were writing the global setting for all games).

Added Per-game BIOS option. Long-press a game --> Per-game BIOS button to boot that specific game with its own BIOS (like for a EU disc on a EU BIOS, a US disc on a US BIOS). Global BIOS is the default you choose, "Use global" reverts it.

Online patches now work from the library. The patch/cheat browser reads the game's serial straight from the disc, so you can browse and install patches from a game's per-game settings without booting it first (previously it only worked in-game).

ANGLE is now an OpenGL driver option. When you select the OpenGL renderer, the driver picker shows an ANGLE option (GLES-on-Vulkan), the OpenGL counterpart to the Vulkan driver list. Helps non-Adreno GPUs (Mali/Xclipse/PowerVR) whose native OpenGL driver is weak. Available in both the Renderer settings and the in-game Graphics menu.

New GPU driver source: ExynosTools (WearyConcern1165/ExynosTools) added to the in-app driver downloader, helps Xclipse devices on Vulkan.

releases.name: ARMSX2 2.6.0.4
releases.body:
ARMSX2 2.6.0.4

What's new: 

Fast-Forward button in the in-game menu, the Session tab now has a one-tap Fast-Forward (between Continue and Restart). It kicks the game to max speed and resumes, reopen the menu and it shows an "On" state to switch it back off. (Same as the Frame limit button, but more obvious). 

GPU driver recommendation, added back the feature from the old UI,  the driver picker now shows your detected GPU model and the recommended driver source for it. Appears in both the in-game menu's driver section and full Settings.

For Gyro, pick the aim stick option, Aim mode can now drive the Right or Left analog stick (per-game), for games that aim with the left stick such as Resident Evil 4.

Fixes: 

Cheats/patches on-off toggle now works on PNACH files saved with (CRLF) line endings, no more "Couldn't update … (unusual formatting)" popup. Fixes to the Online browser manager as well. 

More GameDB game fixes now baked in, thanks to @XDarkFallenX and tester Jer for helping with these!

MotoGP 3: Minimum Blending Level 0, fixes transparency effects

Evil Dead: A Fistful of Boomstick, Minimum Blending Level 0,  fixes transparency effects

Resident Evil: Outbreak, Minimum Blending Level 0,  fixes transparency effects

Marvel: Ultimate Alliance, Instant VU1 off + MTVU off, fixes broken rendering

SBK 09: Superbike World Championship, MTVU off, fixes broken rendering

Cy Girls (Ice & Aska discs), Round Sprite 2,  fixes NPCs walking on-screen

Plus upstream GS texture-cache improvements (better clears, format-overlap handling, preload). 

Enjoy!

releases.name: ARMSX2 2.6.0.3
releases.body:
ARMSX2 Refresh 2.6.0.3

What's new: 

Custom achievement unlock sounds: pick any audio file (like the Xbox 360 achievement sound) for RetroAchievements unlocks, in the RetroAchievements --> options screen.

Hide games from your library: long-press a game --> Hide from library, with a Show hidden games toggle to bring them back and un-hide in the dropdown toolbar in the top right. 

Clear cached data in Settings --> App: frees space by wiping the shader and cover-art caches (they rebuild automatically).

Green Play button in a game's per-game settings to launch it directly.

Optional game titles under cover art now work in the Shelf library layout as well.

Fixes:

Per-game settings no longer stick to the last game after closing it, and now open correctly from the Shelf layout.

Fixed the ANGLE renderer crashing / black-screening on Mali-G77 devices (Retroid Pocket 4 Pro, etc.). This mode is recommended for many Mali users, and may help other kinds of devices as well.

Major Vulkan speedup on Mali GPUs, brought back accurate blending now runs in-tile instead of the slow per-primitive barrier fallback (re-enabled the fast feedback-loop path modern Mali drivers support, was present in V38 but was lost in the unification).

The in-game Performance tab is now a yellow ⚡.

releases.name: ARMSX2 2.6.0.2
releases.body:
2.6.0.2
• Jak 3: per-game graphics fixes (all regions)
• New game fixes: Baldur's Gate: Dark Alliance, Atelier Iris

releases.name: ARMSX2 2.6.0.1
releases.body:
Fixes:

Controller remapping works again, fixes the 2.6.0 regression where the [Bind] popup didn't register button presses. 

In-game Render tab: picking a resolution below Native now actually applies (was snapping back to Native).

RetroAchievements unlock sound no longer occasionally fails to play.

New:

Black and OLED themes (pure black) alongside System / Light / Dark.

Boot BIOS directly with no game, from the menu.

Scroll memory, the in-game menu and the Settings screen remember where you were when you reopen them (great for the long Fixes list).

RetroAchievements panel shows the game's file type (ISO/CHD/BIN) next to the Hardcore badge.

Per-game (GameDB):

Ar Tonelico 1 & 2: Round Sprite = Full (vertical lines in FMVs/portraits).
Aeon Flux: EE Cycle Rate 130% (performance).
Musashi: Samurai Legend: Merge Sprite on (cutscene ghosting).
Tekken 4, 5 & Tag Tournament (all regions): clamp fixes (camera, coin-noise, effects).

Added a button in the three dot drop down in the top game library toolbar to re-access the setup screen to change APP/BIOS/ROM folder.

releases.name: ARMSX2 2.6.0
releases.body:
A feature and compatibility release. Highlights are gyroscope motion controls, an optional ANGLE renderer path for devices with weak OpenGL drivers, in-game disc swapping, safer settings migration, and a batch of per-game fixes.

New features:

Gyroscope motion controls. Two modes: Aim, for camera look in shooters, and Steering, for tilt in racers. Sensitivity, smoothing, and axis inversion are all adjustable, and every option can be set globally or per game. Found in the Controls tab. Thank you to @sashkinbro for this feature! 

OpenGL via ANGLE. An optional toggle that runs the OpenGL renderer through ANGLE, translating OpenGL ES to Vulkan with a bundled driver. Useful on devices whose native OpenGL ES driver is broken or slow. In the Renderer tab, applies on game restart. Thank you to @sashkinbro for this feature!

Swap Disc without closing the game. A new Swap Disc action in the in-game menu changes the mounted disc while the game keeps running. Made for multi-disc titles and for cheat discs such as CodeBreaker or GameShark that hand off to a game disc.

Xclipse GPU profile. Samsung Xclipse (Exynos) devices can now be selected explicitly in the GPU Profile list.

Show game names under every game. The library can display titles beneath all cover art, not just favorites. Toggle it from the library overflow (three-dot) menu.

FXAA anti-aliasing. A fast post-process anti-aliasing pass that smooths jagged edges with a light blur. In the Renderer tab.

CAS sharpening. AMD FidelityFX Contrast-Adaptive Sharpening to crisp up the image, with an adjustable sharpness slider and a choice of Sharpen or Sharpen plus Resize. In the Renderer tab.

Improvements: 

Immersive library. The Android navigation bar now stays hidden in the library and menus, matching gameplay, and reappears on an edge swipe.

Achievements in story order. The RetroAchievements list follows the game's progression order instead of sorting alphabetically, so it is clear what to unlock next.

Settings survive a reinstall onto an existing folder. If you reinstall and point setup at a data folder you already have, your global settings and per-game overrides are recovered instead of resetting. Settings are also mirrored into the data folder so this keeps working on future reinstalls. Those migrating from builds with the old UI should also now have their previous settings restored. 

Redesigned in-game menu icons. The pause menu tab icons now match their functions (display, wrench, gamepad, gear, trophy).

More settings are searchable. The new gyroscope and ANGLE options now show up in settings search.

Mali-G615. Fixed a startup freeze on the Vulkan renderer caused by broken GPU timing queries on that GPU.

Bug fixes: 

Cheats work again. Named PNACH cheats were silently doing nothing. They now apply correctly from the in-game Patches and cheats manager, whether toggled locally, imported, or installed from the online browser. They remain disabled automatically in RetroAchievements Hardcore.

Fast-forward no longer turns itself off when you change a setting.

Game compatibility (GameDB): 

Kingdom Hearts II. Fixed ghosting and trailing on hardware renderers.

Rumble Racing. Fixed missing car shadows.

Mortal Kombat: Shaolin Monks. Fixed geometry and effect issues.

Avatar: The Last Airbender (The Burning Earth, Into the Inferno). Fixed missing lighting and skin color.

Tekken 5. Fixed camera behavior and the constant coin sound, and fixed broken effects.

MediaTek Mali. Fixed duplicated framebuffer regions in Tekken 5.

Thank you to @sashkinbro for these improvements and @XDarkFallenX for testing these improvements on ARMSX2, on an SD712 no less!

Credits for Unification, New UI, and other improvements present in this Stable release: 
@jpolo1224 @isztldav @bmdhacks @sashkinbro @J1coding @Vivimagic @bagasromadon

releases.body:

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V38
releases.body:
Fixed the major slowdown when loading over the disc/network with block chaining on. Battlefield 2: Modern Combat online play (and any disc-streaming load) no longer freezes for 1-2 minutes, it now loads as fast as it should without giving up the EE block-chaining speed-up. This should help the slow DEV9 HDD / OPL loads being 10s slower as well. 

Added Multitap support, connect up to 8 controllers through the PS2 multitap (Pad tab ---> toggle on), extra players share Player 1's button layout.

Added an OSD Scale, new slider (in Settings ---> Overlay tab) to make the on-screen messages & performance stats bigger or smaller (50–250%).

You can now toggle Perf-Stats (OSD) with a hotkey, bindable in the Hotkeys tab to flip the on-screen stats overlay on/off in-game.

Rapid-fire / Turbo, per-binding Turbo toggle in the Pad tab, hold a button to auto-repeat it.

You can now Export / Import per-game profiles, save or load a game's settings as a file from the Info tab. 

Added GameHub-8Elite driver source to the in-app GPU driver browser.

Added GPU-based driver recommendation, the driver browser now detects your GPU and suggests a matching driver pack, (feel free to use whatever you'd like anyways).

Added D-Pad key spacing in the Touch Layout editor, select the D-Pad to get a spacing slider that spreads the four directions apart (NetherSX2-style).

Added a Multi-touch Reach slider (in the Pad tab ---> On screen touch controls) controlling how far from a button's center a touch still counts, so multi-button presses work with more room between buttons.

Ported a batch of PPSSPP + Dolphin GPU driver-bug workarounds:

Adreno: color-mask-under-depth fix, GL persistent-mapped-buffer crash guard, incomplete-pipeline made non-fatal.

Mali: depth/stencil load-op normalization and a z==w depth nudge to prevent artifacts.
PowerVR: swapchain width alignment fix.

Added Valkyrie Profile 2: Silmeria fixes to every other region version. 

This will be the last version released in this format, we are planning a restructure of the project and distribution of releases for all platforms, opting in favor of more traditional Stable/Nightly releases and one unified master branch. This will take some time, so please enjoy what we've released so far! What comes next is even more exciting. Thanks!

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V37
releases.body:
Added full UI translations for Spanish, Portuguese (Brazil), French, German, Italian, Russian, Polish, Turkish, Japanese, Korean, Chinese (Simplified & Traditional), Arabic, Indonesian, Vietnamese, and Thai. Switch language instantly from the new Language tab in Settings. Full controller support. 

Added Display Filter settings in the Render tab. Display Effects: Nearest (raw pixels), Smooth, or Sharp for how the image scales to your screen. 

Reapplied a new PGO profile since the old one was stale from numerous recompiler changes. Helps performance. 

Faster Mali (Vulkan) readbacks, readback-heavy games, games should stall far less on Mali. (Blending Max for mali can also still be of help for graphical issues, and should be less of a perf hit then before). 

Mali (OpenGL): fixed texture-wrapping + framebuffer-mask corruption from Mali's GLSL compiler, improved vsync stability.

Adreno: faster texture-binding path + CAS sharpening hardened so a driver rejection can't stop the renderer starting.

Broader groundwork across Adreno / Turnip / Xclipse / Mali / PowerVR drivers to match Dolphin and Eden Android on GPU handling. Let me know if you experience improvements! 

Cleaner GPU driver browser, "Browse Online" now groups downloadable drivers by uploader (KIMCHI, Mr Purple, StevenMXZ) into collapsible dropdown menus.

releases.name: ARMSX2 linux 0.0.1
releases.body:
The linux release is equivalent to the MacOS, but compiled for linux.
Enjoy!

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V36
releases.body:
True Crime: New York City. @isztldav Fixed the rainbow/static texture corruption, the flickering & disappearing geometry, and the shimmering street textures. A game that was long considered unfixable on ARM now runs correctly. (The underlying EE fix also helps other BlueTongue-engine games.)

More potential improvements to keeping screen on while playing. 

EE FPU recompiler accuracy fix (the real cause of the True Crime corruption).

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V35
releases.body:
Everybody's Golf 4, Fixed the OpenGL graphical corruption (black-boxed character faces, floating rectangle, blue bars) and the white-polygon flicker. Also fixed the freeze when loading into a match. 

Chaos Legion, Fixed player attacks not registering damage on enemies. 

Ghost Hunter, Fixed the soft-lock on the intro ladder.

Pro Yakyuu Spirits 2010, Fixed the mid-game freeze.

Tales of the Abyss, Fixed the progression stall (couldn't climb stairs). 

Energy Airforce – Aim Strike! Memory-card save data now carries over correctly (ported from PCSX2 #14625).

Mana-Khemia, Fixed pause-menu/bulletin sprite misalignment and the "Best"-version save issue (ported from PCSX2 #14625).

Fixed a class of Adreno OpenGL corruption in games using accurate alpha test, the depth-feedback path was emitting a second framebuffer-fetch output the driver rejected, corrupting the affected draws. Now routed through a single coherent depth fetch (`gl_LastFragDepthARM`), fixing occluded triangles poking through as white shards. (Fixed the graphics in Everybody's Golf 4 on OpenGL, let me know if any other games with weird GL graphics were fixed. 

Improvements to memory card .zip file importing. 

Keep screen on while playing, the display no longer sleeps mid-game after the system idle timeout.

Added a scroll indicator to the settings tabs.

Added a Clear Shader Cache button in the Renderer tab to flush corrupt shader caches.

The Skip MPEG toggle now shows a warning that it's a last-resort FMV hack which can break interactive cutscenes (e.g. God of War II, Battlefield 2), best set per-game, not globally.

Added a DEV9 HDD storage-speed probe to help diagnose slow HDD (OpenPS2Loader) game loads on external/SD storage.

Settings sliders no longer change value when you scroll, scrolling that starts over a slider (like Frame Skip) now scrolls the list instead of jogging the slider, only a deliberate left/right drag or a tap adjusts it.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V34
releases.body:
From a new addition to the ARMSX2 team, @bmdhacks performance improvements: 

microVU FMUL broadcast fold, lane-indexed multiply, less VU-thread time (helps VU-heavy games. Demanding games run smoother. Speeds up how the emulator runs the PS2's 3D/physics unit and main CPU, so heavy titles (God of War, Shadow of the Colossus, GT4, fighters) hold their frame rate better. Biggest gains on stronger chips; weaker devices get some breathing room too.

CNTVCT_EL0 fast clock, reads the hardware counter directly instead of clock_gettime every frame, frees EE-thread headroom on weak cores. Less wasted CPU. Trims a bunch of per-frame overhead, clock checks, memory allocations, redundant reads, which frees up power on lower-end phones and handhelds.

GS vertex-kick made call-free + mid-frame Vulkan readback submit, GPU runs concurrently with recording on readback-heavy frames. Smoother Vulkan graphics. The GPU starts working sooner, so games with lots of on-screen effects (reflections, motion blur) run better, especially on Adreno.

SPU2 NEON mixer, placement-new per-block assembler (no malloc/free per block). Faster audio mixing.

RGB5A1 CPU pack, vtlb overflow-safe range check. A few small rendering and stability fixes.

EE recompiler mixed-operand constant folding, folds a known-constant operand into the instruction instead of a memory load. Smarter CPU code generation. When the emulator already knows one of the numbers a calculation will use, it bakes that number straight into the instruction instead of reading it back from memory each time, a small speedup that applies to basically every game.

More improvements are planned from bmdhacks, so stay tuned! 
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Added FIFO_RELAXED present mode which should help the 60 to 30 fps cliff (tears a late frame instead of stalling a whole refresh).

Real display refresh-rate reporting, pacing now aligns to the actual panel (90/120 Hz), not just 60Hz.

Added Export memory cards, one-tap Export saves a card out to Downloads/Drive/anywhere for backup or migration.

Added a Sustained Performance toggle, hold a steady thermally-sustainable clock for long handheld sessions, (opt-in). 

Fixed an issue with activating Hardcore RetroAchievements while in global settings/the game library, where it would boot you into the BIOS after activation with no indication that HC was active, unlike when you activate in a game. 

Added a New GPU-driver source, StevenMXZ Adreno-Tools-Drivers.

A fix to Analog stick off-axis bleed, "push up also presses right" on both sticks (regression from the radial-deadzone change) fixed by re-adding off-axis noise rejection.

Per-game cheats list now shows only the current game's .pnach (was listing every games).

Fix to a bug where settings sometimes would apply live but not show it. 

Overlay/slider drag, sliders now follow your finger continuously instead of jumping once and stalling.

GameDB fix, Silent Hill 3 FMV black-screen (PCSX2 #14659: dropped the superseded software-renderer FMV hack; kept the deinterlace fix).

Added a new setting, Optimal Frame Pacing. Sets the VSync queue size to 0, making every frame be completed and presented by the GS before input is polled and the next frame begins. Using this setting can reduce input lag at the cost of higher CPU and GPU requirements.

releases.name: ARMSX2 macOS 2.6.0
releases.body:
This release focuses on the native ARM64 EE recompiler — new fastmem path, more ops kept in JIT, and accuracy fixes that bring it closer to the x86 recompiler.

ARM64 recompiler — performance

- Host-MMU fastmem for EE load/store. The hot path is now a single base-relative Ldr/Str (x28 = fastmem base) with a SIGSEGV backpatch to the slow vtlb helper for MMIO/handler pages, replacing the inline vmap lookup on every access. Gated behind CHECK_FASTMEM with the vmap path retained as fallback.
- Fused unaligned 64-bit access. LDL+LDR / SDL+SDR pairs (an unaligned 64-bit access) collapse from the ~30-instruction shift/mask/merge dance into one fastmem Ldr/Str x.
- microVU lane-indexed FMUL fold. Broadcast-operand FMAC multiplies fold the lane select into the multiply (Fmul Vd.4S, Vn.4S, Vm.S[lane]) — bit-identical, one fewer instruction and one fewer live NEON register.
- More EE ops stay in JIT instead of single-stepping the interpreter:
  - MMI MADD/MADDU/MADD1/MADDU1 + MFHI1/MTHI1/MFLO1/MTLO1.
  - Native BC0F/BC0T via CPCOND0, enabling DMA-wait idle-skip.
  - MFC0/MTC0 of Count/PERF inline-interp in-block (the dominant fallback in Count busy-poll games).
- FlushCache/iFlushCache syscalls are skipped with a flat hardware-measured cycle charge, matching the x86 recompiler (the rec doesn't model the CPU cache).
- Per-block assembler is placement-new'd into a thread-local buffer instead of heap-allocated per JIT block — one fewer alloc/free per block compile.

ARM64 recompiler — accuracy / bug fixes

- EE FPU non-full clamp now matches the x86 recompiler, not the interpreter. Non-full clamp modes (eeClampMode 0/1/2) were reproducing the interpreter's denormal-flush and overflow/underflow flag behavior; the recompiler does neither. Fixes True Crime: New York City and other games with an eeClampMode gamefix.
- Mirror x86 recBranchCall behavior for correct event-test timing.

Upstream PCSX2 sync

Merged the latest PCSX2 master, including:

- GS/HW: better ROV discard behavior, force depth ROV when alpha test needs depth feedback, improved texture-creation hazard handling (incl. DX12 fb optimization and imgui).
- Deps: SDL3 updated to v3.4.12.
- GameDB: Gran Turismo fixes, Silent Hill 3 / Mana-Khemia gamefix cleanups, and assorted fixes.
- PAD: latest controller database.

Acknowledgements
- @bmdhacks — yaps2 fork: LDL/LDR fusion, microVU FMUL broadcast fold, placement-new per-block assembler.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/ARMSX2/ARMSX2/compare/MacOSv2.5.2...MacOSv2.6.0

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V33
releases.body:
Fixed some issues with Ratchet: Deadlocked (still some graphical issues, I did not have enough time to look into it, but will in the future when I can). Invisible / stuck-right alignment / flickering model fixed. 

Added new PS2 stick directions (8 new "(send)" rows) triggers stay proportional. (Clear the button row you steal.)

Stick/D-pad + button hotkey combos now bind.

Added Per-stick feel settings: Deadzone / Outer / Anti-Deadzone / Sensitivity / Acceleration separate for L & R (old values migrate).

Radial stick shaping, diagonals should no longer eaten by a square deadzone.

Analog merge layer, multiple writers to one direction stop releasing each other.

Touch stick honors Invert X/Y + Swap (was gamepad-only) + per-stick deadzone.

Added a Slow Down (toggle) hotkey, 50%, mutually exclusive with FF, blocked in RA Hardcore.

Added a Search feature: title/serial, press the Y-button or click on the search toolbar icon, has a gamepad on-screen keyboard. 

Added a Hide Recently Played shelf toggle for one unified library in the top toolbar.

Added Display Resolution (HW scaler), Screen / 3× / 2× / 1× PS2 in the perf tab.

Adreno framebuffer-fetch fast path default-ON + migration, let me know if this helps, you can opt it off if you notice issues in the render tab.

Added a Per-game memory card picker in-game + card manager in game.

Fixed per-game memcard issues (compared to global instead of factory mcd001.ps2).

You can now fully control the Save-state and load state menu with a controller. 

Fixed DeX + first-cold-launch crash. 

Fixes for Chinese BIOS, copies ROM1/ROM2/EROM siblings (re-run BIOS setup once for Chinese BIOS users).

Fixed BIOS self-copy truncation.

Added a "My local patches & cheats" unified browser, as well as PNACH 2.0 support.

Fixed per-cheat toggle being ignored. 

Imported cheats keep [Section] labels (individually toggleable).

Added Hide Settings Summary + Control Inputs toggles (the bottom OSD stuff).

The OSD as a whole is now off by default, can be toggled back on for benchmarking etc.

Fixed the back button being weird. 

Happy July 4th!

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V32
releases.body:
Added Per-cheat PNACH toggle. Enable/disable individual cheats from the in-app Patches manager, choices now persist across resets instead of applying whole files. (Hardcore mode still disables all cheats.) You can also click the Edit button once cheats are in to toggle them ON/OFF. 

Added ELF / homebrew in the main game library so .elf files show up with cover art (if they have one, otherwise a missing cover art but you can add one yourself in the Per-game info tab by long pressing on a game cover and scrolling to the info tab).

Added Save State & Exit and Reset Game hotkeys, "Save State & Exit" writes an autosave and closes (returns to your frontend when launched from one).

Auto-load last state on boot, optional toggle in the load-state manager.

Exit App button in the library "More" menu, with a confirmation screen. 

You can now hold the Android back button in-game to exit the app.

ELF per-game settings should now persist across a reset (previously fell back to global on boot).

DEV9 HDD custom path now opens an existing image in place instead of copying and creating a new one if one already exists. 

Adreno framebuffer-fetch now on by default on Turnip/Mesa, the fast tiler-native blend path, big help on transparency-heavy scenes. Proprietary Adreno stays opt-in.

Mali framebuffer-fetch detection fix, drivers exposing it under the ARM name were being kicked to the slow path, now get the fast one. (Let me know if this helps Mali folks). 

Added Per-Game Memory Cards option in the Card Manager, boot each game with its own Slot 1 Card, Restart the game to apply.

releases.name: ARMSX2 iOS 2.4.0.
releases.body:
# ARMSX2 iOS 2.4.0

ARMSX2 iOS 2.4.0 is a big update focused on making the app feel better while you are actually playing.

The pause menu has been rebuilt, Per-Game Settings has been redesigned, more options can now be tuned per game, and a lot of rough edges around the library, audio, RetroAchievements, memory cards, icons, and compatibility have been cleaned up.

This update also brings over a large amount of platform and core work that helps improve performance, stability, and game compatibility across the app.

A big thank you to **@isztldav** and **@jpolo1224**  for the amount of platform work that helped make many of these features possible. A lot of this update builds on work that started across macOS and Android before being adapted and polished for iOS.

Thank you to **Bagas** for the new app icons.

## New pause menu

The old in-game menu has been replaced with a new full-screen pause overlay.

It opens instantly, pauses gameplay while it is open, and uses a cleaner graphite layout with grouped actions. Resume is now pinned clearly in the footer, so getting back into the game feels more deliberate.

The new pause menu is split into clearer sections for quick actions, game-specific options, tools, reset, and exit. It also shows the active controller skin and layout, so it is easier to see what setup you are using without digging through settings.

The paused-game background now uses a stable dim instead of a blur effect. This keeps the menu readable and avoids the washed-out look that could happen over a paused game frame.

The pause menu also behaves better when returning from the background. If a game was running and you switch back to the app, the pause menu opens first instead of dropping you straight back into gameplay.

The Q Menu / pause button has also been cleaned up. It should stay visible, restore correctly if hidden, and no longer leave controller-only users stuck without a way back into the menu.

## Rebuilt Per-Game Settings

Per-Game Settings has had a full redesign.

The old tab layout has been replaced with a cleaner category list:

* General
* Graphics
* Audio
* CPU & Speedhacks
* Virtual Pad
* Fixes & Compatibility
* Cheats & Patches

The panel now matches the new pause menu style, with a darker graphite look, a proper Save / Cancel footer, and protection against losing changes by accident.

If you try to close the panel with unsaved changes, the app now asks before discarding them. If there are no changes, Cancel simply becomes Close.

Opening Per-Game Settings should also feel faster now, since overrides are loaded in a cleaner pass instead of through lots of small reads.

There are many more per-game options now, including:

* FXAA
* Shade Boost
* brightness, contrast, saturation, and gamma
* TV / CRT shader
* CAS sharpening and sharpness
* max anisotropy
* screen offsets
* overscan
* anti-blur
* disable interlace offset
* integer scaling
* skip duplicate frames
* dithering
* hardware download mode
* CPU / GPU CLUT
* VSync queue size
* sync to host refresh
* texture replacement options
* fast-forward volume
* audio buffer size and latency
* IOP, VU0, and VU1 recompilers
* EE Cycle Skip
* widescreen patches
* no-interlacing patches
* per-game renderer selection
* Accurate Alpha Test
* Texture Inside RT
* manual game fixes such as OPH Flag, Skip MPEG, Instant DMA, VU Sync, GIF FIFO, Full VU0 Sync, and more

Visual settings can now apply while a game is running. Slider changes are handled more carefully too, so dragging a slider should not stall gameplay the way it could before.

Per-game Fast Boot has also been fixed so it applies correctly at boot without leaking the resolved value back into global settings.

## Better landscape support

Landscape layouts have been cleaned up across iPhone and iPad.

The pause menu and Per-Game Settings now use floating rounded panels instead of awkward edge-to-edge layouts. Safe areas around the notch, Dynamic Island, and virtual pad are handled better.

Large iPhones like Pro Max and Plus models now use the correct phone landscape layout instead of accidentally being treated like iPads.

iPad keeps the larger layout, including a two-column pause menu and a rail/detail Per-Game Settings view in landscape.

## Library improvements

The game library now supports animated backgrounds.

GIF, APNG, and animated WebP files can be used behind the game grid. Animations are decoded more carefully, capped for safety, pause in the background, and respect Reduce Motion by falling back to a static frame.

The library also now has a persisted metadata cache. Game title, serial, CRC, and region metadata can be reused across launches, so unchanged games do not need to be fully scanned every time.

Game cards have also been polished. They line up better, handle longer titles more cleanly, support Dynamic Type better, and show region flags.

## Alternate app icons

2.4.0 adds alternate app icons under Appearance.

The included icons are:

* Default
* Synthwave
* Christmas
* Dark
* Frosted
* Gray
* Light
* Mystic Purple
* Purple
* Pride Dark
* Pride Light

LiveContainer installs also get an export fallback. If the container cannot change the Home Screen icon directly, you can export the icon and use it as a shortcut or container icon instead.

## Audio changes

There is a new Left / Right Channel Swap option.

This is useful for flipped-speaker setups or reverse-landscape setups where the stereo channels feel backwards. It applies live without needing a restart.

The emulator volume slider now goes up to 150%. The default is unchanged, but values above 100% can amplify the output. As usual with amplification, it may distort depending on the game and device volume.

There is also an on-screen notifications toggle for transient in-game messages.

A mute-after-menu issue has been fixed as well. Audio should no longer stay muted after opening the menu during certain save-state or settings flows.

2.4.0 also includes an optional SPU2 NEON reverb path on iOS. This is an advanced INI-only option for now. There is no iOS UI toggle for it yet.

## RetroAchievements

RetroAchievements now has sounds for unlocks, messages, and leaderboard submits.

Hardcore Mode is safer to disable now. The app asks for confirmation before turning it off, so a stray tap should not accidentally drop you out of Hardcore.

The old “softcore” wording has also been updated to “Casual.”

## Memory-card improvements

Memory cards can now be deleted from settings, with confirmation.

If a deleted card was assigned to slot 1 or slot 2, that slot is cleared automatically. Slots also self-heal if a card was removed outside the app, such as through the Files app.

Cards currently loaded in slot 1 or 2 are now marked in the list, making it easier to see what is active.

## Performance and compatibility

This update includes a large batch of arm64 recompiler work.

Several Emotion Engine paths that previously had to fall back to slower handling now have native arm64 coverage, including MADD-related paths, HI/LO handling, BC0/CPCOND0 behavior, DMA wait-loop handling, EI/ERET, COP0 Count/PERF handling, DI, traps, SYNC/CACHE, and SkipMPEGHack.

In practice, this should help performance and compatibility in some titles, especially games that are heavy on DMA, timing loops, or paths that previously hit fallback behavior.

The MTGS spin-wait window has also been reduced on Apple silicon, which should reduce wasted CPU time during frame handoff and help thermals and battery behavior.

## Graphics and stability fixes

A number of shared-core fixes are included in this release.

Texture-replacement packs with an off-by-one mip count should no longer crash.

Shader and pipeline cache handling has been made safer, which should reduce the chance of broken cache data causing rendering issues after a crash or interrupted write.

Several texture-cache and framebuffer handling fixes have been brought in, which should help with corruption, stretched frames, or incorrect rendering in affected games.

The on-screen display scale is now capped so overlays stay readable on high-DPI screens.

DEV9 now has a DNS fallback on iOS, which helps hostname resolution for online and HDD features.

## Game compatibility

The GameDB has been synced with upstream and includes targeted compatibility updates.

Notable fixes and tuning include:

* Jackie Chan Adventures clamp fixes
* Ace Combat 5 / Zero partial texture preloading
* Gran Turismo tuning
* Burnout 3 memory-card cross-save filters
* standalone ELF / homebrew per-game settings lookup fixes

This should be understood as an upstream GameDB sync plus targeted fixes, not as thousands of newly added games.

## Other fixes and cleanup

iOS no longer shows some irrelevant startup warnings, such as warnings about controller bindings that do not make sense with the virtual pad, or renderer warnings that do not apply to the iOS setup.

The performance overlay now shows extra rendering status when relevant, and in-game navigation behaves better after surface resize or orientation changes.

There was also a cleanup pass through stale code paths, unused bridge methods, unused virtual-pad helpers, old localization strings, and other internal leftovers.

## Final note

A huge thank you to the testing team.

This update had a lot of moving parts, and the amount of testing, retesting, feedback, and bug reports behind it made a real difference. A lot of the polish in 2.4.0 only happened because people spent real time using rough builds and pointing out what still needed work.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V31
releases.body:
USB keyboard support for games that need one (EverQuest Online Adventures, Konami keyboard titles, etc.). Toggle can be found in the Network tab.

DEV9 hard-drive (HDD) support, enable the internal HDD for games/homebrew that require it; the image is created safely on internal storage instead of filling your SD card. (On SD setups, the HDD 8gb file will still default to internal, I tried having it on SD and it causes serious issues from how much slower SD cards are. 

Added Left/Right channel swap, new toggle that fixes reversed stereo on flipped-speaker / reverse-landscape handhelds in the Audio tab. 

Added RetroAchievements sounds, unlock and notification chimes now play. 

Added Animated backgrounds, you can now set an animated GIF / WebP / APNG as your library wallpaper. 

New settings-gear icon and a redesigned "Recently Played" shelf (art by @Vivimagic). 

Country flag moved into the cover reflection, tidier layout, title text sits closer to the art. Thank you to @Trixarian for this. 

Bigger collapsible-section arrows + taller tap targets, no more mis-tapping the wrong section. 

Even cover heights when game titles are turned on.

ELF / homebrew per-game settings now apply, previously only the global profile took effect. 

Added more descriptions for every Performance-tab toggle, GameDB Fixes and Advanced Speedhacks are now fully documented. 

Added a "On-screen notifications" toggle, you can now hide shader-compilation and status pop-ups in the Overlay tab.

Fast-Forward pop-ups no longer stack up (and can be turned off entirely). Also in the Overlay tab. 

Added more GPU pipeline-statistics OSD, optional vertex/pixel shader-invocation line on Vulkan. Can be toggled In the Overlay tab.

A fix for Jackie Chan Adventures where Jackie would fall through the floor into an endless black void. (Full EE/FPU + Extra VU clamping now baked in). Also increased the size of the in-game gear icon, tap the top middle of the screen to reveal it. Enjoy!

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V30
releases.body:
Performance improvements to the recompiler: 

The EE recompiler now has direct block chaining. Patchable direct-branch tails replace the LUT-indirect dispatch between blocks, cutting per-block dispatch overhead. Should help weaker CPUs.

The EE recompiler now has SIGSEGV-backpatch fastmem, extended. Fast inline memory access now covers FP and vector loads/stores (LWC1/SWC1, LQ/SQ), not just integer.

The EE recompiler now has fast-tier register cache coverage across MULT/DIV/MTHI/MTLO (fewer full register spills).

Added PGO (Profile-Guided Optimization). Shipping cores are now built with a profile trained on GoW2 / Jackie Chan / VP2 / LEGO Batman / GT4. Combined, these games are all EE, VU, or GS heavy, so it should hopefully help a bit. You can read more about PGO [here:](https://www.google.com/url?sa=t&source=web&rct=j&opi=89978449&url=https://developer.android.com/games/agde/pgo-overview&ved=2ahUKEwjh4rWF3rGVAxWak4kEHaCEKCkQFnoECBcQAQ&usg=AOvVaw3lx-OOqUE93Xjn_kr81O9y)

Please let me know if performance has improved for you! 

Atomic VK shader-cache fix, the pipeline cache now writes to a .tmp file and atomically renames over the real one. A swipe-kill or crash mid-write can no longer leave a half-written cache that the Adreno driver accepts and renders garbage from. The previous valid cache survives an interrupted write instead of being clobbered. (The shader index/blob cache was already crash-safe, it bounds-checks entries and appends blob-before-index, so no change was needed there.)

GameDB: ported PCSX2 #14647, Gran Turismo tuning (GT2000/GT3/GT4 demo + retail blending / half-pixel / autoflush, GT4 Online-Test VU clamp) plus a new Lupo Cup 2 (PAPX-90509) entry and Burnout 3 memcard filters. 

Made some fixes to DEV9 Ethernet on Android. Added a public-DNS fallback (Android had no /etc/resolv.conf) and dropped the gateway gate that blocked adapter init (/proc/net/route is SELinux-restricted). Online adapter + DNS should now work correctly. (Let me know if it still does not) 

DEV9 DNS Host Mappings (Settings ---> Network). Map hostnames ---> IPs via the internal DNS resolver, should help some specific private/fan-server play (like RE Outbreak / obsrv). 

Touch gliding across control boundaries. Replaced the three regional touch layers with one unified layer, so you can slide between face buttons and triggers (X--->L1) and hold multi-touch combos (L3+R3).

Memory-card self-heal. Deleting a card with an external file manager no longer leaves a stale slot that mislabels your other card.

RetroAchievements: "Softcore" has been renamed to "Casual" (per RA's own rename, Hardcore unchanged).

Improved Readability. Lightened the dark-blue UI text (0xFF0033CC ---> 0xFF4C8DFF) that was unreadable for some on the black background.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V29
releases.body:
Added Per-game controller mapping, button binds, stick modes, and custom stick directions can now be set per game. While a game is loaded, use the Global / This Game toggle at the top of the menu: "This Game" saves an override just for that title, everything falls back to your global layout otherwise. 

Added Touch Gliding (NetherSX2 style), slide your finger from one on-screen button onto another and the first one stays held, so you can hold several buttons with one finger. Toggle it in the touch layout editor. Off by default.

Added Touch haptics, a short vibration when you press an on-screen button, like PPSSPP / Azahar. Toggle it off under Pad ---> On-Screen Controls ---> Touch Haptics. Separate from physical controller rumble. On by default.

Added Emulation Screen Orientation options, you can now lock the app to Landscape, Portrait, Auto-Rotate, or follow your device setting. In Render tab ---> Display & Resolution.

Folder-type memory cards now format and save on a custom data folder. They previously failed with "Format failed!" when your data folder lived on shared storage, folder cards make directory writes that Android's storage layer was blocking so they now use a path that works.

Sliding onto L1 / R1 with multi-touch now registers the press.

Deleting a memory card now recreates a fresh empty card in that slot instead of leaving it empty.

The settings menu now remembers the tab you were on and keeps sections expanded/collapsed the way you left them, instead of snapping back to the first tab every time you reopen it.

Custom analog-stick mapping is now more reliable, pick a target (any PS2 button including the D-pad, a hotkey, or Analog) directly from a list, instead of having to press a physical button. You can now map a stick direction to a button you've unbound, and every direction has a visible Clear button.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V28
releases.body:
Added on-screen Save State / Load State buttons (opt-in through the Touch Layout editor), tap the save/load button to open the slot picker and choose which slot to save/load from.

Tap-to-hold (latch) for on-screen face/shoulder buttons, tap once to hold (R1 to crouch), tap again to release, enable per-button in the touch layout editor.

Audio volume slider has been increased up to 150% for quiet games (above 100% can clip).

Multi-touch is now on by default, fixed not being able to move the analog stick and press buttons (or multiple buttons) at the same time. Toggle to turn it off still in the Touch Layout editor.

Pressure-modifier button hidden by default, and the Performance / Renderer / Pad settings tabs now start collapsed for a cleaner layout.

Settings Reset is now per-tab: Reset Tab clears only the current tab, Reset All / Reset Game does the full reset.

Macro-config dialog now uses the dark theme.

Touch stick + button now register at the same time (like move and jump together).

Clearing a D-pad or trigger (L2/R2) binding now takes effect in-game (previously only face buttons honored an unbind).

Single-button hotkeys now bind reliably on controllers that report two key codes per press.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V27
releases.body:
Performance updates to the EE recompiler (helps every device, especially weaker CPUs). 

1. DMA-wait spin elimination (BC0/CPCOND0): the EE now natively compiles the COP0 branch games use to busy-wait on DMA, and idle-skips the spin entirely. Was the single biggest EE cost in DMA-heavy games. (7M interpreter fallbacks before, now 0).

2. Multiply-accumulate native codegen (MADD family): MADD/MADDU/MADD1/MADDU1 + the pipeline-1 HI/LO moves (MFHI1/MTHI1/MFLO1/MTLO1) now compile natively instead of single-stepping. (2M interpreter fallbacks before, now 0). 

3. Interrupt-gating native codegen (EI/ERET): enable-interrupts and exception-return now compile as block terminators instead of single-stepping, notably faster boot/menu/loading across all games.

Performance updates for GS (OpenGL, all GPUs including Mali). 

OpenGL shader-cache fix: stopped per-frame shader recompilation (a selector-equality bug). Large GS-time reduction on the OpenGL-HW renderer, reduced GS usage from 90% to low 20% in Jackie Chan Adventures.

Arabic / RTL on-screen controls no longer mirrored. In a right-to-left system language the touch controls were flipped left-right, now forced to absolute layout.

You can now unbind a pad button, the "Clear" button on each Pad binding leaves a button blank, freeing that physical button to be assigned as a hotkey.

Added an on-screen fast-forward button, an optional fast forward touch button that toggles Turbo. This button is opt-in enable, you can enable and position it in the touch-layout editor (it's off by default, so existing layouts are unchanged).

Added Touch and physical controller Macro/combo buttons (M1–M4), one tap fires multiple buttons together (R1+R2+R3 for example), configure in Pad settings.

The Pad, Performance, and Renderer settings tabs now group their options under expand/collapse headers (like the Fixes tab), so they're much easier to scan instead of one long wall of settings.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V26
releases.body:
Valkyrie Profile 2: Silmeria now boots (was a long-standing black screen). Game-specific fixes from the GameDB are now on by default (matching desktop PCSX2), so the VU fix VP2 needs is applied. This also un-breaks any game that relies on that GameDB fix.

Skip MPEG now works, the FMV-skip hack was missing from the Android (ARM64) recompiler, now ported over.

For God of War II, walls no longer go transparent on the OpenGL renderer (fixed a GLES depth-precision bug, also sharpens depth for all games on OpenGL).

VP2 intro cutscene renders correctly when upscaling, GOW2, and LEGO Batman upscales cleanly both out of the box, no need to head to the fixes tab for these.

Audio now self-heals if the audio device is busy at launch (no more "no sound until I restart my device" if your audio device bugs out).

Added Stick Invert X / Invert Y / Swap X-Y (for pads with mirrored/rotated sticks).

Added Stick Anti-Deadzone, helps fix games that ignore the stick until ~45% then jump (Cold Fear, Area 51).

Bind stick directions to hotkeys or other pad mappings (Quick Save/Load, etc.).

Smoother analog triggers on non-Xbox pads (less jitter, full range).

Rumble / Vibration on-off toggle.

Added correct region flags for India / China / Korea / Hong Kong releases (reads the GameDB now, not just the serial).

Add a game to your home screen as a launcher shortcut, press and hold a game (or on controller Y/triangle), navigate to the Info section of settings (last tab in per-game settings) ---> Add to Home Screen button). (Let me know if this is not working). 

Added collapsible sections in the Fixes tab to reduce clutter.

Added the Gabominated patch collection as a 2nd source in the in-app import patches browser.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V25
releases.body:
Added faster anisotropic filtering from PCSX2, optimized AF shader (early isotropic fallback, cheaper math). Port of PCSX2 [https://github.com/PCSX2/pcsx2/pull/14465]
(https://github.com/PCSX2/pcsx2/pull/14465).

Added ROV (Rasterizer Ordered View) toggle for HW accurate blending in renderer settings, port of PCSX2 https://github.com/PCSX2/pcsx2/pull/14555. Exposed in Renderer for benchmarking mainly, it's Vulkan-only and applies live. Honestly it isn't as performant on mobile as it is on PC from my testing, and some extensions ROV needs are not present on Turnip. I didn't see gains on mobile, added for parity and benchmarking purposes.

Added "Accurate blending fast path (experimental)" in renderer settings, the mobile-native take on accurate blending via the Vulkan tile-memory framebuffer-fetch path, usually faster than ROV on a tiler. Vulkan only, restart to apply (Mali already uses it by default, so the toggle is for Adreno users only). Some drivers show artifacts with this setting, so be sure to test and benchmark your games.

Added Accurate Alpha Test toggle in renderer settings, applies live.

Added HW AA1 edge anti-aliasing toggle in renderer settings (applies on game restart).

GameDB now flags games that recommend Accurate Alpha Test / AA1 on-screen.

Higher internal resolution settings, upscaling now goes up to 8x (was 5x). Time to fry your GPU!

Rumble fallback to the device's built-in vibration motor for controllers with no rumble actuator (e.g. Odin 3), issue https://github.com/ARMSX2/ARMSX2/issues/241.

Controller skin import now supports the newer per-side analog filenames (analog_stick_left/right), older packs still work.

Renamed "Game Fixes" to "GameDB Fixes" for clarity.

Ported an upstream PCSX2 texture-cache Move correctness fix (https://github.com/ARMSX2/ARMSX2/commit/a6b1040caf9dd3c825a1894a36534212b59894f3).

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V24
releases.body:
Added Custom controller skins, import your own on-screen button art from a folder or .zip (iOS skins done by Bagas work directly), and switch between them in a new Skins tab. Two built-in NetherSX2 skins have been added (NetherSX2 and NetherSX2 Old button skins, thanks @Trixarian).

Added Floating on-screen joystick in the Touch Layout editor, an optional mode where the analog stick re-centers under your thumb wherever you press down, instead of a fixed spot.

Added a Stick outer / anti-dead zone slider so short-throw sticks (Odin and similar handhelds) hit full tilt before the physical edge.

You can now edit the touch layout from the main menu, reach the touch layout editor from _Settings ---> Pad ---> Edit On-Screen Touch Layout_ without needing a game running. 

You can now Show/hide individual on-screen buttons in the Touch Layout Editor, toggle any button in or out per layout (hidden ones are greyed + crossed out while editing, and will not appear in game). Unhide them at any time. 

Added a confirmation popup before disabling Hardcore, turning Hardcore off now asks first, so you don't disable it by accident.

Added Mr Purple's driver repo (purple-turnip) as a second in-app driver download source alongside K11MCH1.

For Ace Combat 5 / Ace Combat Zero an automatic GameDB fix (partial texture preloading) to cut FMV/hash-cache stutter has been added.

releases.name: ARMSX2 macOS 2.5.2
releases.body:
- implemented MetalFX

Enjoy upscaled games!

releases.name: ARMSX2 macOS 2.5.1
releases.body:
This is a minor update but it's recommended to all users.

- Fixed a metal render issue that was leading to a crash in games like `Bloody Roar 3`

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/ARMSX2/ARMSX2/compare/MacOSv2.5.0...MacOSv2.5.1

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V23
releases.body:
Added support for a second controller, so you can now play local co-op 2 player games with a friend, as well as per-player mapping in the Pad section of settings. 

First controller to press in-game = Player 1, the next distinct controller = Player 2 (auto-assigned, re-paired each game launch). Port-aware native pad input (setPadButtonForPort) routes each controller to its own PS2 port, per-port D-pad state so the two pads can't release each other's directions.

Per-player button mapping: button binds, stick modes, and custom stick directions are now per-player. Pad settings has a Player 1 / Player 2 selector, "Reset" is also per-player. Player 1 keeps your exact existing binds (P2 uses defaults until set).
Player 2 is enabled at game boot when a second controller is connected beforehand, second controller has rumble support as well (except DualSense, only works wired on a pc). 

Added NTSC / PAL per-region frame rate caps (NetherSX2-style, thanks @Trixarian for the help) as an alternative to the Max Display FPS cap setting. Separate Frame Rate (Hz) sliders for NTSC and PAL games. 

Added a List view as an alternative to the shelf view.

Added manual Rows control with cover-size adjustment (toggled from the top right buttons ---> More ---> Rows Auto (can be toggled to 2, 3, 4, or 5, and back to auto, depending on how zoomed out you'd like the shelves and covers to be. 

Expanded the setup wizard, after clicking the Let's Go button on initial setup, you will now see a new screen with optional recommended settings: aspect ratio, internal resolution, anti-blur, widescreen, deinterlacing, and an Optimal/Fast preset.
Renderer choice (OpenGL / Vulkan) + custom GPU driver browser in setup.

New Stick Deadzone, Sensitivity, and Acceleration sliders in the Pad section of settings. 

Added UI scaling per screen in the Overlay section of settings. Independent UI Size (borders) and UI Font Size sliders to fit 21:9 / 16:10 / 4:3 / handheld screens, scales the library + menus only. 

Added a "Reset global to defaults" (global) and "Reset this game to global" (per-game), with a confirmation tap. 

ARM64 recompiler performance improvements and game fixes (ported from @isztldav macOS branch)

Faster, more faithful EE recompilation, MIPS trap opcodes now use native block-conditional codegen, and SYNC/CACHE are handled inline instead of breaking blocks apart.

Fixed an after-BIOS hang by porting the proper interrupt-disable delay (games like Jak X now boot past it).

Squashed an MMI lane-permute codegen bug that could abort compilation outright.
Synced with upstream PCSX2:

Graphics fixes across HW/DX11/DX12/GL, AA1 rendering, DATE setup, depth feedback, and ROV reporting.

GameDB updates, and assorted stability fixes.

Import File for memory cards no longer renames or forces Slot 1 on a second import (unique name, no forced slot).

A deleted card no longer reappears at BIOS boot (case-insensitive slot match).

Added a Per-card Delete button and confirmation, added up/down/left/right controller navigation for memory card settings. 

Speed Limit % max raised to 1000, will only push as fast as your device can go, same as the Frame Limit off button. 

The cap/FPS stats line ("Display: 25 (cap 30)") is now hidden when the OSD is off for the Max FPS Display cap setting.

HostFS functionality has been added back, you can find the toggle to enable it in the Patches section of settings. (Let me know if you still have any issues). 

Added a per-player Test rumble button (Pad tab) that reports whether Android can drive that controller's rumble.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V22
releases.body:
Added support for custom game covers, set your own artwork for any game. Long-press a game ---> Info Section (Last tab in settings) ---> Set Custom Cover, or drop images into the covers/custom folder named after the game. Make sure to remove the previous custom cover first before changing it to another. 

Added a new hotkey for Fast Forward (Toggle) for controllers, press once to lock fast-forward on, press again to turn it off, an alternative to having to hold the button through long JRPG grinds. The original Fast Forward (Hold) hotkey is also still an option. 

Per-game Renderer settings no longer bleed through to other games. Your choices made in global settings will carry over automatically.

Added SPU2 SIMD audio (experimental) setting in the Audio section of settings, an optional NEON fast path for sound processing that can free up CPU on lower-end devices. Off by default; toggle it under Audio settings. Credit to @Phantomnameless for bringing the feature up to me.

Redesigned the library toolbar: the top-right buttons are now clean icons with labels underneath (Scan, BIOS, Memory Cards, Cover Art, plus a ⋮ More menu for the rest), replacing the cramped text buttons.

For Memory Cards, the manager now clearly shows which card is loaded in each slot (highlight + a check mark).

Cleaner pause menu: the exit (x) button is now red, and the green play button no longer shows while you're already in-game. Long-press a game in the game library and press the play button there, an alternative to launching games from the cover (touch only).

releases.name: ARMSX2 macOS 2.5.0
releases.body:
Another step forward for native ARM64 PlayStation 2 emulation on Apple Silicon! This build folds in the latest upstream PCSX2 improvements and continues sharpening the macOS ARM64 recompiler.

What's new on the ARM64 side:
- Faster, more faithful EE recompilation — MIPS trap opcodes now use native block-conditional codegen, and SYNC/CACHE are handled inline instead of breaking blocks apart.
- Fixed an after-BIOS hang by porting the proper interrupt-disable delay (games like Jak X now boot past it).
- Squashed an MMI lane-permute codegen bug that could abort compilation outright.

Synced with upstream PCSX2:
- Graphics fixes across HW/DX11/DX12/GL — AA1 rendering, DATE setup, depth feedback, and ROV reporting.
- A nicer UI — memory card sorting, smoother game-list backgrounds, and a Big Picture controller-navigation fix.
- GameDB updates, debugger/recompiler tooling improvements, and assorted stability fixes.

Grab the build, fire up your favorite game, and enjoy — and as always, if something misbehaves, bug reports and logs are hugely appreciated. 🎮

Happy gaming! ✨

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/ARMSX2/ARMSX2/compare/MacOSv2.4.0...MacOSv2.5.0

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V20
releases.body:
Custom data folder is back for Github Builds (APKs). With All-Files Access you can now keep your memory cards, save states, and configs in any folder you choose, for data that survives an uninstall and you don't need to manually back up, and your games load correctly from it. Pick it in setup: App Data Folder ---> Custom Folder.
Google Play builds are unchanged, they stay on scoped storage (no Custom Folder), for policy compliance.

The pause overlay/settings menu will now automatically open if the app goes out of focus, (like your device falling asleep, or when changing to another app without closing ARMSX2), so resuming is now simpler. 

The gear icon now remains available when setting on-screen controls to never in Pad section of settings. Tap the top middle of the screen to reveal it as normal. 

CRC now shows in the Game Properties Info tab and can be copied like the rest.

Rumble small-motor fix: light rumble no longer comes through at full strength on single-motor controllers.

You can now map the left and right stick to D-Pad or Face buttons on controller.

Time Played / Last Played is now tracked per game, shown in the Info Tab (last section of settings). 

You can now map the Analog button on physical controllers in the Pad section of settings. Fixes games that need it to enable the sticks (like Driving Emotion Type-S)

Custom Speed Limit % and Display FPS Cap sliders (reverted frame rate control name), you can now pick any value instead of fixed presets, per game, for fine-tuning smoothness. Instead of presets like 60, 30, 20, 15, you can now choose any value from 1-60. For emulation speed, instead of presets like 100, 150, 200, you can now choose any value from 10% to 200%. Turning off the Frame Limit button in the Play section of settings will bypass this slider, and push the emulator as fast as your device can handle. 

Mali GPUs: set Renderer ---> GPU Profile ---> Adreno for more speed (V16-level) on heavy games like GT4, at the cost of possible blending glitches, keep Auto or Mali for correct graphics but slower speeds in some games. Testers reported DBZ and Black were fine with the Mali profile, but games like GT4 were slower. I do not have a Mali device to test, so it is difficult and time consuming for me to try and improve Mali without owning a Mali device, hopefully Dib can help in the future. Or if someone gifts me a Mali device ;)

That's all for a bit, enjoy!

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V19
releases.body:
For per-game touch layouts you can now set a virtual controller layout per game instead of one global layout. Profiles are saved to an inputprofiles folder in your app folder.

Rumble now works on connected Bluetooth and USB controllers, (please let me know if this issue is still persisting for you). 

Added button in the game library info button menu to either copy the ARMSX2 app data folder location, or use a downloaded file manager to access it directly. 

Long-press a game in the library and scroll to the last section "Info" to see its Title, Serial, CRC, Region, compatibility and path. Tap any field to copy it.

Renamed Max FPS Cap to Frame Rate Control.

DDS texture packs no longer crash (such as Resident Evil 4). 

No more "freezing" after the menu: the game would stayed paused after closing the menu in some cases (for example after saving a controller layout), and you had to re-open the menu and hit Resume. The game now resumes whenever you leave the menu.

Global vs per-game settings no longer bleed into each other, switching the Global/Game scope and saving could write to the wrong one. Each tier is kept separate now.

Gamepad face buttons work immediately at game load, no more pressing X/Triangle a few times to "wake up" the controller.

Audio no longer goes silent when toggling heavy game fixes, sound keeps playing through the change.

Fixed storage selection during initial setup. Choosing Internal or SD Card now works and gives clear feedback, picking SD Card on a device with no memory card tells you instead of silently doing nothing. Changing the storage location after setup now prompts a restart so it actually takes effect.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V18
releases.body:
New Max FPS Cap in Settings ---> Performance ---> Off / 60 / 30 / 20 / 15. Caps the on-screen frame rate to save battery and heat without slowing the game; emulation keeps running at full speed and only displayed frames are dropped. It's fully independent of the Speed Limit %, so you can run, say, 150% speed and a 30 fps display cap together. When a cap is active the OSD shows Display: N (cap N) so you can see the real presented rate.

The OSD now shows Display: N (cap N) when a cap is active, so you can see the real presented frame rate (the FPS/VPS lines still show the internal/vsync rate).

Added individual Hardware info (CPU/GPU model) and Emulator version toggles in Settings ---> Overlay, previously these could only be hidden via the master OSD switch in the Play section of settings. 

Per-game settings are now written out as individual .ini files (one per game). They're stored in the gamesettings folder inside the app's data directory:
/storage/emulated/0/Android/data/com.armsx2/files/gamesettings/

Moved the settings gear from the top-right corner to the top-center, so it's easier to reach.

The game library no longer scrolls sideways, covers wrap onto more shelves to fill the screen instead.

Added a green Resume button next to the X (exit) in the in-game menu, so getting back into the game is obvious rather than it just being in the Play section of settings.

The library now re-scans your games automatically on boot, so anything you've added shows up without a manual refresh.

Settings toggle boxes in the performance section of settings are now 35% smaller. 

Play section of settings bubbles should no longer balloon on tall displays (so smaller screens will have all settings).

Taken from @isztldav work on Mac, PCSX2 2.7 upstream commits:

Improved line anti-aliasing (AA1): sharper, less-blurry thin lines when upscaling.
Texture-cache accuracy: more precise "exact target" matching (OverlapsValid), reducing some texture-cache misdetections.

Updated controller database (adds 8BitDo Ultimate 2 mappings).

Bumped the shader cache version.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V17
releases.body:
Fixed graphics corruption on Mali GPUs under Vulkan. Devices with ARM Mali graphics showed white bands and blown-out visuals on transparency, bloom, and reflection effects unless you forced Blending Accuracy to Maximum. The renderer now handles Mali's hardware blending correctly at default settings, so those games render properly without needing blending at max. Other GPUs are unaffected.

Added pressure-sensitive button support (DualShock 2 analog presses). Games like Metal Gear Solid 2/3 and the 3D Grand Theft Auto titles rely on soft/partial button presses that previously had no way to be performed. Hold the new Pressure Modifier (either the on-screen "P½" button (under the D-pad) or a button you bind under Settings ---> Hotkeys ---> "Pressure Modifier (hold)" for physical controllers, then press a face, shoulder, or D-pad button for a 50% press. (Please let me know if it doesn't take effect in a game that uses pressure.)

Added multi-touch for the rest of the on-screen controls. You can now press multiple face buttons and triggers at the same time (L2 + R2, or holding a trigger while pressing a face button). Enable it with the "Multi-Touch" toggle in the overlay edit toolbar, same as before.

Due to restrictions by the GooglePlay store, external app folders were borked, replacing that feature is a simple Internal / SD Card choice. On the Play release, picking a custom folder left you unable to proceed past setup, and on sideloaded builds games would crash afterward. Setup now lets you choose Internal storage (your main device) and the default private app folder, or SD Card for memory cards, this was the best option I could come up with to remain complaint with Google. If you are ever fresh installing, be sure to back up your data first. (Arbitrary folders elsewhere on storage aren't possible without "All files access," which was removed for Google Play compliance.)

Mapping the D-pad in Pad settings now works on handhelds. On devices that report the D-pad as an analog like the AYN Odin 3, Retroid handhelds, etc., trying to bind it would just navigate the menu instead of capturing the input. The D-pad now binds correctly, and normal navigation resumes afterward.

Fixed audio cutting out after using the in-game menu. Sound could go silent after loading a save state or applying fixes, it now keeps playing. (Please let me know if you still have this issue on your device.)

Game library scrolling is smoother overall.

Fixed the app launcher icon. The logo appeared oddly sized, it now sits correctly within the adaptive icon shape.

releases.name: ARMSX2 iOS 2.3.1
releases.body:
From @J1coding 
**Cheats & Patches manager:** Each game now has its own manager for `.pnach` cheats and patches, available from the game long-press menu or the in-game quick menu. You can view named entries, enable/disable them per game, remove individual entries, or delete full patch/cheat files without manually editing text files.

**Patch and cheat downloads:** The manager can download matching PCSX2 patches or community cheats using the game’s serial/CRC, then merge them into that game’s files. Downloads start disabled so you choose what to activate, and trusted custom source URLs can be configured.

**Safer `.pnach` handling:** Imports now merge instead of blindly replacing content. Matching named blocks are updated, unrelated entries are preserved, backups are made before managed writes, and URL comments like `http://` / `https://` are no longer broken by `//` comment parsing.

**Compatibility Lab retired:** The old Compatibility Lab has been removed from the iOS menus and help. This is separate from the new Cheats & Patches manager; it was not replaced by it.

**Virtual pad upgrades:** The D-pad can now support one-finger diagonals and smoother rolling/quarter-circle inputs. Face-button combo zones let overlapping button areas trigger two-button combos while normal multi-touch still works. Both features can be disabled in VPad settings.

**Touch reliability fixes:** Virtual buttons now release when their view disappears, helping prevent stuck inputs after orientation changes, menus, or screen transitions.

**VPad editor polish:** The per-game VPad editor has its skin picker back, and the maximum virtual-control scale was raised from 4× to 7× for larger custom buttons and touch areas.

**Latest PCSX2 core updates:** Synced upstream PCSX2 work includes renderer and texture-cache fixes, improved AA1 line rendering at higher resolutions including Metal, refreshed shader cache handling, and other low-level maintenance.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V16 Mali Hotfix
releases.body:
Fixes for more Mali devices on Vulkan, PLEASE FRESH INSTALL FOR MALI USERS!

I have already sent out the Google Play Store build, so Mali users use this hot fix for now, I will update the PlayStore again once the other update rolls out. Thanks!

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V16
releases.body:
From @jpolo1224 Added RetroAchievements Hardcore support on Android, including Hardcore status states, settings UI, login recovery/status handling, and enforcement around save-state loading and restricted features. Thank you to @LiquifiedSnow and the rest of the RA team for working with us!

Fixed Vulkan crashing on Mali GPUs. Devices with ARM Mali graphics (many MediaTek phones) would crash when loading a game with the Vulkan renderer. Vulkan now works on Mali. Other GPUs are unaffected. This should improve performance for Mali users!

EE Cycle Rate / Cycle Skip no longer revert each other. Adjusting one setting then the other previously reset the first, sliders now always commit against the current values.

Pause overlay Play tab fits all screens. The action grid (Resume, Save State, etc.) could overflow and clip the bottom row on shorter screens, it now scales to always show all three rows. (Please let me know if this issue still persists on your device).

Library help text no longer pushes the gear off-screen on small phones. Replaced the long left-side text with an info button that opens a dedicated, scrollable Library Help screen.

Mapping controller buttons in Pad settings works for every button. Binding B or Y no longer exits the menu. Normal navigation resumes after the bind.

Added Two-button hotkey combos.  In the Hotkeys tab, press one button for a single bind or two together for a combo (Select + R1 for Save State for example).

Added new settings from PCSX2 2.7:

• HW Accurate Alpha Test
• Draw Buffering
• Goemon TLB (game fix)
• VU Sync (game fix)
• SW Thread Tile Height
• Disable Vertex Shader Expand (restart to apply)
• Use Blit Swap Chain (restart to apply)
• Disable Shader Cache (restart to apply)

Fixed Limit 24-bit Depth hack (if it still is not working, please let me know! It may need a game restart to apply.)

Game library now scans subfolders and no longer skips certain .chd files.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V15
releases.body:
The emulator can now fully be navigated with a controller. Full controller navigation across the pause overlay, game library, settings, the memory card manager, and the RetroAchievements screens with both D-pad and analog sticks.

Pause overlay now works with both the D-pad and the analog stick (handhelds with or without a stick).

Press the (X) button on a highlighted game to open its per-game settings.

Rightmost covers now fully scroll into view when navigating a shelf with a controller.

Added various new Hotkeys settings:

New dedicated Hotkeys tab (moved out of the Pad tab).

New bindable hotkeys: Toggle Texture Dumping, Cycle Save Slot, Fast Forward (hold), Increase/Decrease Resolution, Open Achievements, Close Game.

Quick Save/Load State now use the currently selected slot (cycle it with Cycle Save Slot).

Texture Dumping: Added a runtime controller Toggle Texture Dumping hotkey to flip dumping on/off mid-game (for example, to stop dumping during FMVs).

Added a RetroAchievements button at the top of the pause overlay; tap it or press Y on a controller to open it.

Sign-in, the login form (username/password/cancel/sign-in), and scrolling the achievement list now work with a controller.

Settings tabs now use the full screen width, so sections and long option rows are no longer cut off.

Added a dedicated exit button for the pause overlay, tapping empty space no longer closes the overlay by accident.

Added useful relevant information in various parts of the emulator.

Brought over new settings for further PCSX2 2.7 parity:

Display fixes: Anti-Blur, Screen Offsets, Show Overscan, Disable Interlace Offset, Sync to Host Refresh, Disable Framebuffer Fetch, Override Texture Barriers.

Hardware/software fixes: Skip Draw Start/End, Spin GPU/CPU for Readbacks, Auto-Flush (SW), Mipmapping (SW), Software Rendering Threads.

Display/quality: Integer Scaling, Dithering, Vsync Queue Size.

Performance: VU0 Round Mode, VU1 Round Mode.

Create new memory cards: Folder type, and File cards in 8 / 16 / 32 / 64 MB.
The memory card manager now scrolls, so the creation form is fully reachable with and without a controller.

Brought in a PR from @misantronic

[RAOfflineProxy](https://github.com/misantronic/RAOfflineProxy) is a local proxy that sits between the emulator and retroachievements.org to cache RA data and enable offline achievement tracking. It needs emulators to expose a host override so it can point them at the loopback proxy.

This adds that to the refresh line — a port of https://github.com/ARMSX2/ARMSX2/pull/164 (merged to master), re-implemented
for the refresh architecture (native-lib.cpp JNI, com.armsx2, the existing
[Achievements] Host setting).

What it does

network_security_config: allow cleartext to 127.0.0.1/localhost (targetSdk 36 blocks it by default).
RetroAchievementsHostOverrideReceiver: broadcast to set/clear a loopback host; validated, persisted, and replayed on next launch if set while the app is closed.
native-lib JNI: drives the existing [Achievements] Host setting, forces hardcore off while active (restored on clear), and rebuilds the client.
Usage

adb shell am broadcast -a com.armsx2.action.SET_RETROACHIEVEMENTS_HOST_OVERRIDE \
  -e host "127.0.0.1:8080" -n com.armsx2/com.armsx2.RetroAchievementsHostOverrideReceiver
Clear with CLEAR_RETROACHIEVEMENTS_HOST_OVERRIDE. Tested on device.

Related work from @misantronic thank you for the PR!

https://github.com/ARMSX2/ARMSX2/pull/164 (ARMSX2 master)
https://github.com/hrydgard/ppsspp/pull/21760
https://github.com/dolphin-emu/dolphin/pull/14671
https://github.com/rafaelvcaetano/melonDS-android/pull/1633
https://github.com/libretro/RetroArch/pull/19093
https://github.com/flyinghead/flycast/pull/2371

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V14
releases.body:
Fixed the boot crash on certain handhelds (AYANEO Pocket S Mini, Retroid Pocket Mini, and other Adreno devices) where V9 and newer would crash when launching any game. Was caused by an ImGui style value going below the minimum the newer ImGui enforces; affected both OpenGL and Vulkan on those devices. Enjoy the fix!

Added new settings: 
FPU Multiply
OPH Flag
Emulate GIF FIFO
DMA Busy
Delay VIF1 Stalls
I-Bit
Full VU0 Sync
VU Add-Sub
VU Overflow
Extra XGKICK Delay
Skip Duplicate Frames
EE FPU Round Mode (Nearest / Negative / Positive / Chop)
EE/FPU Clamping and VU Clamping modes.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V13
releases.body:
Added the new standard universal Android APK build: one APK now includes both 4K-page and 16K-page ARM64 cores. This should fix instability and crashes for the app on a range of devices. (Although it does increase the apps size, worth it though). Runtime now automatically loads the correct native core for the device: emucore_4k or emucore_16k. Restored strict page-size matching per core, fixing crashes seen on some 4K devices while keeping 16K-device support.

Added separate Swap Disc and Boot Disc flows. Boot Disc functions as Change Disk

Added native live disc hot-swap support for multi-disc for things like CodeBreaker. (Please report if it's working!)

Fixed an ARM64 recompiler zero-register shift issue that could crash or miscompile some games. (Ratchet and Clank series of games are now fixed, worth testing to see what other games were fixed as well).

Improved import patches readability.

Below-native resolution: added 0.25x, 0.5x, and 0.75x internal resolution options for more performance on lower-end devices.

EE/FPU Clamping setting: None / Normal / Extra / Full, in the Performance tab.

VU Clamping setting: None / Normal / Extra / Extra+Sign, in the Performance tab.

Save Manager: the Load State screen now manages saves per game. Each slot shows its date and size, you can delete a slot, back up and restore all of a game's saves, and there is an auto-save-on-exit toggle.

releases.name: ARMSX2 Refresh V12
releases.body:
Save/Load state hotkeys: quick-save and quick-load now bind to physical controller buttons, so you can save/restore without opening the menu. Configurable under Settings → Controller → Controller hotkeys (alongside the existing Menu/Pause bind).

Added VSync option under Renderer settings. Restart the game to apply.

Added Speedhack startup profiles: Optimal (default) and Fast (aggressive: EE cycle skip + fast CDVD, for low-end devices) presets at the top of Performance. Tweaking any individual speedhack un-highlights both, indicating a custom setup.

Added Region flags in the library: each cover now shows a 🇺🇸 / 🇪🇺 / 🇯🇵 / 🇰🇷 / 🌏 flag underneath, so you can tell regional versions of the same game apart at a glance.

Added more audio panel settings to match parity with PCSX2:
Time Stretch: keeps pitch stable when speed varies
Audio Buffer: 20–200 ms
Output Latency: 5–100 ms
Fast-Forward Volume: 0–100%

EE Cycle Rate / Cycle Skip now actually takes effect, (broke in V11).

releases.name: ARMSX2 iOS 2.3.0
releases.body:
Added RetroAchievements Hardcore support on iOS, including Hardcore status states, settings UI, login recovery/status handling, and enforcement around save-state loading and restricted features. Thank you to @LiquifiedSnow and the rest of the RA team for working with us! 

Brought over recompiler changes I did on Android to fix Ratchet and Clank series of games (should fix other games as well!)

Added RetroAchievements in-game support polish: achievement toast notifications with badges, current-game achievements panel, notification/leaderboard/overlay toggles, and better overlay defaults for iOS screens.

Brought over major Mac/core ARM64 improvements into iOS from @isztldav, especially EE/VU recompiler work around COP2/VU0 sync, native VU macro paths, load/store/branch handling, cycle accounting, and VU clamp/IR fixes.

From @J1coding 

VPad sizing overhaul: on-screen controls now have adjustable visible width/height instead of one uniform scale. Aspect lock keeps normal resizing simple, while unlocked sizing allows wider or taller controls when a layout or skin needs it. Sizes are clamped to a safe range, art stays centered while resizing, and analog sticks still scale safely as square controls.
